# Hospital Management Information System (HMIS)

## [OPD Module]

### **1 Appointment Management**

### **1.1 Appointment Booking: P1**

- 1.1.1 Walk-in registration
- 1.1.2 Telephone
- 1.1.3 Web portal & mobile app booking

### **1.2 Doctor Availability & Scheduling: P1**

- 1.2.1 Consultant-wise daily/weekly availability calendar
- 1.2.2 Shift-based scheduling (morning/evening clinics)
- 1.2.3 Configurable time slots (e.g., 10 mins, 15 mins)
- 1.2.4 Department-wise and location-wise scheduling (for multi-location hospitals)
- 1.2.5 Holidays, leave management, and block slot settings

### **1.3 Slot Management: P1**

- 1.3.1 Time-slot based appointments
- 1.3.2 Buffer time between appointments
- 1.3.3 Doctor-defined limits on number of appointments per slot/day

### **1.4 Priority & Categorization: P1**

- 1.4.1 Appointment priority levels: emergency, VIP, regular
- 1.4.2 Token priority for elderly, differently-abled, children

### **1.5 Confirmation & Notifications: P2**

- 1.5.1 SMS/Email/WhatsApp confirmation with appointment details
- 1.5.2 Reminder notifications 24 hrs and 1 hr before the appointment
- 1.5.3 Reschedule/cancellation confirmation alerts
- 1.5.4 Optional call-back notification to patient by doctor/hospital

### **1.6 Appointment Rescheduling & Cancellation: P2**

- 1.6.1 Patient and hospital-side rescheduling rights
- 1.6.2 Auto-adjustment of queue and slot availability
- 1.6.3 Logging of cancellation reasons (patient, system, doctor)
- 1.6.4 Waiting list adjustment logic for cancelled slots

### **1.7 Check-In & Queue Management: P2**

- 1.7.1 Digital or manual check-in upon arrival
- 1.7.2 Queue number generation (token system)
- 1.7.3 Real-time patient status updates (Waiting / In-Consultation / Done / No-show)
- 1.7.4 Estimated wait time display at counters or on app
- 1.7.5 Integration with digital signage/display boards

### **1.8 Walk-In Appointment Handling: P1**

- 1.8.1 Slot assignment from current availability
- 1.8.2 Manual queue token assignment
- 1.8.3 Doctor override option to squeeze-in patients

### **1.9 Doctor/Clinic Dashboard: P1**

- 1.9.1 View today's appointments with patient details
- 1.9.2 Status of each appointment (booked, checked-in, cancelled, completed)
- 1.9.3 Integrated view of patient's previous history
- 1.9.4 Quick links to EMR, prescriptions, test ordering

### **1.10 Admin & Supervisor Functions: P2**

- 1.10.1 Bulk scheduling/unblocking of doctor calendars
- 1.10.2 View all appointments by department/date/doctor
- 1.10.3 Assign or reassign appointments manually
- 1.11 Patient Portal & Mobile App Functions: P2**
  - 1.11.1 View, book, reschedule, or cancel appointments
  - 1.11.2 Check doctor availability
  - 1.11.3 View token/waiting status
  - 1.11.4 Receive reminders and reports

### **2 Patient Registration**

### **2.1 Unique Patient Identification: P1**

- 2.1.1 Auto-generation of a **Unique Medical Record Number** for each patient
- 2.1.2 Cross-verification to avoid duplicate entries (based on name, mobile, CNIC #)
- 2.1.3 Barcode/QR code generation for MR cards or visit slips
- 2.1.4 Search functionality (MR#, mobile, name, old record)

### **2.2 Patient Demographic Data Capture: P1**

- 2.2.1 Full Name (First, Middle, Last)
- 2.2.2 Gender (Male, Female, other)
- 2.2.3 S/O, D/O, W/O
- 2.2.4 Date of Birth / Age (with auto-calculation of one based on the other)
- 2.2.5 Marital Status
- 2.2.6 Nationality
- 2.2.7 Blood Group
- 2.2.8 Occupation
- 2.2.9 Education level (optional)

### **2.3 Contact & Address Details: P1**

- 2.3.1 Permanent address
- 2.3.2 Current/Local address (with copy-to-permanent option)
- 2.3.3 Mobile number (mandatory), Alternate number
- 2.3.4 Email ID (optional)
- 2.3.5 Emergency contact name, relationship, and phone number

### **2.4 Identification Documents: P1**

- 2.4.1 CNIC
- 2.4.2 Upload and scan facility for documents
- 2.4.3 Document type selection dropdown

### **2.5 Registration Categories: P1**

- 2.5.1 Patient type: New / Repeat / Referred / Corporate / Government Scheme Beneficiary
- 2.5.2 Visit type: General / Emergency / Review
- 2.5.3 Department and Doctor Selection (based on schedule)
- 2.5.4 Referral source (Self / Physician / Hospital / Online)
- 2.5.5 Special category: Senior Citizen / Child / Govt. Employee

### **2.6 Visit Details Management: P1**

- 2.6.1 Auto-generation of **OPD Visit Number** (linked to MR #)
- 2.6.2 Department/clinic assigned
- 2.6.3 Consulting doctor auto-suggest based on schedule
- 2.6.4 Visit date/time stamp

2.6.5 Visit status (Scheduled, Checked-In, No-show, Cancelled)

### **2.7 Insurance: PI**

2.7.1 Insurance provider details (policy number, validity)

2.7.2 Corporate tie-up (employer name, ID)

2.7.3 Government schemes (SSP, etc.)

2.7.4 Eligibility validation and document capture

2.7.5 Preauthorization reference (if required)

## **3 Medical History**

### **3.1 Patient History Dashboard: PI**

3.1.1 Centralized view of the patient's longitudinal health record

3.1.2 Quick summary view for:

3.1.2.1 Past diagnoses

3.1.2.2 Previous consultations (OPD/IPD/ER)

3.1.2.3 Medications and allergies

3.1.2.4 Investigations and procedures

### **3.2 Filters by:**

3.2.1 Department

3.2.2 Date range

3.2.3 Doctor or specialty

### **3.3 Past Diagnoses & Conditions: PI**

3.3.1 Separate tagging for:

3.3.1.1 **Chronic diseases** (e.g., diabetes, hypertension)

3.3.1.2 **Acute illnesses** (e.g., infections)

3.3.1.3 **Resolved conditions**

3.3.2 Onset date, severity, current status, remarks

3.3.3 Auto-carry-forward of chronic conditions to every new visit

### **3.4 Past Surgeries & Procedures: PI**

3.4.1 Structured entry of:

3.4.1.1 Surgical history (e.g., appendectomy, CABG)

3.4.1.2 Minor and diagnostic procedures (e.g., biopsy, endoscopy)

3.4.2 Fields for:

3.4.2.1 Date, facility, surgeon name

3.4.2.2 Outcome, complications, follow-up advice

### **3.5 Medication History: PI**

3.5.1 Full medication timeline:

3.5.1.1 Past and ongoing prescriptions

3.5.1.2 Start/end dates, dosage, adherence status

3.5.2 Differentiation between:

3.5.2.1 Long-term/chronic meds

3.5.2.2 Temporary/episodic meds

3.5.3 Cross-linking with prescription module

### **3.6 Allergies & Adverse Reactions: PI**

3.6.1 Drug, food, environmental allergies

3.6.2 Entry of:

3.6.2.1 Allergen, reaction type, severity

3.6.2.2 Source of information (patient-reported, documented)

3.6.3 Integration with prescribing workflow to trigger alerts

### **3.7 Family History: P1**

3.7.1 Document hereditary diseases (e.g., diabetes, cancer, heart disease)

3.7.2 Family member affected, relation to patient, age of onset

3.7.3 Structured template + free-text option

### **3.8 Social & Lifestyle History: P1**

3.8.1 Smoking, alcohol, drug use

3.8.2 Physical activity level

3.8.3 Occupational exposure, stress, sleep pattern

3.8.4 Dietary habits

3.8.5 Integrated lifestyle risk scoring (optional)

### **3.9 Menstrual & Reproductive History (for females): P1**

3.9.1 Menarche, cycle regularity, menopause

3.9.2 Pregnancy history (gravida, para, abortions, live births)

3.9.3 Contraceptive use

3.9.4 Any gynecological conditions

### **3.10 Immunization History: P1**

3.10.1 Childhood and adult vaccinations (e.g., Tetanus, Hepatitis B, COVID-19)

3.10.2 Dates, doses, next due date

### **3.11 Hospitalization History: P1**

3.11.1 Admission dates and discharge summaries from:

3.11.1.1 IPD

3.11.1.2 Day care/surgical unit

3.11.2 Summary of diagnosis, procedures, and outcomes

3.11.3 Linked discharge documents for quick access

### **3.12 Visit-Based History Access: P1**

3.12.1 Past visit notes (OPD, IPD, and ER) with:

3.12.1.1 Date, department, doctor

3.12.1.2 Diagnoses, test results, clinical notes

3.12.2 Access to prescriptions, reports, certificates from past encounters

## **4 Examination Findings**

### **4.1 Structured Examination Templates: P1**

4.1.1 Predefined templates for common clinical examinations (e.g., general examination, system-specific examination like cardiovascular, neurological, gastrointestinal, etc.)

4.1.2 Standardized fields to capture key examination findings:

4.1.2.1 **General appearance** (alert, lethargic, in distress, etc.)

4.1.2.2 **Vitals:** BP, Pulse, Temperature, Respiratory rate, Oxygen saturation

4.1.2.3 **Height/Weight/Body Mass Index (BMI)**

4.1.2.4 **Head, eyes, ears, nose, throat (HEENT) examination**

4.1.2.5 **Cardiovascular examination** (heart sounds, pulses)

- 4.1.2.6 **Respiratory examination** (lung sounds, chest movement)
- 4.1.2.7 **Abdominal examination** (palpation, tenderness, organomegaly)
- 4.1.2.8 **Musculoskeletal examination** (joint movement, muscle strength)
- 4.1.2.9 **Neurological examination** (reflexes, coordination)
- 4.1.2.10 **Skin** (rashes, ulcers, cyanosis)

### **4.2 Free-text Clinical Notes: P1**

- 4.2.1 Ability to enter free-text clinical observations, especially for rare or complex findings
- 4.2.2 Text format with structured input fields for:
  - 4.2.2.1 **Findings**
  - 4.2.2.2 **Location of findings** (e.g., left upper quadrant of abdomen)
  - 4.2.2.3 **Duration of findings**
  - 4.2.2.4 **Nature and severity**
  - 4.2.2.5 **Patient-reported symptoms** (pain scale, discomfort)
- 4.2.3 Automatic linking to diagnosis and treatment sections

### **4.3 Vitals Recording: P1**

- 4.3.1 Ability to record vital signs, including:
  - 4.3.1.1 **Blood Pressure:** Systolic, Diastolic, Pulse pressure, Position (lying, sitting, standing)
  - 4.3.1.2 **Heart Rate:** Regularity, rhythm, rate
  - 4.3.1.3 **Temperature:** Oral, rectal, axillary, tympanic
  - 4.3.1.4 **Respiratory Rate:** Normal vs. abnormal, shallow vs. deep
  - 4.3.1.5 **Oxygen Saturation** (SpO2)
  - 4.3.1.6 **Height & Weight** with BMI calculation
- 4.3.2 Automatically populate vital trends over time (e.g., previous readings vs. current)

### **4.4 System-Specific Examination: P1**

- 4.4.1 Ability to enter findings from examinations specific to particular systems:
  - 4.4.1.1 **Cardiovascular:** Heart sounds, murmurs, pulses, jugular venous distension (JVD)
  - 4.4.1.2 **Respiratory:** Lung sounds (wheezes, crackles, rhonchi), chest expansion
  - 4.4.1.3 **Neurological:** Reflexes, muscle strength, sensation, cranial nerve function
  - 4.4.1.4 **Gastrointestinal:** Tenderness, organomegaly, bowel sounds
  - 4.4.1.5 **Musculoskeletal:** Joint flexibility, deformities, muscle strength, coordination

### **4.5 Scoring Systems & Grading: P2**

- 4.5.1 Support for clinical grading of findings (e.g., scale for joint tenderness, heart murmur grading, neurological scale)
- 4.5.2 Integration with severity scoring systems:
  - 4.5.2.1 Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS)
  - 4.5.2.2 Pain scoring (e.g., Numerical Rating Scale, Visual Analog Scale)
  - 4.5.2.3 Modified Ashworth Scale (for spasticity)
- 4.5.3 Support for **symptom checklists** with predefined options (e.g., **dyspnea, edema, rash**)

### **4.6 Findings Linked to Diagnosis & Treatment: P2**

- 4.6.1 Auto-population of findings into working diagnosis or assessment section
- 4.6.2 Ability to link specific findings to planned investigations (e.g., abnormal lung sounds linked to chest X-ray order)
- 4.6.3 Automatic flagging of abnormal findings to alert clinicians of potential concerns
- 4.6.4 Documentation of clinical impressions (e.g., suspected diagnosis or differential diagnosis)

### **4.7 Clinical Decision Support: P2**

- 4.7.1 **Real-time alerts** based on findings, such as:
  - 4.7.1.1 **Critical values** (e.g., BP > 180/110, abnormal pulse)
  - 4.7.1.2 **Abnormal findings** (e.g., murmur, asymmetry)
  - 4.7.1.3 **Clinical guidelines** based on recorded findings (e.g., asthma exacerbation management guidelines if wheezing is noted)
- 4.7.2 **Evidence-based recommendations** (e.g., referral to a specialist, need for specific tests)

### **4.8 Multiple Examiner Documentation: P1**

- 4.8.1 Ability to record findings from different examiners (e.g., nurse's initial findings, followed by the doctor's detailed examination)
- 4.8.2 Track **who** recorded the findings (e.g., nurse, doctor, or resident)
- 4.8.3 Option to **update findings** based on subsequent examinations or reassessments (e.g., re-checking BP)

### **4.9 Digital Signature & Approval Workflow: P1**

- 4.9.1 Digital signature for finalizing examination findings
- 4.9.2 Option for secondary review or approval by senior physician or specialist
- 4.9.3 Ability to track changes or updates to findings in case of reassessment

## **5 Investigations/Tests**

### **5.1 Test Ordering & Request Management: P1**

- 5.1.1 **Integrated Test Ordering:**
  - 5.1.1.1 Direct access to test catalogs (Lab and Radiology) from the OPD consultation page.
  - 5.1.1.2 Categorization of tests: **Routine, Emergency, Specialty-specific** (e.g., cardiac, infectious diseases).
  - 5.1.1.3 Option to search by **test name, test code, or department**.
- 5.1.1.4 **Multi-test ordering:** Ability to select and order multiple tests in one go (e.g., CBC, Urinalysis, Chest X-ray).
- 5.1.1.5 Support for **customized panels** (e.g., diabetes screening panel, lipid profile).
- 5.1.2 **Test Order Details:**
  - 5.1.2.1 Test name, code, description, cost (optional), priority (routine or urgent).
  - 5.1.2.2 **Clinical justification** or reason for the test (pre-populated or free-text).
  - 5.1.2.3 **Pre-test instructions** (e.g., fasting, collection guidelines).
  - 5.1.2.4 **Order notes** for specific instructions (e.g., special sample handling).
- 5.1.3 **Priority and Urgency Levels:**
  - 5.1.3.1 Ability to mark tests as **Routine, Urgent, or Stat** with real-time alerts to laboratory staff.
- 5.1.3.2 Integration with **Lab/Diagnostic Department Workflows** for prioritization.

### **5.2 Order Management Workflow: P1**

- 5.2.1 Automatic transmission of test orders to:
  - 5.2.1.1 **LIS (Laboratory Information System)**
  - 5.2.1.2 **RIS (Radiology Information System)**
- 5.2.2 Status updates: **Ordered → Sample Collected → In Progress → Result Ready**
- 5.2.3 Order cancellation and modification with audit trail
- 5.2.4 Doctor notification when results are available

### **5.3 Results Communication: P1**

- 5.3.1 Results entered via LIS/RIS and auto-pushed to: **P1**

- 5.3.1.1 Patient's EMR
- 5.3.1.2 Doctor Workbench
- 5.3.1.3 Patient Portal/App **P2**
- 5.3.2 Auto-flagging of critical values and abnormal results
- 5.3.3 Upload or auto-linking of radiology reports (PDF, DOC, HL7 format)
- 5.3.4 PACS integration for image viewing (Radiology)
- 5.3.5 Patient Notification:
  - 5.3.5.1 Auto-generated SMS, email, or push notifications to patients when test results are available.
  - 5.3.5.2 **Patient Portal:** Secure access for patients to view their results and download reports.

- 5.3.5.3 **Doctor Communication:** Immediate access for clinicians to view test results and document findings during consultation.

#### - 5.3.6 Result Interpretation: **P2**

- 5.3.6.1 Summary of abnormal findings with clinical interpretation notes from pathologists, lab technicians, or radiologists.

- 5.3.6.2 Option to schedule follow-up consultations or tests based on results.

### - 5.4 Report Generation and Access: P2**

- 5.4.1 Customizable Reports:

- 5.4.1.1 **Test summary reports** showing all ordered tests with status, results, and dates.

- 5.4.1.2 **Trended data:** View patient's test results over time (e.g., blood glucose levels, cholesterol levels).

- 5.4.1.3 **Searchable history:** Ability to filter by **test type, date, doctor, or department**.

- 5.4.2 Report Exporting:

- 5.4.2.1 Export test results to **PDF, Excel, or structured formats** for external use or sharing.

- 5.4.2.2 **Print-friendly formats** for patient handouts or physician consultations.

- 5.4.3 Specialized Reports:

- 5.4.3.1 **Clinical pathology reports** summarizing test findings, recommendations, and next steps.

- 5.4.3.2 **Diagnostic imaging reports** (e.g., radiology findings with annotated images).

### - 5.5 Test History and Tracking: P1**

- 5.5.1 Patient's Test History:

- 5.5.1.1 Access to a comprehensive list of **previous test results** across different visits or encounters.

- 5.5.1.2 Tracked by test type, result trends, and the frequency of tests.

- 5.5.1.3 Integration of **longitudinal records** to track recurring tests (e.g., monitoring a chronic condition over time).

- 5.5.2 Test Tracking System:

- 5.5.2.1 **Order tracking** to monitor test status in real-time (pending, in-progress, completed).

- 5.5.2.2 **Cross-department visibility** (lab, radiology, pharmacy) for coordinated care and faster results handling.

## **6 Treatment Plan**

### **6.1 Treatment Plan Creation: P1**

- 6.1.1 Treatment Plan Initialization:

- 6.1.1.1 Automatically generate or manually create a treatment plan at the point of care during an OPD consultation.
- 6.1.1.2 Allow **clinician-driven creation** with **preset templates** based on diagnosis (e.g., diabetes management, hypertension care).
- 6.1.1.3 **Customizable plans** based on individual needs (e.g., for special populations like pediatrics, geriatrics, or chronic conditions).
- 6.1.1.4 Include a **problem list** that outlines patient issues to be addressed in the treatment plan (e.g., hypertension, respiratory infection, weight loss).

#### 6.1.2 Integrated Diagnosis:

- 6.1.2.1 Treatment plan integrates with **diagnoses** entered during the consultation, automatically populating specific treatment guidelines, protocols, or therapies based on the diagnosis.
- 6.1.2.2 Option to **link specific interventions** with each diagnosis.

### 6.2 Goal Setting: P2

- 6.2.1 Clinical Goals:
  - 6.2.1.1 Clinician-defined short-term and long-term **goals** for each diagnosis.
  - 6.2.1.2 SMART goal setting (Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound) for treatment.
  - 6.2.1.3 **Example Goals:**
    - 6.2.1.3.1 For diabetes: Achieve HbA1c < 7% in 3 months.
    - 6.2.1.3.2 For hypertension: Reduce BP to < 140/90 mmHg within 6 weeks.
- 6.2.2 Patient-Centered Goals:
  - 6.2.2.1 Ability to set goals that are aligned with **patient preferences** (e.g., pain relief, improving mobility, or quality of life).
  - 6.2.2.2 Support for **collaborative goal-setting** with the patient, ensuring patient involvement and buy-in.

### 6.3 Interventions & Therapeutic Modalities: P1

- 6.3.1 Prescription Management
  - 6.3.1.1 Digital prescription builder with:
    - 6.3.1.1.1 Drug name (brand & generic)
    - 6.3.1.1.2 Dose, route, frequency, duration
    - 6.3.1.1.3 Time of administration (e.g., before/after food)
    - 6.3.1.1.4 Notes to pharmacist/patient
  - 6.3.1.2 Auto-suggestions from hospital formulary
  - 6.3.1.3 Favorite drug templates per doctor
  - 6.3.1.4 Drug-allergy interaction alerts
  - 6.3.1.5 Multilingual prescription printouts (optional)
  - 6.3.1.6 E-prescription send to pharmacy integration
  - 6.3.1.7 Include **non-pharmacological interventions** (e.g., physical therapy, dietary changes, and exercise routines).
- 6.3.1.8 Integration with the **Pharmacy Module** for automatic medication prescribing, including warnings for drug interactions and contraindications.
- 6.3.2 Referrals:
  - 6.3.2.1 Automatically generate **referral requests** to specialists (e.g., cardiologist, endocrinologist) based on the treatment plan.
  - 6.3.2.2 Support for **external referral management**, with integration to specialist schedules, if applicable.
- 6.3.3 Procedures & Follow-ups:

- 6.3.3.1 Procedure ordering (e.g., dressing, ECG, minor OT)
- 6.3.3.2 Procedure documentation:
  - 6.3.3.2.1 Type, date, time, outcome
  - 6.3.3.2.2 Pre- and post-procedure notes
  - 6.3.3.2.3 Consent form upload (optional)
- 6.3.3.3 Integration with procedure billing
- 6.3.3.4 Set up **follow-up visits or monitoring schedules** (e.g., 2-week follow-up for blood pressure check).
- 6.3.3.5 Include **patient education**: Printouts, brochures, or links to trusted resources for self-management.

### **6.4 Treatment Plan Duration & Timelines: P1**

#### **6.4.1 Duration and Milestones:**

- 6.4.1.1 Define **start and end dates** for the treatment plan, allowing for review of progress at designated intervals.
- 6.4.1.2 Include **milestones** that represent key points in the treatment process (e.g., reassessment of treatment effectiveness, follow-up tests, and consultation).

#### **6.4.2 Timeline Visualization: P2**

- 6.4.2.1 A graphical timeline for treatment interventions and goals, showing when each goal should be reviewed and when assessments are scheduled.
- 6.4.2.2 Include reminders for **patient follow-ups, lab test schedules, and medication refills**.

### **6.5 Progress Tracking: P1**

#### **6.5.1 Monitoring and Evaluation:**

- 6.5.1.1 Track **patient progress** against treatment goals and interventions.
- 6.5.1.2 Record **patient feedback and self-reported outcomes** (e.g., pain scales, activity levels, symptoms).
- 6.5.1.3 Allow clinicians to **update treatment goals** based on progress (or lack thereof).

#### **6.5.2 Periodic Reviews:**

- 6.5.2.1 Automatically schedule periodic **reviews** of the treatment plan (e.g., monthly reviews for chronic conditions, post-surgical follow-up).
- 6.5.2.2 Ability to **revise treatment plans** based on progress (e.g., change medications if BP is not controlled).

### **6.6 Clinical Decision Support: P2**

#### **6.6.1 Guideline-Based Suggestions:**

- 6.6.1.1 Integration with **clinical guidelines** to provide evidence-based treatment suggestions based on the diagnosis and patient data.

##### **6.6.1.2 Alerts** for treatment deviations, such as not adhering to clinical guidelines or missing recommended steps.

#### **6.6.2 Risk Assessment:**

- 6.6.2.1 **Risk-based alerts** to guide clinical decision-making based on the patient's age, comorbidities, and history (e.g., high risk of heart attack, stroke, or diabetic complications).
- 6.6.2.2 Integration with **decision support tools** for identifying high-risk patients who need enhanced monitoring or interventions.

### **6.7 Multidisciplinary Collaboration: P2**

#### **6.7.1 Collaborative Care Plans:**

- 6.7.1.1 Option for **multidisciplinary team members** (e.g., nurses, physical therapists, dieticians, social workers) to collaborate and add their recommendations directly to the treatment plan.
- 6.7.1.2 **Role-based access** to treatment plan, with different levels of visibility and permissions for various care team members.
- 6.7.1.3 Integration with **specialty modules** (e.g., **Physical Therapy, Dietary, Psychiatry**), allowing relevant professionals to update and add components to the treatment plan.
- 6.7.2 **Notes and Communication:**
  - 6.7.2.1 Support for **internal communication** between healthcare team members, with space for **comments or recommendations**.
  - 6.7.2.2 Option for **multidisciplinary meetings** to review the treatment plan and discuss patient progress.

### **6.8 Patient Education & Engagement: P2**

- 6.8.1 **Patient Education Materials:**
  - 6.8.1.1 Provide **educational content** based on the patient's condition and treatment plan (e.g., diet recommendations for diabetes, exercise plans for cardiovascular patients).
  - 6.8.1.2 Support for **video resources, patient handouts, or links to trusted websites**.
- 6.8.2 **Self-management Tools:**
  - 6.8.2.1 Allow patients to track their own progress (e.g., log BP readings, blood sugar levels, pain intensity) via the **patient portal**.
  - 6.8.2.2 Set **self-management goals** in alignment with the treatment plan (e.g., adherence to exercise plan, dietary changes).
- 6.8.3 **Communication with Patients:**
  - 6.8.3.1 Secure messaging through the **patient portal** for direct communication with healthcare providers regarding the treatment plan.
  - 6.8.3.2 **Appointment reminders** for follow-up visits, tests, and treatments.

## **7 Pharmacy Integration**

### **7.1 Prescription Integration: P1**

- 7.1.1 Seamless transfer of OPD e-prescriptions from the OPD to the Pharmacy system
- 7.1.2 Real-time view of:
  - 7.1.2.1 Drug name, strength, dosage, frequency, duration
  - 7.1.2.2 Instructions for use (before/after food, etc.)
  - 7.1.2.3 Quantity auto-calculated based on prescription
- 7.1.3 Drug allergy and interaction alerts before dispatch
- 7.1.4 Support for generic & branded prescriptions

### **7.2 Pharmacy Dispensing Workflow: P1**

- 7.2.1 View pending prescriptions per patient
- 7.2.2 Medicine availability check with **live inventory sync**
- 7.2.3 Partial dispensing (with doctor/patient consent)
- 7.2.4 Substitute suggestions (with approval logic)
- 7.2.5 Prescription status update:
  - 7.2.5.1 Prescribed → Ready → Dispensed → Rejected/Short
- 7.2.6 Print patient-friendly prescription label

### **7.3 Billing Integration: P1**

- 7.3.1 Auto-generation of **pharmacy bill** linked to the OPD visit
- 7.3.2 Support for:

7.3.2.1 Cash billing

7.3.2.2 Insurance/TPA schemes

7.3.2.3 Government-sponsored programs

7.3.2.4 Credit/corporate clients

7.3.3 Real-time billing updates reflected in patient account

### **7.4 Inventory & Stock Integration: P1**

7.4.1 Real-time stock tracking for OPD pharmacy counters

7.4.2 Alerts for low stock or out-of-stock items

7.4.3 Batch-wise inventory with expiry tracking

7.4.4 FIFO/FEFO (First Expiry First Out) dispensing logic

7.4.5 Drug expiry, returns, and near-expiry alerts

7.4.6 Stock transfer between pharmacy locations (e.g., central → OPD counter)

### **7.5 Formulary Management: P1**

7.5.1 Central formulary with:

7.5.1.1 Generic and brand names

7.5.1.2 Restricted drugs

7.5.1.3 Substitution mapping

7.5.1.4 Drug classification (antibiotic, antihypertensive, etc.)

7.5.2 Formulary updates by pharmacy admin

7.5.3 Doctor's quick-pick/favorites linked to formulary

### **7.6 Patient Communication & Labels: P1**

7.6.1 Print dispensing label with:

7.6.1.1 Patient name, MR#

7.6.1.2 Drug name and strength

7.6.1.3 Dosage instructions in local language

7.6.2 SMS/WhatsApp alerts when medicine is ready (optional)

7.6.3 Prescription copy with billing receipt for patient

### **7.7 Drug Interaction & Safety Checks: P1**

7.7.1 Alerts for:

7.7.1.1 Drug-drug interaction

7.7.1.2 Drug-allergy conflict

7.7.1.3 Maximum dose limits

7.7.2 Restricted drug alerts

7.7.3 Flagging of non-formulary or unapproved prescriptions

## **8 Follow-Up Instructions**

### **8.1 Follow-Up Plan Creation: P2**

8.1.1 Automatic Generation of Follow-Up Instructions:

- 8.1.1.1 **Clinician-driven follow-up instructions** are automatically generated at the end of the consultation, based on the diagnosis, treatment plan, and patient's needs.
- 8.1.1.2 Instructions should include a **summary of the treatment plan**, along with key follow-up actions (e.g., test schedules, medication instructions, and future appointments).
- 8.1.1.3 Ability to **customize** instructions for different conditions and patients, such as specific advice for chronic diseases (e.g., diabetes, hypertension, COPD).
- 8.1.1.4 **Standardized Templates:**
- 8.1.1.5 **Pre-defined templates** for common conditions (e.g., post-surgical instructions, diabetic care, maternity follow-ups) to save time while maintaining consistency.
- 8.1.1.6 **Personalized templates** based on patient preferences or previous follow-up patterns.

### 8.2 Follow-Up Appointment Scheduling: P2

#### 8.2.1 Automated Appointment Scheduling:

- 8.2.1.1 **Integrated appointment scheduling** system that allows automatic creation of follow-up appointments based on the treatment plan.
- 8.2.1.2 Patients are provided with a follow-up appointment schedule (e.g., "Return for follow-up visit in 2 weeks").
- 8.2.1.3 **Appointment reminders** sent via SMS, email, or patient portal to ensure the patient attends their follow-up visit.
- 8.2.1.4 Ability for the system to **flag missed follow-ups** and send automated reminders or alerts to the care team for re-engagement.

#### 8.2.2 Follow-Up Visit Type & Frequency:

- 8.2.2.1 Clearly define the **type of visit** (e.g., general follow-up, lab work, specialist consultation, surgery review).
- 8.2.2.2 **Frequency of follow-up visits:** Indicate when the next visit is due (e.g., "Next visit due in 3 months" for chronic conditions, or "Follow-up within 1 week" for post-operative care).
- 8.2.2.3 Support for **virtual follow-up visits** via telemedicine platforms, including the scheduling of virtual appointments.

### 8.3 Medication and Treatment Instructions: P1

#### 8.3.1 Medication Adherence Instructions:

- 8.3.1.1 Detailed instructions about **prescribed medications**, including dosage, frequency, and duration of the treatment.
- 8.3.1.2 **Side effects** or potential adverse reactions noted, with instructions on what to do if they occur (e.g., "If you experience dizziness, contact your doctor").
- 8.3.1.3 Ability to **review and adjust medication** during follow-ups based on patient progress or lab results.

#### 8.3.2 Refills and Renewals:

- 8.3.2.1 **Automatic medication refills** can be triggered based on the treatment plan and duration of medication.
- 8.3.2.2 Clear guidance on how patients can obtain **medication refills** during follow-up (e.g., through the pharmacy, online portals, or during the next consultation).

### 8.4 Diagnostic Tests & Procedures: P1

#### 8.4.1 Follow-Up Tests and Lab Work:

- 8.4.1.1 Clear instructions on required **diagnostic tests** (e.g., blood tests, imaging, ECG) with specific **timing** for when tests should be completed before the next visit.
- 8.4.1.2 Automated reminders to **schedule tests** and ensure results are available for the next follow-up visit.
- 8.4.1.3 Integration with the **laboratory and radiology modules** to automatically add tests to the follow-up instructions and to track the patient's completion of those tests.
- 8.4.2 Specialty Referrals and Procedures:
  - 8.4.2.1 Provide instructions for **referrals** to specialists (e.g., cardiology, dermatology) for further evaluation or treatment.
  - 8.4.2.2 Scheduling of **procedures** or additional investigations based on the patient's needs (e.g., surgery, physiotherapy).

### **8.5 Lifestyle and Preventive Care Instructions: P1**

- 8.5.1 Health Education and Lifestyle Modifications:
  - 8.5.1.1 Provide specific **lifestyle change recommendations** related to the patient's diagnosis (e.g., dietary changes, exercise routines, smoking cessation).
  - 8.5.1.2 Educational materials on **self-care**: Include links to online resources, handouts, and recommended lifestyle practices to enhance health.
  - 8.5.1.3 Customizable advice for **chronic disease management** (e.g., blood sugar monitoring for diabetes, weight management for obesity).
- 8.5.2 Preventive Measures:
  - 8.5.2.1 Offer **vaccination reminders**, especially for preventable diseases (e.g., flu shots, HPV vaccinations).
  - 8.5.2.2 **Screening schedules** for preventive care (e.g., mammograms, pap smears, colon cancer screening).

### **8.6 Patient Communication and Engagement: P2**

- 8.6.1 Clear and Accessible Instructions:
  - 8.6.1.1 **Patient-friendly language** used in follow-up instructions, avoiding medical jargon.
  - 8.6.1.2 **Interactive instructions** where patients can read or listen to explanations via multimedia (e.g., video instructions, info graphics).
- 8.6.2 Patient Portal Integration:
  - 8.6.2.1 All follow-up instructions, including appointments, medications, and test results, are accessible via the **patient portal**.
  - 8.6.2.2 **Secure messaging**: Patients can send follow-up questions or seek clarification about their instructions directly through the portal.
- 8.6.3 Patient Reminders and Notifications:
  - 8.6.3.1 Automatic reminders through SMS, email, or push notifications about follow-up actions (e.g., "Remember to take your medication", "Don't forget your blood test tomorrow").
  - 8.6.3.2 Customizable reminder intervals: Reminders can be set for days or weeks before the scheduled appointment or test.

### **8.7 Follow-Up Instructions for Special Populations: P1**

- 8.7.1 Chronic Disease Management:
  - 8.7.1.1 Follow-up instructions tailored for patients with **chronic conditions** (e.g., diabetes, hypertension) that include specific management instructions (e.g., regular monitoring of blood glucose levels, blood pressure check).
  - 8.7.1.2 **Proactive monitoring**: Encourage regular logging of health parameters (e.g., blood pressure, weight, glucose) through the **patient portal** or mobile apps.
- 8.7.2 Post-Operative Care:

- 8.7.2.1 Detailed post-operative care instructions, including wound care, activity restrictions, and signs of complications.
- 8.7.2.2 Automatic generation of **follow-up visits** for wound healing, rehabilitation, or surgical review.
- 8.7.3 Pediatric and Geriatric Care:
  - 8.7.3.1 Special follow-up instructions for **pediatric patients**, including vaccination schedules, growth tracking, and milestone check-ups.
  - 8.7.3.2 For **geriatric patients**, follow-up instructions related to medication adjustments, fall prevention, mobility support, and cognitive assessments.

### 8.8 Progress Tracking and Updates: P2

#### 8.8.1 Follow-Up Review:

- 8.8.1.1 Track and update follow-up instructions based on patient progress. If a goal has been achieved or a new issue arises, the follow-up instructions should be modified accordingly.
- 8.8.1.2 Use **progress notes** to document the outcomes of follow-up actions (e.g., did the patient comply with the prescribed exercise regimen? Did the patient complete the required lab tests?).

#### 8.8.2 Outcome Evaluation:

- 8.8.2.1 Periodically assess whether the treatment plan is progressing as expected, and adjust future follow-up instructions to reflect this (e.g., change medication, add additional tests, and modify lifestyle instructions).
- 8.8.2.2 Provide a structured way to evaluate the patient's health improvement based on clinical criteria (e.g., improved blood pressure, reduction in symptoms).

### 8.9 Multidisciplinary Coordination: P3

#### 8.9.1 Team-Based Follow-Up:

- 8.9.1.1 Enable coordination among various departments (e.g., primary care, dietetics, physiotherapy) to deliver unified follow-up instructions.
  - 8.9.1.2 Include input from different specialties in the follow-up instructions (e.g., referral to physiotherapy after surgery, dietary changes for managing hypertension).
- ### 8.9.2 Interdisciplinary Follow-Up Plans:
- 8.9.2.1 For complex patients, such as those with multi-morbidity, provide a coordinated follow-up plan involving multiple departments, ensuring a holistic approach to patient care.

## **11 OPD SOPs / PROTOCOLS AND GUIDELINES REPOSITORY**

### **11.1 Digitized Repository of OPD SOPs / Protocols and Guidelines**

- 11.1.1 OPD SOPs and Protocols Repository: P1
  - 11.1.1.1 SOP/Protocol Name: [List of all available SOPs and protocols]
  - 11.1.1.2 Document Type: [Link to PDF/Word document for each SOP]
  - 11.1.1.3 Date Last Updated: [MM/DD/YYYY]
  - 11.1.1.4 Version History: [Link to historical versions of the SOPs/Protocols]
  - 11.1.1.5 Search & Filter Options:
    - 11.1.1.5.1 Search Bar: [Allow staff to search for specific SOPs, procedures, or diseases]
    - 11.1.1.5.2 Filter by Role: [Filter guidelines by role (e.g., Doctors, Nurses, Support Staff)]
    - 11.1.1.5.3 Filter by Protocol Type: [Filter by category like Trauma, Cardiology, Infectious Disease, etc.]
    - 11.1.1.5.4 Filter by Status: [Active/Archived]

### **11.2 Training and Education: P1**

- 11.2.1 E-Learning Modules:

- 11.2.1.1 Training Course Title: [e.g., “Basic Trauma Management,” “Infection Control Practices”]
- 11.2.1.2 Course Description: [Brief overview of the course]
- 11.2.1.3 Module Content:
- 11.2.1.3.1 Videos: [Training videos covering various emergency care protocols, donning/doffing PPE, etc.]
- 11.2.1.3.2 Interactive Quizzes: [Quizzes to assess understanding of the protocol content]
- 11.2.1.3.3 Case Studies: [Real-life case study simulations for application of protocols]
- 11.2.1.4 Staff Progress Tracking:
- 11.2.1.4.1 Completion Status: [Track who has completed the training module]
- 11.2.1.4.2 Passing Criteria: [Display minimum score to pass e-learning courses]
- 11.2.1.4.3 Progress Tracking: [Allow staff to see their own training progress]
- 11.2.1.4.4 Certificates: [Automated certification upon completion of a training module]
- 11.2.1.5 Training Module Library:
- 11.2.1.5.1 Available Courses: [List of available e-learning modules]
- 11.2.1.5.2 Module Duration: [Time required to complete each module]
- 11.2.1.5.3 Review Status: [Indicates when the module was last reviewed and updated]

### **11.3 Regular Push Notifications for Updated Guidelines**

- 11.3.1 Push Notification System: P1
- 11.3.1.1 Notification Types:
- 11.3.1.1.1 Guideline Updates: [Notify staff whenever SOPs or protocols are updated]
- 11.3.1.1.2 New Training Modules: [Alert staff to new e-learning training content available]
- 11.3.1.1.3 Outbreak or Crisis Protocols: [Immediate push notifications for any new emergency protocols]
- 11.3.1.2 Notification Features:
- 11.3.1.2.1 Urgency Level: [Categorize notifications by urgency: Critical, High, Low]
- 11.3.1.2.2 Customizable Preferences: [Allow users to set their own notification preferences for specific SOPs or guidelines]
- 11.3.1.2.3 Detailed Alerts: [Notifications will include a brief description of the update and a direct link to the updated SOP]
- 11.3.1.3 Push Notification History:
- 11.3.1.3.1 Archived Notifications: [Allow users to view past notifications for reference]
- 11.3.1.3.2 Acknowledgement Tracking: [Staff must acknowledge receiving notifications to ensure they've seen updates]

## **12 Administrative Section**

### **12.1 Staff and Personnel Management: P1**

- 12.1.1 Staff Scheduling:
- 12.1.1.1 **Doctor, nurse, and support staff scheduling** for OPD hours, ensuring adequate coverage and availability.
- 12.1.1.2 **Shift management:** Ensure that work shifts are assigned according to the hospital's operational requirements (e.g., morning, evening shifts).
- 12.1.1.3 **Automated reminders** for staff about upcoming shifts, with the ability to manage shift swaps and vacation requests.
- 12.1.2 Staff Profiles:

- 12.1.2.1 Maintain detailed profiles of **medical and non-medical personnel**, including qualifications, specialties, work history, and contact details.
- 12.1.2.2 **Certification and licensing tracking:** Ensure that the credentials of clinicians (doctors, nurses, specialists) are up to date and compliant with regulatory requirements.
- 12.1.3 Staff Roles and Permissions:
  - 12.1.3.1 Define **role-based access control** to restrict access to sensitive patient and administrative information (e.g., doctors, nurses, administrative staff, finance).
  - 12.1.3.2 Grant permissions based on roles to ensure data security and accountability in accessing patient data, billing information, and system functionalities.

### 12.2 Resource Management: P1

#### 12.2.1 Room and Facility Management:

- 12.2.1.1 **Appointment room booking:** Ensure availability and allocation of consultation rooms, examination rooms, and other facilities.
- 12.2.1.2 **Equipment availability:** Monitor and track the use of medical equipment in the OPD, such as ECG machines, ultrasound machines, and examination tools.
- 12.2.1.3 **Resource allocation:** Manage the allocation of hospital resources based on real-time requirements, including **waiting areas, restrooms, and waiting chairs**.

#### 12.2.2 Inventory Management:

- 12.2.2.1 **Inventory tracking:** Ensure the availability of necessary consumables like gloves, medical supplies, and medications in the OPD.
- 12.2.2.2 **Automatic stock updates:** Automatically update inventory after patient visits, medication dispensed, or tests conducted.
- 12.2.2.3 **Low stock alerts:** Set automatic notifications to alert the admin team when stocks fall below a certain threshold to ensure timely reordering.

### 12.3 Patient Flow Management: P1

#### 12.3.1 Appointment Scheduling:

- 12.3.1.1 **Centralized scheduling system** to manage patient appointments for various specialties, ensuring optimal appointment distribution across available doctors.
- 12.3.1.2 **Real-time updates** on patient appointments, cancellations, and no-shows, allowing for smooth rescheduling and utilization of available time slots.
- 12.3.1.3 **Waiting time management:** Monitor and manage patient waiting times, provide estimates to patients, and prioritize urgent cases when necessary.

#### 12.3.2 Patient Tracking:

- 12.3.2.1 Monitor **patient movement** through different stages of the OPD visit (e.g., registration, consultation, laboratory tests, pharmacy).
- 12.3.2.2 **Patient queuing system:** Display the patient's position in the queue, ensuring transparent communication with patients about expected waiting times.

#### 12.3.3 Patient Feedback:

- 12.3.3.1 Collect and monitor **patient feedback** on their experience during the OPD visit, covering areas like registration, waiting time, clinician interaction, and facility quality.
- 12.3.3.2 Use feedback data to improve service quality and address any operational inefficiencies or patient dissatisfaction.

### 12.4 Policies and Workflow Configuration: P2

#### 12.4.1 Operational Policy Setup:

- 12.4.1.1 **Customize hospital policies** within the system, such as OPD hours, consultation fees, patient eligibility criteria, and admission procedures.
- 12.4.1.2 Configure workflows for various operational processes, including appointment scheduling, patient registration, billing, and discharge.
- 12.4.2 **Fee Structure Management:**
  - 12.4.2.1 Define and manage **fee structures** for various OPD services (e.g., consultation fees, diagnostic tests, medication).
  - 12.4.2.2 Ensure **discounts and concessions** are applied based on patient categories (e.g., insurance, low-income patients, government schemes).
- 12.4.3 **Workflow Automation:**
  - 12.4.3.1 Enable automation of common administrative tasks such as patient reminders, appointment confirmations, and insurance verification.
  - 12.4.3.2 Implement automated workflows for referrals to other departments or specialists, including the creation of referral letters and follow-up instructions.

## 13 Billing/Payment Details

### 13.1 Patient Billing Information Management: P2

- 13.1.1 **Automated Patient Registration:**
  - 13.1.1.1 Capture **demographic information** (name, age, address, etc.) and **insurance details** at registration.
  - 13.1.1.2 Verify **patient insurance coverage** and eligibility before the consultation or treatment.
  - 13.1.1.3 Ensure **unique patient identification** through MR# (Medical Record Number) or other unique identifiers.
- 13.1.2 **Billing Information Generation:**
  - 13.1.2.1 Automatically generate billing details after each consultation, including the consultation fee, tests, procedures, medications, diagnostic services, etc.
  - 13.1.2.2 Link the patient's treatment plan, diagnoses, and clinical procedures with billing codes.
  - 13.1.2.3 Support for dynamic billing: Billing information should be updated in real-time based on new treatments, medications, tests, or additional services.

### 13.2 Charge Capture and Fee Structure: P2

- 13.2.1 **Automatic Fee Calculation:**
  - 13.2.1.1 Integrate with **hospital fee structures** for services such as consultations, laboratory tests, imaging, pharmacy, and surgery.
  - 13.2.1.2 **Customizable fees:** Ability to define different fee structures for specific patient categories (e.g., insurance-covered, self-paying, low-income, etc.).
- 13.2.1.3 Option to include or exclude **service taxes** (e.g., VAT, GST) and other additional charges.
- 13.2.2 **Itemized Bill Generation:**
  - 13.2.2.1 Provide an **itemized bill** with a breakdown of services rendered, detailing individual charges (e.g., doctor's fee, test fees, medicine charges).
  - 13.2.2.2 **Discounts and concessions** can be applied to the itemized bill as needed (e.g., hospital-sponsored discounts, insurance approvals).

### 13.3 Insurance Processing and Claims Management: P2

- 13.3.1 **Insurance Information Capture:**
  - 13.3.1.1 Capture and store **patient insurance information** including provider details, policy number, coverage limits, and co-payment details.
  - 13.3.1.2 Verify **insurance eligibility** automatically before initiating services.
- 13.3.2 **Claims Generation:**

- 13.3.2.1 Automatically generate **insurance claims** based on the billing information and in the appropriate format.
- 13.3.2.2 Support for **multiple insurance plans**, including primary and secondary insurance.
- 13.3.2.3 **Claims tracking:** Allow insurance claims to be tracked throughout the process (e.g., submitted, under review, approved, denied).
- 13.3.3 **Claim Rejections & Adjustments:**
  - 13.3.3.1 Provide tools to manage **claim rejections and denials**, with an option to modify or appeal claims.
  - 13.3.3.2 Generate **adjustment entries** when necessary, reflecting insurance reimbursements or patient's out-of-pocket costs.
- 13.3.4 **Co-payments and Deductibles:**
  - 13.3.4.1 Automatically calculate **patient co-pays and deductibles** based on the insurance policy.
  - 13.3.4.2 Provide a clear breakdown of **patient responsibilities** (e.g., out-of-pocket expenses, co-pays, deductibles) vs. insurance coverage.

### **13.4 Payment Management: P2**

- 13.4.1 **Payment Modes:**
  - 13.4.1.1 Accept multiple **payment modes** (e.g., credit/debit card, cash, cheque, online payment, insurance payment).
  - 13.4.1.2 Support for **online payment gateways** for real-time payments through the patient portal or mobile app.
  - 13.4.1.3 Integration with **payment processing systems** to automatically update patient balances upon successful payment.
- 13.4.2 **Payment Collection:**
  - 13.4.2.1 Track **partial payments, deposit payments, or full payments** and adjust the remaining balance accordingly.
  - 13.4.2.2 Ability to issue **payment receipts** for all types of transactions.
- 13.4.3 **Payment Installments:**
  - 13.4.3.1 Offer **installment payment plans** for high-cost services (e.g., surgeries or long-term treatments) with agreed-upon amounts and timelines.
- 13.4.3.2 Record **payment schedules** for future payments and send automated **payment reminders**.
- 13.4.4 **Payment History:**
  - 13.4.4.1 Maintain a **payment history** for each patient, accessible by both the patient and the healthcare provider.
  - 13.4.4.2 **Statement generation:** Allow patients to download or receive monthly billing statements detailing payments, outstanding amounts, and any insurance-related transactions.

### **13.5 Refunds and Adjustments: P2**

- 13.5.1 **Refund Processing:**
  - 13.5.1.1 Support for **refund requests** for overpayments or duplicate payments.
  - 13.5.1.2 Ability to **adjust patient bills** in case of billing errors, insurance adjustments, or changes in the treatment plan.
- 13.5.2 **Adjustment Tracking:**
  - 13.5.2.1 Track **adjustments** made to billing (e.g., discounts, insurance adjustments, or corrections in billing codes).
  - 13.5.2.2 Generate **adjustment reports** for internal audits.

### **13.6 Patient Financial Summary: P2**

- 13.6.1 **Patient Invoice:**

- 13.6.1.1 An easy-to-understand **patient financial summary** including services rendered, charges, discounts, insurance details, and payments made.
- 13.6.1.2 A final bill that clearly differentiates between the patient's responsibility and the insurance contribution.
- 13.6.2 Outstanding Balance:
  - 13.6.2.1 Display **outstanding balance** with a clear breakdown of charges, payments, and adjustments.
  - 13.6.2.2 Offer **payment options** for outstanding balances, including links to online payment gateways or instructions for in-person payments.

## 14 Dashboard

### 14.1 Real-Time Data Overview: P1

- 14.1.1 Patient Flow Monitoring:
  - 14.1.1.1 Display real-time data on the **current patient flow** in the OPD, including:
    - 14.1.1.1.1 **Number of patients waiting** for consultation.
    - 14.1.1.1.2 **Patients being treated** or in consultation.
    - 14.1.1.1.3 **Patients in the pharmacy** or waiting for lab results.
    - 14.1.1.1.4 **Triage status** of patients.
  - 14.1.2 Live Appointment Information:
    - 14.1.2.1 Show live information on upcoming appointments and consultation schedules.
      - 14.1.2.1.1 Display the list of patients to be seen by the doctor and their estimated waiting time.
      - 14.1.2.1.2 Allow filtering by department, doctor, or time slot.
    - 14.1.3 Staff Availability and Scheduling:
      - 14.1.3.1 Real-time status of **staff availability**, including doctors, nurses, and administrative personnel.
        - 14.1.3.1.1 Show which staff are on-duty, on-call, or absent.
      - 14.1.3.1.2 Display **shift overlaps or staff shortages** for optimal resource allocation.

### 14.2 Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) and Metrics:

- 14.2.1 Patient Volume & Visits: P1
  - 14.2.1.1 **Daily/weekly/monthly statistics** on the number of patients visiting the OPD.
  - 14.2.1.2 Break down by patient **category** (e.g., new vs. follow-up) and by **department** (e.g., cardiology, orthopedics).
  - 14.2.1.3 **Real-time patient count** across various stages (waiting, consultation, treatment).
- 14.2.2 Consultation and Wait Times: P2
  - 14.2.2.1 Track **average wait time** per patient and display it in real-time.
  - 14.2.2.2 Show the **average consultation time** for doctors and **patient satisfaction** based on wait times.
- 14.2.3 Revenue and Billing Data: P3
  - 14.2.3.1 Display **daily/weekly/monthly revenue** generated from OPD consultations, diagnostic tests, and pharmacy sales.
  - 14.2.3.2 Show **outstanding payments, insurance claims, and billing discrepancies**.
- 14.2.4 Appointment Trends: P1
  - 14.2.4.1 Visualize **appointment trends** over time, identifying peak hours/days and allowing for better resource planning.
  - 14.2.4.2 Display **no-show rates and cancellation rates** for appointments.
- 14.2.5 Resource Utilization: P2

14.2.5.1 Track and visualize **resource utilization** in the OPD, including:

14.2.5.1.1 **Room occupancy** (consultation and examination rooms).

14.2.5.1.2 **Medical equipment usage** (e.g., ECG machines, ultrasound).

14.2.5.1.3 **Staff workload** (e.g., number of patients handled per doctor/nurse).

### **14.3 Patient Demographics and Analytics**

14.3.1 Demographic Breakdown: P1

14.3.1.1 Display real-time **patient demographic data**, including:

14.3.1.1.1 Age, gender, location, and insurance status.

14.3.1.1.2 **Disease trends**: What common conditions are being treated in the OPD?

14.3.1.1.3 **Specialty-based patient distribution** (e.g., cardiology, pediatrics).

14.3.2 **Disease Prevalence**: P1

14.3.2.1 Visualize data on the **prevalence of specific diseases** within the outpatient population.

14.3.2.2 Show trends over time to help hospital management allocate resources accordingly (e.g., increase cardiology staff during peak heart disease season).

14.3.3 **Patient Feedback and Satisfaction**: P2

14.3.3.1 Display **real-time patient satisfaction scores** based on feedback collected via surveys or ratings after consultations.

14.3.3.2 Analyze common patient complaints (e.g., waiting times, service quality) to help with process improvement.

### **14.4 Operational Alerts and Notifications**

14.4.1 **System Alerts**: P2

14.4.1.1 Display real-time **alerts** for important events, such as:

14.4.1.1.1 Staff absenteeism or shift changes.

14.4.1.1.2 Overbooked schedules or appointment conflicts.

14.4.1.1.3 Critical equipment malfunctions or maintenance required.

14.4.2 **Resource Shortages**: P2

14.4.2.1 Alert administrators to any shortage of medical supplies, medications, or staff.

14.4.2.2 Send notifications for scheduled equipment maintenance or room availability.

14.4.3 **Pending Tasks/Requests**: P2

14.4.3.1 Show a list of **pending administrative tasks** or requests that need attention (e.g., patient referrals, insurance approvals, or billing inquiries).

### **14.5 Visual Data Representation**

14.5.1 **Graphical Analytics**: P2

14.5.1.1 Use **charts, graphs, and heat maps** to visualize data for easier understanding and quick decision-making.

14.5.1.1.1 **Line charts** for appointment trends, patient visits, and revenue over time.

14.5.1.1.2 **Bar charts** for resource utilization and staff performance.

14.5.1.1.3 **Pie charts** for demographic distribution and disease prevalence.

14.5.2 **Heat Maps**: P2

14.5.2.1 Show **heat maps of patient flow**, helping administrators visualize high-traffic areas and time periods in the OPD.

14.5.2.2 Use **color-coded indicators** (e.g., green for normal, yellow for warning, red for critical) to highlight areas that need attention.

### **14.6 Reports and Insights**

14.6.1 **Automated Reporting**: P2

14.6.1.1 Generate automated **daily/weekly/monthly reports** on OPD performance, staff activity, patient demographics, and revenue generation.

14.6.1.2 Provide options to export reports in different formats (e.g., PDF, Excel) for analysis or compliance.

14.6.2 Custom Report Builder:

- Allow users to **create custom reports** based on specific data points such as patient visit statistics, financial performance, or staff productivity.

14.6.3 Comparative Analysis: P2

14.6.3.1 Allow comparison of current data with previous periods (e.g., **year-over-year or month-over-month performance**).

14.6.3.2 Display **benchmarking data** to compare performance with other healthcare institutions, if available.

### **14.7 Mobile and Remote Access**

14.7.1 Mobile Dashboard: P3

14.7.1.1 Ensure the dashboard is **mobile-friendly** or has a dedicated mobile app for real-time access by doctors, managers, or other hospital staff, allowing them to track patient flow, resources, and KPIs from anywhere in the hospital.

14.7.2 Remote Access: P3

14.7.2.1 Allow authorized users to access the dashboard remotely via **secure login**, enabling management and staff to stay updated even when away from the hospital.

A photograph of the interior of an Emergency Department (ED) module. The floor is light-colored tile, and the ceiling has recessed lighting.
