  - 5.3.1.2 **Barcode scanning:** The phlebotomist can scan a barcode or QR code on the patient's wristband or sample container to confirm that the correct sample is being collected for the right patient. (P-2)
  - 5.3.1.3 **Digital signature:** In cases of high-priority or critical samples, phlebotomists may need to sign electronically, confirming they've received the task and collected the sample. (P-1)
- 5.3.2 **Date and Time Stamping:** (P-1)  
The system must **automatically record** the date and time when the phlebotomist acknowledges the task. This helps in maintaining an audit trail for accountability and tracking.
- 5.3.3 Ensures the phlebotomist is responsible for acknowledging tasks, maintaining clear records of when and which tasks were completed.

## 5.4 Patient Identification & Verification

- 5.4.1 Display patient photo (optional), name, hospital ID, gender, ward, bed number, DOB (optional), and encounter info at time of collection(P-1)
- 5.4.2 Use of **barcode/wristband scanning** for patient ID verification (P-2)
- 5.4.3 Alerts for duplicate collections or wrong patient/sample pairing (P-2)
- 5.4.4 Reduces identification errors and improves patient safety. (P-2)

## 5.5 Sample Labeling and Barcode Generation

- 5.5.1 Automatic **barcode generation** for each sample container: (P-2)
  - 5.5.1.1 Includes patient ID, test code, sample ID, and collection date/time
- 5.5.2 Support for **reprinting** labels if needed (with audit trail) (P-2)
- 5.5.3 Use of pre-configured **label printers** compatible with LIS (P-2)
- 5.5.4 Each barcode must be unique and match sample + test. (P-2)

## 5.6 Sample Type & Container Management

- 5.6.1 Define required **specimen type** per test: (P-2)
  - 5.6.1.1 Whole blood, serum, plasma, urine, CSF, tissue, etc.
- 5.6.2 Define required **container types** per specimen: (P-2)
  - 5.6.2.1 EDTA, citrate, plain, sterile cup, swab, etc.
- 5.6.3 Auto-suggest or warn if wrong container used (P-2)

## 5.7 Sample Collection Details(P-1)

Capture the following at the time of sample collection:

- 5.7.1 Collector name and ID
- 5.7.2 Collection date and time
- 5.7.3 Location (ward/OPD/phlebectomy/home)
- 5.7.4 Pre-test conditions (e.g., fasting, timed collection)
- 5.7.5 Special instructions or notes (e.g., difficult draw, hemolyzed)

## 5.8 Sample Collection Confirmation(P-1)

- 5.8.1 **Sample Collection Status Updates:**

After the sample is collected, the phlebotomist should update the system with the status of the sample collection. The statuses might include:

- 5.8.1.1 **Sample Collected:** Confirmation that the sample has been successfully collected.
- 5.8.1.2 **Sample Collected with Issues:** If there were any challenges during collection (e.g., difficulty in drawing blood), the phlebotomist can note this.
- 5.8.1.3 **Sample Not Collected:** In cases where the sample could not be collected (e.g., patient refusal, technical difficulties), the phlebotomist must mark the status accordingly.

- 5.8.2 **Patient Consent Verification:** (P-3)

The system should ensure that **patient consent** for the sample collection is obtained before the procedure. The phlebotomist should acknowledge this within the system as part of the sample collection process, especially for sensitive tests like blood transfusion or genetic testing.

- 5.8.3 Reduces errors and confirms that the correct sample is collected from the right patient with the necessary consent.

## 5.9 Sample Status Tracking (P-1)

- 5.9.1 Maintain real-time status of each sample:
  - 5.9.1.1 Pending
  - 5.9.1.2 Collected
  - 5.9.1.3 In Transit
  - 5.9.1.4 Received in Lab
  - 5.9.1.5 Rejected
  - 5.9.1.6 Processing
  - 5.9.1.7 Repeat
- 5.9.2 Sample status should be viewable by lab, nurses, and clinicians (with role limits).

## 5.10 Sample Transport & Handover(P-2)

- 5.10.1 Log **handover of samples** from collection point to lab reception
- 5.10.2 Generate **batch slips** for bulk sample transfers
- 5.10.3 Record transport time, staff name, and receiving personnel
- 5.10.4 Temperature logging support (for sensitive samples)
- 5.10.5 Helps maintain chain of custody and supports accreditation (NABL/CAP/ISO).

## 5.11 Sample Rejection Workflow(P-2)

- 5.11.1 Allow rejection with mandatory reason:
  - 5.11.1.1 Hemolysis, insufficient volume, wrong container, labeling error
- 5.11.2 Optional image upload for documentation (e.g. hemolyzed sample)
- 5.11.3 Auto-notification to ordering clinician
- 5.11.4 Re-collection workflow initiation (with reference to original order)

## 5.12 Priority and Urgency Handling(P-1)

- 5.12.1 Highlight **STAT/urgent** tests for fast-track collection and delivery
- 5.12.2 Flag critical samples for special handling (e.g., blood gas, CSF)
- 5.12.3 Auto-prioritization based on test configuration

## 5.13 Home Sample Collection (Optional) (P-2)

- 5.13.1 Scheduling interface for **home collection**:
  - 5.13.1.1 Address, time slot, special instructions
- 5.13.2 Technician mobile app integration for:
  - 5.13.2.1 Route planning
  - 5.13.2.2 Barcode scanning
  - 5.13.2.3 Digital patient signature
  - 5.13.2.4 On-the-spot labeling

## 5.14 Alerts & Notifications (P-2)

- 5.14.1 Alert lab if:
  - 5.14.1.1 Sample is not collected within scheduled time
  - 5.14.1.2 Sample has exceeded allowable **transport time**
  - 5.14.1.3 Duplicate sample ID is attempted
- 5.14.2 Alert clinicians when:

5.14.2.1 Sample rejected

5.14.2.2 Sample delayed significantly

## 5.15 Integration with LIS/Analyzer(P-2)

5.15.1 Sample barcode scanned into device or middleware system

5.15.2 Status update automatically synced with HMIS

5.15.3 Support for sample rerouting (e.g., Microbiology → Molecular Lab)

## 6 Lab Sections Acknowledgment of Samples Receipt

### 6.1 Task Assignment to Laboratory Sections(P-2)

#### 6.1.1 Test Order Routing:

Once a test order is created (whether initiated by a consultant, non-consultant provider, or via CPT Counter Orders), it should be routed to the appropriate laboratory **section** based on the test type (e.g., hematology, biochemistry, microbiology, etc.).

6.1.1.1 **Automatic routing:** The system should automatically identify the section based on predefined test criteria (e.g., a CBC goes to Hematology, a liver function test goes to Biochemistry).

6.1.1.2 **Manual rerouting:** In cases of unusual test requests or complex multidisciplinary tests, authorized users should have the ability to manually assign the test to the appropriate section.

#### 6.1.2 Task Visibility:

Once assigned, the **laboratory section** should have clear visibility of all the **pending tests**. The system should provide:

6.1.2.1 **Real-time list** of tests to be processed.

6.1.2.2 **Priority level** of each test (routine, STAT, urgent).

6.1.2.3 **Test-specific instructions** that may be relevant for that section (e.g., special handling, test-specific preparation).

6.1.3 Ensures tests are properly routed to the correct sections of the laboratory for processing.

### 6.2 Section Acknowledgment of Task Receipt(P-2)

#### 6.2.1 Acknowledgment of Task:

The **section head** or **assigned technician** in each section (e.g., hematology, microbiology) must acknowledge receipt of the test order or sample. This acknowledgment confirms:

6.2.1.1 The **section** is aware of the task.

6.2.1.2 The **task is ready to be processed** within the appropriate timeframe.

6.2.1.3 **Resources (staff, equipment)** are available to perform the test.

6.2.2 Methods of Acknowledgment:

6.2.2.1 **Click-to-confirm acknowledgment:** When the section receives the task, the technician can click a button or checkbox in the system to acknowledge the order.

6.2.2.2 **Barcode or QR code scanning:** The technician can scan the **sample ID barcode** or **test order barcode** for quick acknowledgment and validation.

6.2.2.3 **Digital signature:** In certain high-priority tests, a digital signature from the technician or section head might be required to confirm task acknowledgment.

6.2.3 **Date and Time Stamping:**

The system should automatically record the **date** and **time** of each acknowledgment. This timestamp helps in tracking how long it took for a section to acknowledge and begin processing the sample.

6.2.4 Ensures accountability and clarity in when each section acknowledges the task.

### 6.3 Task Processing Confirmation by the Section(P-2)

6.3.1 **Processing Status Updates:**

After acknowledgment, the section should update the system with the **status of the test processing**. The possible status updates can include:

6.3.1.1 **In Progress:** The test is currently being processed.

6.3.1.2 **Completed:** The test has been completed, and results are ready.

6.3.1.3 **Delayed:** If there are delays in processing (e.g., equipment malfunction, insufficient sample), the section should update the status as **delayed**.

6.3.1.4 **Unable to Process:** If there's a problem (e.g., insufficient sample, test failure), the section should notify the system that the task cannot be processed.

6.3.2 **Error Reporting:**

The section should be able to report any issues that occur during the processing of the test, such as:

6.3.2.1 **Sample inadequacy** (e.g., hemolysis, insufficient volume).

6.3.2.2 **Technical errors** (e.g., faulty equipment).

6.3.2.3 **Processing Issues** (e.g., incorrect reagents).

6.3.3 **Task Completion and Notification:**

Once the test is complete, the section should confirm the **completion** in the system. This could trigger:

6.3.3.1 **A notification to the requesting clinician** or consultant.

6.3.3.2 **Automatic updating of the task status to "Completed"**.

6.3.3.3 **The ability to prepare the test result for further processing or review**.

6.3.4 Ensures that each section tracks its progress and issues any necessary alerts in real-time.

### 6.4 Section-Specific Comments and Documentation(P-2)

6.4.1 **Comments Section:**

Each section should have the ability to add **comments or notes** to the test order to

explain any issues, clarifications, or special circumstances that arose during the processing of the test. These comments can include:

6.4.1.1 Special handling requirements.

6.4.1.2 Deviations from standard protocols.

6.4.1.3 Patient-specific instructions (e.g., fasting requirements for blood tests).

6.4.2 **Documentation Attachments:**

In addition to comments, each section can attach relevant **documentation** (e.g., images of slides, graphs, or test-specific results) to the order, ensuring that all information related to the sample is consolidated.

6.4.3 Allows sections to document and share important information about the test processing.

## 6.5 Cross-Section Communication(P-3)

6.5.1 **Inter-Section Communication:**

The system should provide a **communication channel** for different laboratory sections to communicate directly with each other. For example:

6.5.1.1 **Hematology** can ask **Microbiology** for confirmation on sample quality.

6.5.1.2 **Biochemistry** may need to request additional samples from **Phlebotomy** if the first sample was insufficient.

6.5.2 **Internal Alerts and Notifications:**

If there are any issues requiring cross-sectional collaboration (e.g., discrepancies in results, need for further tests), the system should send internal alerts or notifications to the relevant sections.

6.5.3 Improves communication and collaboration between laboratory sections to resolve issues quickly.

## 6.6 Audit Trail and Compliance(P-3)

6.6.1 **Audit Trail:**

The system must maintain a **detailed audit trail** for each test order and sample, recording:

6.6.1.1 Who acknowledged the test and when.

6.6.1.2 Who processed the test and when.

6.6.1.3 Any updates or modifications made by different sections (e.g., test status updates, special instructions).

6.6.1.4 Comments made by different sections throughout the process.

6.6.2 **Compliance Reporting:**

The audit trail helps ensure **compliance** with regulatory standards (e.g., **CLIA**, **CAP**) and hospital policies, ensuring that all required actions were completed by the relevant sections at each step of the testing process.

6.6.3 Ensures full traceability of each test and maintains compliance with regulatory standards.

## 6.7 Performance Metrics and Reporting(P-2)

6.7.1 **Section Performance Reports:**

The HMIS should be able to generate **performance reports** for each laboratory section, allowing managers to track key metrics such as:

- 6.7.1.1 **Test acknowledgment times:** How long it takes for each section to acknowledge tasks.
- 6.7.1.2 **Test processing times:** Average time taken for each section to process and complete tests.
- 6.7.1.3 **Delays and bottlenecks:** Identifying sections that may have frequent delays in test processing.
- 6.7.2 **Quality Control:**  
Reports should also include **quality metrics** related to test accuracy, error rates, and sample integrity, helping to identify areas for improvement within each laboratory section.
- 6.7.3 Provides insight into laboratory performance and helps identify inefficiencies.

## 7 Test Cancellation (P-2)

### 7.1 Cancellation Criteria

- 7.1.1 **Test Cancellation Reasons:**  
The system should allow users to select from predefined reasons for cancellation. These reasons should be customizable and can include:
  - 7.1.1.1 **Duplicate order:** If the same test is ordered multiple times.
  - 7.1.1.2 **Incorrect test:** If the wrong test is ordered (e.g., wrong test type or wrong patient).
  - 7.1.1.3 **Patient withdrawal:** If the patient decides not to proceed with the test.
  - 7.1.1.4 **Test no longer required:** If the clinician decides that the test is not necessary.
  - 7.1.1.5 **Sample issue:** If there's a problem with the sample (e.g., insufficient volume, contamination).
  - 7.1.1.6 **Lab error:** If the test was ordered in error by the laboratory staff.
  - 7.1.1.7 **Patient's medical condition change:** If the patient's condition changes and the test is no longer needed.
- 7.1.2 **Customizable Reason List:**  
The list of reasons for test cancellation should be customizable to fit the specific workflows and policies of the hospital or clinical laboratory.
- 7.1.3 Provides clear, consistent reasons for canceling tests, ensuring proper documentation.

### 7.2 Authorization and Workflow for Test Cancellation(P-3)

- 7.2.1 **User Roles and Permissions:**  
The cancellation process should be controlled based on user roles. For example:

- 7.2.1.1 **Clinicians or ordering physicians** should be able to cancel tests, provided they have the correct permissions.
- 7.2.1.2 **Laboratory personnel** (e.g., laboratory technician, phlebotomist) should also have the ability to cancel tests, especially if they have not yet been processed.
- 7.2.1.3 **Supervisors/Managers** should be involved in the approval process if the cancellation is for more complex reasons (e.g., lab errors or clinical discretion).

The system should have role-based access controls to ensure only authorized users can cancel tests.

#### 7.2.2 Approval Workflow:

A **multi-step approval process** should be implemented in certain cases, particularly for high-cost or critical tests (e.g., cancer screenings or genetic testing). This approval process might involve:

- 7.2.2.1 **Initial cancellation request** by the clinician or lab staff.
- 7.2.2.2 **Review and approval** by a lab supervisor or the attending physician.
- 7.2.2.3 **Final confirmation** from the lab manager or senior pathologist.

- 7.2.3 Ensures that test cancellations are managed properly and authorized by the right personnel.

### 7.3 Automated Notifications and Alerts(P-3)

#### 7.3.1 Clinician Notification:

When a test is canceled, the **ordering clinician** should receive an automatic notification via the HMIS system, email, or SMS, depending on the urgency and nature of the cancellation. This ensures that the clinician is aware of any changes or decisions regarding the test.

#### 7.3.2 Laboratory Notification:

The laboratory team should receive a notification about the cancellation, allowing them to stop any ongoing tests or preparations. The system should also provide reminders for canceled tests to avoid any confusion or backlog.

#### 7.3.3 Patient Notification:

In cases where it is appropriate or required (e.g., patient withdrawal or a significant change in medical condition), the **patient** should be informed about the test cancellation. This could be through direct communication from the clinical staff or via a **patient portal**.

#### 7.3.4 Alert for Duplicate Cancellations:

The system should have an alert mechanism to prevent a test from being canceled multiple times, which could create confusion and disrupt workflows.

- 7.3.5 Ensures that all stakeholders (clinicians, laboratory staff, and patients) are informed in a timely and organized manner.

### 7.4 Test Status Update(P-3)

#### 7.4.1 Status Tracking:

Once a test is canceled, the system should automatically update the test status to **“Cancelled”** in the **test order management system**. This status should reflect in various parts of the system, such as:

7.4.1.1 **Test results:** If the test was partially processed, any results or data generated up until that point should be properly flagged.

7.4.1.2 **Patient records:** The patient's medical record should reflect that the test was canceled and provide the reason for cancellation.

7.4.2 **Sample Handling:**

If the test is canceled after the sample has been collected, the system should also document what happens to the sample. This includes:

7.4.2.1 **Sample storage:** Whether the sample is discarded, stored for potential retesting, or reused for a different test.

7.4.2.2 **Condition of the sample:** If the sample was already processed, whether it was discarded or preserved for future use.

7.4.3 Tracks test cancellation status and ensures that all relevant data is updated accordingly in the system.

## 7.5 Audit Trail and Documentation(P-3)

7.5.1 **Comprehensive Audit Trail:**

The system should maintain a **complete audit trail** of all cancellation activities, including:

7.5.1.1 **Who initiated the cancellation?**

7.5.1.2 **Who approved the cancellation?**

7.5.1.3 **Reason for cancellation.**

7.5.1.4 **Time and date of cancellation.**

7.5.1.5 **Any changes made post-cancellation.**

This ensures traceability and accountability for each cancellation request.

7.5.2 **Cancellation Documentation:**

Detailed documentation related to the cancellation should be available, including any clinical notes, laboratory staff comments, and communications with the patient or clinician. This helps provide a transparent history of why the cancellation took place.

7.5.3 Ensures compliance, accountability, and transparency through detailed audit trails and documentation.

## 7.6 Billing and Financial Impact Management(P-3)

7.6.1 **Billing Adjustments:**

If a test is canceled after being ordered or processed, the HMIS should ensure that **billing** is adjusted accordingly:

7.6.1.1 **Partial or Full Refunds:** If the test was charged, a partial or full refund should be issued based on hospital policy and billing protocols.

7.6.1.2 **Cancellation Fee:** Some tests may have a cancellation fee due to preparatory work done (e.g., specimen collection, processing). The system should handle these financial adjustments.

7.6.2 **Cost Tracking:**

The system should track the costs associated with the cancellation, including the

materials or resources used. This helps in auditing and understanding the financial impact of cancellations.

- 7.6.3 Ensures that financials are updated in real time, ensuring accurate billing and adjustments.

## 7.7 Reporting and Analytics(P-3)

### 7.7.1 Cancellation Reports:

The system should be able to generate **reports** on test cancellations, providing insights into:

- 7.7.1.1 **Frequency of cancellations:** How often tests are canceled and the most common reasons for cancellation.
- 7.7.1.2 **Impact on turnaround time (TAT):** How cancellations affect overall lab efficiency and turnaround time for tests.
- 7.7.1.3 **Financial Impact:** The direct or indirect financial consequences of canceled tests (e.g., refunds, lost revenue).

### 7.7.2 Trend Analysis:

The system can support **trend analysis** by identifying patterns over time, such as frequent cancellations of a particular test or tests canceled due to clinician errors. This can help improve laboratory processes or guide clinical decision-making.

- 7.7.3 Provides valuable insights into cancellation trends and performance metrics for continuous improvement.

## 7.8 Re-Order Option Post-Cancellation(P-3)

### 7.8.1 Reordering Tests:

If a test is canceled, clinicians may need to reorder it. The system should allow for:

- 7.8.1.1 **Reordering the same test:** A simple option for the clinician to reorder the same test without re-entering all the information.
- 7.8.1.2 **Related test suggestions:** If the test cancellation is due to a clinical change, the system may suggest **alternative tests** or **related investigations** that may be more appropriate based on the patient's current condition.

- 7.8.2 Ensures that the cancellation process does not disrupt patient care and provides continuity in testing.

# 8 Result Entry Modes

## 8.1 Manual Entry Interface(P-1)

### 8.1.1 User-friendly form for each test result:

- 8.1.1.1 Numeric values (with units)
  - 8.1.1.2 Text results (e.g. culture report)
  - 8.1.1.3 Dropdowns for interpretation (e.g. Positive/Negative)
  - 8.1.1.4 Support for microbiology reports (e.g. organism identification + sensitivity)
- 8.1.2 Keyboard shortcuts or batch entry support for high-volume tests (e.g., CBC)

## 8.2 Automated Device Integration(P-2)

- 8.2.1 Results auto-imported from lab instruments via:
  - 8.2.1.1 HL7, ASTM, or proprietary middleware interfaces
- 8.2.2 Validation of:
  - 8.2.2.1 Barcode/sample ID match
  - 8.2.2.2 Patient/test mapping
  - 8.2.2.3 Time of testing
- 8.2.3 System should support **manual override/edit** if auto-imported results need correction (with audit trail).

### 8.3 Reference Range Handling(P-1)

- 8.3.1 Display reference range dynamically based on:
  - 8.3.1.1 Age
  - 8.3.1.2 Gender
  - 8.3.1.3 Pregnancy status (if applicable)
  - 8.3.1.4 Unit of measurement
  - 8.3.1.5 Area-wise distribution
- 8.3.2 Support for custom ranges per test or lab (P-2)

Out-of-range results should be **highlighted or color-coded** (e.g., red for high/low), (P-1)

### 8.4 Result Flagging(P-2)

- 8.4.1 Automatically flag:
  - 8.4.1.1 **Abnormal values**
  - 8.4.1.2 **Critical values** (requiring urgent clinical action)
  - 8.4.1.3 **Delta check** alerts (significant change from last result)
- 8.4.2 Configurable thresholds per test

Trigger **alerts or warnings** for critical results before validation.

### 8.5 Multi-Level Validation Workflow(P-2)

- 8.5.1 Entry Level (Lab Technician)
  - 8.5.1.1 Inputs results
  - 8.5.1.2 Marks as ready for validation
- 8.5.2 First Validation (Senior Technician / Specialist)
  - 8.5.2.1 Reviews result for accuracy
  - 8.5.2.2 Flags for rechecking if needed
- 8.5.3 Final Validation (Pathologist / Consultant)
  - 8.5.3.1 Approves or rejects results
  - 8.5.3.2 Option to add interpretation or comments
  - 8.5.3.3 Signs off electronically

System must **record timestamps, user IDs, and validation levels** for traceability.

## **8.6 Bulk Result Management(P-3)**

- 8.6.1 Tools for:
  - 8.6.1.1 Batch entry (e.g., enter all glucose results for 50 patients)
  - 8.6.1.2 Bulk validation of normal results (auto-validation rules)
  - 8.6.1.3 Batch release of finalized reports

Reduces time in high-throughput labs.

## **8.7 Amendment & Correction Workflow(P-2)**

- 8.7.1 Allow authorized users to **edit/correct** already validated results
- 8.7.2 Record:
  - 8.7.2.1 Reason for amendment
  - 8.7.2.2 Who made the change
  - 8.7.2.3 Who approved it
  - 8.7.2.4 Original value (preserved in audit trail)

Complies with lab quality standards (e.g., CAP, ISO 15189).

## **8.8 Result Comments & Interpretation(P-1)**

- 8.8.1 Free-text or template-based comment section per test
- 8.8.2 Support for:
  - 8.8.2.1 Microbiology sensitivity interpretation
  - 8.8.2.2 Histopathology/molecular report narratives
- 8.8.3 Auto-suggestions for common interpretations (configurable)

## **8.9 Report Formatting & Preview(P-1)**

- 8.9.1 Preview full report before releasing:
  - 8.9.1.1 Patient details
  - 8.9.1.2 Test results
  - 8.9.1.3 Comments/interpretation
  - 8.9.1.4 Lab and doctor signatures
  - 8.9.1.5 Sample in-out time
  - 8.9.1.6 Diagnosis
  - 8.9.1.7 Advising doctor/consultant details
- 8.9.2 Support for draft mode and final report status

## **8.10 Report Release & Locking(P-1)**

- 8.10.1 Once validated, mark result/report as:
  - 8.10.1.1 Final
  - 8.10.1.2 Locked (non-editable unless reopened with justification)
  - 8.10.1.3 Auto-release or scheduled release options

## **8.11 Turnaround Time (TAT) Monitoring(P-2)**

- 8.11.1 Log:

- 8.11.1.1 Time of test ordering

- 8.11.1.2 Time of sample collection

- 8.11.1.3 Time of result entry

- 8.11.1.4 Time of final validation

- 8.11.2 TAT reports by test, department, or technician

## 8.12 Critical Result Notification(P-1)

- 8.12.1 Auto-alert (SMS/email/popup) to clinician for:

- 8.12.1.1 Life-threatening values (e.g.,  $K^+ < 2.5$  or  $> 6.5$ )

- 8.12.1.2 Infectious disease confirmations (e.g., TB, HIV, COVID)

- 8.12.2 Optional escalation matrix (e.g., notify consultant if no action in 30 mins)

## 9 Report Generation(P-1)

### 9.1 Report Format & Template Configuration

- 9.1.1 Support for multiple report templates:

- 9.1.1.1 By department (e.g., Biochemistry, Microbiology, Pathology)

- 9.1.1.2 By test type or profile (e.g., CBC, LFT, Culture & Sensitivity)

- 9.1.1.3 Pathology narrative reports (Histopathology, Cytology)

- 9.1.2 Templates must be **customizable**:

- 9.1.2.1 Logo, hospital name, accreditation info (e.g., NABL, ISO 15189)

- 9.1.2.2 Header/footer settings

- 9.1.2.3 Font styles, size, and alignment

- 9.1.3 Ensure consistency and professional presentation across all departments.

### 9.2 Patient & Clinical Details on Report(P-1)

- 9.2.1 Each lab report must include:

- 9.2.1.1 Patient full name, hospital ID, age, gender, and contact info

- 9.2.1.2 Encounter info: inpatient/outpatient status, department, doctor

- 9.2.1.3 Sample collection date/time, test date, reporting date

- 9.2.1.4 Barcode or sample ID for traceability

- 9.2.1.5 Ordering physician's name

Complies with documentation standards and helps in result interpretation.

### 9.3 Result Presentation(P-1)

- 9.3.1 Clearly display:

- 9.3.1.1 Test name

- 9.3.1.2 Result (numeric/text)

- 9.3.1.3 Unit

- 9.3.1.4 Reference range

- 9.3.1.5 Flags for abnormal/critical results (e.g., high/low indicators, red text)

- 9.3.2 Support for **delta values** or historical comparison

- 9.3.3 Add graphical representation for trend analysis (e.g., glucose levels over time)

### 9.4 Interpretation & Comments Section(P-1)

- 9.4.1 Optional space for:
  - 9.4.1.1 Pathologist/consultant interpretation
  - 9.4.1.2 Microbiology sensitivity interpretation (e.g., "E. coli sensitive to Ciprofloxacin")
  - 9.4.1.3 Notes for clinicians (e.g., fasting/non-fasting, sample condition)
- 9.4.2 Predefined or free-text format

## 9.5 Digital Signatures & Authentication(P-1)

- 9.5.1 Allow electronic signature of pathologist or validating doctor
- 9.5.2 Include:
  - 9.5.2.1 Name
  - 9.5.2.2 Qualification
  - 9.5.2.3 Registration/license number
  - 9.5.2.4 Designation (e.g., Consultant Pathologist)
- 9.5.3 Timestamp of final approval
- 9.5.4 Digital certificate support for higher security (optional)

## 9.6 Report Status Control(P-2)

- 9.6.1 Clearly mark status:
  - 9.6.1.1 Draft
  - 9.6.1.2 Under Review
  - 9.6.1.3 Final
  - 9.6.1.4 Amended (with original result shown if applicable)
- 9.6.2 Lock final reports to prevent unauthorized edits
- 9.6.3 Support **addenda** or corrections (with audit logs)

## 9.7 Report Delivery Options(P-1)

- 9.7.1 Download/print as **PDF** (with embedded barcodes/QR codes if needed)
- 9.7.2 Email to patient (with consent)
- 9.7.3 Push to:
  - 9.7.3.1 EMR/EHR for clinician access
  - 9.7.3.2 Patient portal/mobile app
  - 9.7.3.3 Referring physician (external network)
- 9.7.4 Generate batch reports (e.g., for ward round files, mass screenings)
- 9.7.5 Automates routine delivery tasks and improves turnaround time (TAT).

## 9.8 Report Archival and Retrieval(P-2)

- 9.8.1 Secure **long-term storage** of all reports
- 9.8.2 Indexing by:
  - 9.8.2.1 Patient ID
  - 9.8.2.2 Test type
  - 9.8.2.3 Date range
  - 9.8.2.4 Lab department
- 9.8.3 Advanced search and filter capabilities
- 9.8.4 Option to **reprint** old reports with watermark (e.g., "Duplicate Copy")

## 9.9 Bulk and Summary Reports(P-2)

- 9.9.1 Support for:
  - 9.9.1.1 Daily department-wise report bundles
  - 9.9.1.2 Test summary reports for epidemiological reporting
  - 9.9.1.3 Internal QC reports embedded into reports (if needed)
  - 9.9.1.4 Grouped patient reports (e.g., corporate checkups)

## 9.10 Mobile & Web Compatibility(P-2)

- 9.10.1 Reports must be viewable and downloadable via:
  - 9.10.1.1 Clinician dashboards
  - 9.10.1.2 Patient mobile apps or portals
- 9.10.2 Ensure responsive design and secure access on all devices

## 9.11 Visuals & Attachments Support(P-2)

- 9.11.1 Attach:
  - 9.11.1.1 Pathology images (e.g., slide photos)
  - 9.11.1.2 Radiology correlations (if applicable)
  - 9.11.1.3 PDF or JPEG documents from external sources
- 9.11.2 Limit file size and format based on system policy

# 10 Quality Control (QC) (P-2)

## 10.1 QC Program Configuration

- 10.1.1 Define and manage **internal and external QC programs** per lab section:
  - 10.1.1.1 Hematology, Biochemistry, Microbiology, Serology, Molecular, etc.
- 10.1.2 Set up **test-specific QC rules**, frequency, and materials:
  - 10.1.2.1 Daily, batch-wise, shift-wise, per reagent lot
- 10.1.3 Define control levels (e.g., Level 1: Normal, Level 2: Abnormal)
- 10.1.4 System must allow tailored QC schedules and controls per analyzer/test type.

## 10.2 QC Material & Lot Management(P-3)

- 10.2.1 Track details of control materials:
  - 10.2.1.1 Manufacturer
  - 10.2.1.2 Lot number
  - 10.2.1.3 Expiry date
  - 10.2.1.4 Target values and acceptable ranges
- 10.2.2 Alert for:
  - 10.2.2.1 Expired controls
  - 10.2.2.2 Reagent-lot nearing expiry
  - 10.2.2.3 Lot changeover needed
- 10.2.3 Ensures traceability and reduces use of invalid materials.

## 10.3 QC Data Entry & Import(P-3)

- 10.3.1 Allow **manual entry** of QC results (value, date/time, technician)
- 10.3.2 Support **automated Import** from instruments (if analyzers support it)
- 10.3.3 Link QC results to specific:

- 10.3.3.1 Instrument
- 10.3.3.2 Reagent lot
- 10.3.3.3 Control lot
- 10.3.3.4 Date and technician

10.3.4 All QC data must be tied to the testing environment for traceability.

#### **10.4 QC Rule Checking & Validation(P-2)**

- 10.4.1 Apply **Westgard Rules**, Levey-Jennings charts, or lab-defined rules:
  - 10.4.1.1 1-2s, 1-3s, 2-2s, R-4s, 4-1s, 10x, etc.
- 10.4.2 Highlight rule violations automatically
- 10.4.3 Block patient sample result release if QC fails (optional enforcement)
- 10.4.4 Prevents false result release and enforces reliability.

#### **10.5 QC Trend Charting & Visualization(P-2)**

- 10.5.1 Generate **Levey-Jennings charts** for each test/instrument:
  - 10.5.1.1 Mean, SD lines, plotted QC values
- 10.5.2 Support for:
  - 10.5.2.1 Daily/weekly/monthly trends
  - 10.5.2.2 Multi-level chart filters (test, device, control lot)
- 10.5.3 Export charts as image/PDF for documentation
- 10.5.4 Helps in detecting trends, shifts, or outliers before they affect patient testing.

#### **10.6 Instrument & Reagent Performance Tracking(P-3)**

- 10.6.1 Link QC performance to:
  - 10.6.1.1 Instrument ID
  - 10.6.1.2 Reagent batch number
  - 10.6.1.3 Control material lot
- 10.6.2 Identify patterns in QC failures (e.g., recurring problem with a batch)

#### **10.7 QC Review & Approval Workflow(P-2)**

- 10.7.1 Roles:
  - 10.7.1.1 **Technician** enters QC data
  - 10.7.1.2 **Senior Technician/Manager** reviews and validates QC
- 10.7.2 Mandatory comment field when:
  - 10.7.2.1 QC is out of range
  - 10.7.2.2 QC failure is overridden
- 10.7.3 Support for corrective action logging (e.g., recalibration, repeat QC)
- 10.7.4 Ensures every QC deviation is reviewed and justified.

#### **10.8 Preventive Blocking Based on QC Failure(P-3)**

- 10.8.1 Optional setting: prevent patient sample result entry if QC status is "Fail"
- 10.8.2 Configurable per test/instrument
- 10.8.3 Alert lab manager for immediate action

#### **10.9 External Quality Assessment (EQA) Tracking(P-3)**

- 10.9.1 Record EQA results:

- 10.9.1.1 Scheme name (e.g., EQAS, CAP PT)

- 10.9.1.2 Cycle and year

- 10.9.1.3 Sample data and test result

- 10.9.1.4 Submitted values and received feedback

- 10.9.1.5 Grading (Acceptable/Unacceptable)

- 10.9.2 Store certificates and documents

- 10.9.3 Supports accreditation and performance benchmarking.

## **10.10 Audit Trail & Compliance(P-3)**

- 10.10.1 Full logging of:

- 10.10.1.1 QC data entries

- 10.10.1.2 QC validations

- 10.10.1.3 Out-of-range handling

- 10.10.1.4 Corrective actions and review comments

- 10.10.2 Time-stamped with user ID and role

- 10.10.3 Support audit exports for NABL/CAP inspections

## **10.11 Alerts & Notifications(P-3)**

- 10.11.1 Notify lab supervisors of:

- 10.11.1.1 Repeated QC failures

- 10.11.1.2 Missed QC runs

- 10.11.1.3 Lot changes required

- 10.11.2 Dashboard indicators: "QC Pending", "QC Failed", "QC Due Soon"

## **10.12 Reporting & Analytics(P-3)**

- 10.12.1 Daily QC summary reports

- 10.12.2 Monthly compliance dashboards

- 10.12.3 QC trend reports (by test, department, instrument)

- 10.12.4 Error tracking (e.g., % of runs with QC failure)

# **11 Inventory & Reagent Management(P-3)**

## **11.1 Item Master Configuration**

- 11.1.1 Maintain a centralized **Lab Inventory Master** with:

- 11.1.1.1 Item code, name, category (e.g., Reagent, Consumable, Equipment part)

- 11.1.1.2 Unit of measure (e.g., mL, box, pack, unit)

- 11.1.1.3 Storage requirement (e.g., Room Temp, 2–8°C, -20°C)

- 11.1.1.4 Department-wise classification (e.g., Hematology, Microbiology)

- 11.1.1.5 Supplier and manufacturer info

- 11.1.1.6 Minimum shelf life alerts

- 11.1.2 Ensures standardization across departments.

## **11.2 Reagent Batch & Lot Management(P-3)**

- 11.2.1 Register each incoming reagent lot with:

- 11.2.1.1 Lot/Batch number
- 11.2.1.2 Expiry date
- 11.2.1.3 Date of manufacture
- 11.2.1.4 Certificate of analysis (upload able)
- 11.2.1.5 Assigned instruments/tests

#### 11.2.2 Alert if:

- 11.2.2.1 Expired or soon-to-expire lots are used
- 11.2.2.2 Lot tracking is missing on a result

#### 11.2.3 Critical for result traceability and NABL/CAP compliance.

### **11.3 Stock Level Management(P-3)**

#### 11.3.1 Define and monitor:

- 11.3.1.1 Minimum stock level
- 11.3.1.2 Reorder level
- 11.3.1.3 Safety stock

#### 11.3.2 Auto-alerts when stock falls below threshold

#### 11.3.3 Visibility of current and expected stock per item

### **11.4 Goods Receipt & Inspection(P-3)**

#### 11.4.1 Log all incoming stock:

- 11.4.1.1 PO/indent number
- 11.4.1.2 Supplier invoice
- 11.4.1.3 Quantity received vs ordered
- 11.4.1.4 Inspection status (Pass/Fail)

#### 11.4.2 Tag “Quarantine” status until verified by lab QC

### **11.5 Reagent Allocation & Consumption Tracking(P-3)**

#### 11.5.1 Allocate reagents to specific:

- 11.5.1.1 Instruments
- 11.5.1.2 Lab benches
- 11.5.1.3 Departments or tests

#### 11.5.2 Track consumption:

- 11.5.2.1 By test run (auto-linked with LIS)
- 11.5.2.2 Manual usage entry (e.g., for slides, tips, wipes)
- 11.5.3 Calculate cost per test (optional)
- 11.5.4 Enables cost optimization and waste reduction.

### **11.6 Expiry Management(P-3)**

#### 11.6.1 Real-time monitoring of:

- 11.6.1.1 Expired stock
- 11.6.1.2 Stock expiring within a defined timeframe (e.g., 30 days)
- 11.6.2 Auto-block expired items from being issued or used
- 11.6.3 FIFO or FEFO (First Expiry First Out) issuance policy

### **11.7 Instrument-Reagent Mapping(P-3)**

#### 11.7.1 Map reagent kits/batches to specific instruments or test types

- 11.7.2 Log reagent changes on instruments with:

- 11.7.2.1 Date/time of installation

- 11.7.2.2 Lot number

- 11.7.2.3 Technician name

- 11.7.3 Ensure test results are traceable to the reagent and lot used

## **11.8 Reagent Quality Validation Logs(P-3)**

- 11.8.1 Capture:

- 11.8.1.1 Internal QC validation of new lots

- 11.8.1.2 Performance review on initial use

- 11.8.1.3 Rejection reasons for faulty/unstable lots

## **11.9 Issue & Return Management(P-3)**

- 11.9.1 Support issuing stock:

- 11.9.1.1 By department, bench, or user

- 11.9.1.2 With optional approval flow

- 11.9.2 Log partial or full return of unused/defective items

- 11.9.3 Allow for **inter-departmental transfers**

## **11.10 Inventory Audit & Stock Reconciliation(P-3)**

- 11.10.1Physical stock reconciliation module

- 11.10.2Cycle count or full audit tracking

- 11.10.3Discrepancy log:

- 11.10.3.1 Overages/shortages

- 11.10.3.2 Damaged/expired/lost items

- 11.10.4Supports traceability and readiness for external audits.

## **11.11 Indents, Purchase Requests & Vendor Linkage(P-3)**

- 11.11.1.1 Create indents directly from lab dashboard

- 11.11.1.2 Auto-trigger indent when stock below minimum

- 11.11.1.3 Track status of indents, purchase orders, GRNs

- 11.11.1.4 Maintain supplier database with performance ratings

## **11.12 Waste & Disposal Logging(P-3)**

- 11.12.1Log disposal of:

- 11.12.1.1 Expired reagents

- 11.12.1.2 Spillage/damaged items

- 11.12.1.3 Used hazardous lab consumables (e.g., culture media, blood tubes)

- 11.12.2Track disposal method and responsible staff

- 11.12.3Comply with biomedical waste management guidelines

## **11.13 Reports & Dashboards(P-3)**

- 11.13.1Daily/weekly/monthly usage reports

- 11.13.2Expiry forecast reports

- 11.13.3Lot usage history per test/instrument

- 11.13.4Cost tracking by department or reagent

- 11.13.5Audit readiness logs

## 12 Device Integration(P-2)

### 12.1 Supported Device Interface Protocols

- 12.1.1 Must support standard communication protocols for lab instruments:
  - 12.1.1.1 **HL7 (Health Level 7)**
  - 12.1.1.2 **ASTM (American Society for Testing and Materials)**
  - 12.1.1.3 **RS-232 Serial Communication**
  - 12.1.1.4 **TCP/IP** and USB connections
- 12.1.2 Compatibility with middleware like:
  - 12.1.2.1 Data Innovations, Orchard, Roche Cobas IT, Siemens CentralLink
- 12.1.3 Ensures wide compatibility with legacy and modern instruments.

### 12.2 Bidirectional Communication(P-2)

- 12.2.1 **Bidirectional Integration** for applicable analyzers:
  - 12.2.1.1 HMIS sends worklist (sample ID, test codes) to instrument
  - 12.2.1.2 Instrument returns test results to HMIS
- 12.2.2 For unidirectional instruments, at least support:
  - 12.2.2.1 Automated result pulling into LIS

### 12.3 Work list Management(P-2)

- 12.3.1 Auto-generate **sample work lists** based on pending tests:
  - 12.3.1.1 By instrument type
  - 12.3.1.2 By department or shift
- 12.3.2 Send work list to instrument or middleware system
- 12.3.3 Allow real-time **work list tracking** (status, errors, completion)

### 12.4 Barcode-Based Sample Matching(P-2)

- 12.4.1 Each sample must be barcoded and scanned into the instrument
- 12.4.2 System should **validate**:
  - 12.4.2.1 Sample ID
  - 12.4.2.2 Test mapping
  - 12.4.2.3 Department/rack/position (if applicable)
- 12.4.3 Alerts for mismatched or unrecognized barcodes

### 12.5 Automated Result Retrieval(P-2)

- 12.5.1 System must automatically:
  - Pull results from instrument screen or directly validate (based on rules)
- 12.5.2 Support for:
  - 12.5.2.1 Numeric results
  - 12.5.2.2 Flags (e.g., High, Low, Critical)
  - 12.5.2.3 Qualitative values (e.g., Positive/Negative)

### 12.6 Device-Specific Configuration(P-2)

- 12.6.1 Allow device-specific configuration for:

- 12.6.1.1 Test code mapping (instrument code ↔ HMIS code)

- 12.6.1.2 Unit conversion

- 12.6.1.3 Decimal precision

- 12.6.1.4 Reference ranges per test/device

- 12.6.2 Ensures data standardization across platforms.

## **12.7 Real-Time Status Dashboard(P-2)**

- 12.7.1 Show live connection status for each device

- 12.7.2 Display:

- 12.7.2.1 Last data received

- 12.7.2.2 Error logs (e.g., timeout, parse failure)

- 12.7.2.3 Queue size or pending uploads

- 12.7.3 Color-coded status: Online / Offline / Warning / Error

## **12.8 Error & Exception Handling(P-2)**

- 12.8.1 Capture and report:

- 12.8.1.1 Transmission errors

- 12.8.1.2 Sample ID mismatches

- 12.8.1.3 Incomplete results

- 12.8.2 Allow reprocessing or re-uploading of affected results

## **12.9 Result Validation Workflow(P-2)**

- 12.9.1 Auto-mark **normal results** for quick validation

- 12.9.2 Route **abnormal/flagged results** for technician/pathologist review

- 12.9.3 Link results to **QC status** of the device before allowing finalization

## **12.10 Quality Control Integration(P-3)**

- 12.10.1 Capture QC run results from analyzers (if supported)

- 12.10.2 Map QC data to:

- 12.10.2.1 Lot numbers

- 12.10.2.2 Time slots/shifts

- 12.10.2.3 Operator

- 12.10.3 Block result import if QC fails (based on configuration)

## **12.11 Device Utilization & Performance Reports(P-3)**

- 12.11.1 Daily/weekly/monthly reports:

- 12.11.1.1 Number of samples processed per device

- 12.11.1.2 Uptime vs downtime

- 12.11.1.3 Error rate per instrument

- 12.11.2 Helps in capacity planning, maintenance, and procurement

## **12.12 Support for Specialized Devices(P-3)**

- 12.12.1 Enable integrations with:

- 12.12.1.1 **Microbiology systems** (e.g., VITEK, BACTEC)
- 12.12.1.2 **Molecular diagnostics** (e.g., RT-PCR machines)
- 12.12.1.3 **Hematology slide scanners**
- 12.12.1.4 **Coagulation analyzers**

12.12.2 Allow for manual attachment of image files (e.g., digital pathology)

## 13 Result Delivery & Notification

### 13.1 Delivery Workflow Triggers(P-2)

13.1.1 Result delivery should begin **only after final validation** by authorized personnel.

13.1.2 Triggers include:

- 13.1.2.1 Manual release (lab user)
- 13.1.2.2 Auto-release after validation
- 13.1.2.3 Scheduled batch delivery (e.g., at midnight)

13.1.3 Ensures that only verified results are communicated.

### 13.2 Delivery Channels(P-2)

13.2.1 To Clinicians

- 13.2.1.1 Integrated with EMR to show reports directly on the **doctor's dashboard**
- 13.2.1.2 Enable **filtering by patient, date, test, or urgency**

13.2.2 To Patients

- 13.2.2.1 Via **patient portal / mobile app** (secured with login/OTP)
- 13.2.2.2 Optional delivery via:
  - 13.2.2.2.1 SMS with download link
  - 13.2.2.2.2 Email with PDF report (password protected)
- 13.2.2.3 Support for multilingual delivery

13.2.3 To Hospital Departments

- 13.2.3.1 Ward-wise or unit-wise result bundles for inpatients
- 13.2.3.2 Integration with Nursing Station dashboards
- 13.2.3.3 PDF push to PACS/ICU consoles (if configured)

### 13.3 Critical Result Alerts(P-2)

13.3.1 Define critical result thresholds per test

13.3.2 Auto-alert the treating physician or nurse via:

- 13.3.2.1 SMS
- 13.3.2.2 Mobile push notification
- 13.3.2.3 Popup in EMR
- 13.3.3 Escalation rules (e.g., if not acknowledged in X mins → escalate to consultant)
- 13.3.4 Ensures urgent action on life-threatening lab findings.

### 13.4 Notification Settings & Customization(P-2)

13.4.1 Allow customization by role or user:

- 13.4.1.1 Preferred delivery channel (email/SMS/portal)

- 13.4.1.2 Notification frequency (instant, hourly, end-of-day)

- 13.4.1.3 Test-specific alert subscriptions (e.g., notify for COVID, Troponin I)

### **13.5 Report Format & Accessibility(P-2)**

- 13.5.1 Deliver reports in standardized, user-friendly PDF format

- 13.5.2 Include:

- 13.5.2.1 Hospital logo and header

- 13.5.2.2 Barcode/QR code for online verification

- 13.5.2.3 Test interpretation and comments

- 13.5.3 Mobile-responsive viewing

- 13.5.4 Auto-generate **patient summary documents** if multiple results delivered together

### **13.6 Security & Privacy(P-2)**

- 13.6.1 Role-based access to results

- 13.6.2 Password protection for emailed reports

- 13.6.3 Encrypt results during transit (especially over email or public links)

- 13.6.4 Consent management for patient access (especially sensitive tests)

### **13.7 Delivery Status Tracking & Audit(P-2)**

- 13.7.1 Track delivery status of each result:

- 13.7.1.1 Delivered / Viewed / Acknowledged / Failed

- 13.7.2 Show timestamps of:

- 13.7.2.1 Notification sent

- 13.7.2.2 Report accessed

- 13.7.2.3 Acknowledgment (if required)

- 13.7.3 Complete audit trail per result and user

### **13.8 Notification Dashboard(P-3)**

- 13.8.1 For clinicians: pending and unread results list

- 13.8.2 For lab managers: delivery success/failure rate

- 13.8.3 Filter by:

- 13.8.3.1 Patient/test

- 13.8.3.2 Time range

- 13.8.3.3 Department or recipient type

### **13.9 Delayed or Failed Notification Handling(P-3)**

- 13.9.1 Retry mechanism for:

- 13.9.1.1 Failed SMS/email delivery

- 13.9.1.2 Incomplete report generation

- 13.9.2 Alert lab admin if delivery fails repeatedly

- 13.9.3 Offline backup: print results for manual dispatch if needed

### **13.10 Bulk Result Dispatch(P-3)**

- 13.10.1 Scheduled batch result sending:

- 13.10.1.1 Daily inpatient round files
- 13.10.1.2 Health check-up group results
- 13.10.1.3 Screening camp result dispatch

- 13.10.2 Bulk print/email/download options

### 13.11 Multi-Platform Access(P-3)

- 13.11.1 Web portal, mobile app, email link – all synchronized

- 13.11.2 Results visible on:

- 13.11.2.1 Desktop (EMR/HMIS)
- 13.11.2.2 Mobile app (iOS/Android)
- 13.11.2.3 Tab-based ward consoles (optional)

## 14 Integration Requirements (P-2)

### 14.1 Laboratory Information System (LIS) Integration

- 14.1.1 Ensure seamless exchange of data between the **HMIS** and **LIS** for:

- 14.1.1.1 Patient demographics
- 14.1.1.2 Test orders (auto or manual)
- 14.1.1.3 Test results
- 14.1.1.4 Work lists
- 14.1.1.5 QC data

- 14.1.2 Support standard protocols for LIS communication:

- 14.1.2.1 **HL7 (Health Level 7)**: For test orders, results, and patient demographics
- 14.1.2.2 **ASTM (American Society for Testing and Materials)**: For result communication
- 14.1.2.3 **XML/JSON**: For web-based integrations and API-based communication

- 14.1.3 Support bidirectional data flow:

- 14.1.3.1 Sending test orders from HMIS to LIS
- 14.1.3.2 Receiving test results from LIS to HMIS

- 14.1.4 Facilitates data accuracy and reduces manual data entry.

### 14.2 Electronic Medical Record (EMR/EHR) Integration(P-2)

- 14.2.1 Integrate with **EMR/EHR** systems to ensure that:

- 14.2.1.1 **Test orders and results** are automatically displayed in the clinician's dashboard
- 14.2.1.2 **Patient reports** are accessible in the patient's health record

- 14.2.2 Support automatic synchronization for:

- 14.2.2.1 Patient demographics (name, ID, age, etc.)
- 14.2.2.2 Test requests (order ID, test code, status)
- 14.2.2.3 Results display (numeric values, flagged results, images)

- 14.2.3 Ensures that clinicians have real-time access to patient lab results in their workflow.

### 14.3 Patient Portal / Mobile App Integration(P-2)

- 14.3.1 Enable patient result access via:

- 14.3.1.1 **Patient portal** (website or app)

- 14.3.1.2 **SMS/Email alerts** with secure links to the report

- 14.3.2 Allow integration for:

- 14.3.2.1 **Patient-specific notifications** (test result available)

- 14.3.2.2 **PDF generation** for results accessible through the portal or app

- 14.3.2.3 **Secure login/OTP** for patient authentication

- 14.3.3 Facilitates patient engagement and access to lab results in real-time.

#### **14.4 Inventory Management System (IMS) Integration(P-3)**

- 14.4.1 Integrate with **Inventory Management System (IMS)** to track and manage:

- 14.4.1.1 **Reagents, consumables, and instruments** used in the laboratory

- 14.4.1.2 **Lot number, expiry dates, and stock levels**

- 14.4.1.3 **Automatic reorder alerts** when stock falls below minimum levels

- 14.4.1.4 Integration with **QC/Calibration data** to track reagent and consumable lot performance

- 14.4.2 Optimizes reagent usage and ensures that lab testing is performed with valid materials.

#### **14.5 Medical Devices & Instrumentation Integration(P-2)**

- 14.5.1 Integrate with laboratory **instruments and analyzers** for:

- 14.5.1.1 **Automatic test order** transmission to instruments (e.g., via HL7 or ASTM)

- 14.5.1.2 **Result Import** from devices back to the HMIS

- 14.5.1.3 **Device status monitoring** (e.g., uptime, calibration status)

- 14.5.2 Supports integration with diverse diagnostic devices like:

- 14.5.2.1 **Hematology analyzers**

- 14.5.2.2 **Biochemistry analyzers**

- 14.5.2.3 **Microbiology instruments**

- 14.5.2.4 **Immunoassay analyzers**

- 14.5.2.5 **Molecular diagnostic systems** (e.g., PCR)

- 14.5.3 Ensures that results are captured automatically, reducing manual entry errors.

#### **14.6 Billing & Costing Integration(P-2)**

- 14.6.1 Link laboratory test results with the Billing system for:

- 14.6.1.1 Accurate **patient billing** (test cost calculation based on ordered panels)

- 14.6.1.2 **Insurance claim processing** (test orders as part of the claim)

- 14.6.1.3 **Itemized invoices** that include test codes, results, and pricing

- 14.6.2 Track **test usage costs** for financial planning and auditing

- 14.6.3 Improves financial tracking and ensures accurate invoicing.

#### **14.7 Pharmacy Integration(P-3)**

- 14.7.1 Ensure that laboratory tests, especially **drug levels or therapeutic monitoring tests**, are automatically:

14.7.1.1 Linked to **patient prescriptions**

14.7.1.2 Flagged for **drug interactions or side effect monitoring**

14.7.2 Send test orders directly from HMIS to Pharmacy for **medication adjustments** based on test results (e.g., renal function tests before prescribing certain medications)

14.7.3 Ensures better patient safety and optimal medication use.

#### **14.8 Radiology Integration (PACS)**

14.8.1 For tests requiring imaging (e.g. Histopathology, **Microbiology cultures, Pathology slides**):

14.8.1.1 Integrate with **PACS** (Picture Archiving and Communication System) for seamless access to:

14.8.1.1.1 **Images** associated with lab results

14.8.1.1.2 **Reports and findings** linked to test results

14.8.2 Ensure that **lab results and imaging results** are viewable together in the patient's medical record

14.8.3 Provides comprehensive patient diagnostic data in one place.

#### **14.9 Hospital Information System (HIS) Integration(P-1)**

14.9.1 Integrate with the **Hospital Information System (HIS)** for:

14.9.1.1 Patient registration and admission details

14.9.1.2 Orders for lab tests (both inpatient and outpatient)

14.9.1.3 Discharge summaries, including lab test results

14.9.2 Ensure that test orders and results are automatically transferred to the HIS for billing, reporting, and patient discharge

14.9.3 Improves workflow across the hospital and reduces manual data entry.

#### **14.10 Quality Assurance (QA) & Compliance Integration(P-3)**

14.10.1 Integrate with **QA and Compliance** systems to monitor:

14.10.1.1 **Internal audits** (e.g., QC, calibration)

14.10.1.2 **Accreditation management** (e.g., NABL, CAP compliance)

14.10.1.3 **Regulatory reporting** (e.g., CLIA, ISO 15189)

14.10.2 Share **calibration, validation, and quality control** data automatically from devices or middleware systems

14.10.3 Ensures regulatory compliance and continuous quality improvement.

#### **14.11 External System Integration (EQA / PT) (P-3)**

14.11.1 Integrate with External Quality Assessment (EQA) or Proficiency Testing (PT) systems for:

14.11.1.1 Reporting of proficiency test results

14.11.1.2 Automatic comparison of internal lab results with external benchmark results

14.11.2 Ensure automated **submission of test data** for external audits and certification

14.11.3 Supports accreditation and enhances lab performance benchmarks.

#### **14.12 Mobile App & Remote Access Integration(P-2)**

14.12.1 Allow **remote access** to lab test results via a **hospital mobile app**:

- 14.12.1.1 Test results
  - 14.12.1.2 Status updates (e.g., pending, completed)
  - 14.12.1.3 Alerts for critical results
- 14.12.2 Ensure that **technicians and lab managers** can remotely monitor instrument status, stock levels, and QC compliance via mobile apps
- 14.12.3 Facilitates better decision-making and real-time monitoring.

## 15 Infrastructure and Civic Amenities Maintenance – P1

- 15.1.1 Documentation of the dedicated and separate areas for administrative and technical areas
- 15.1.2 Documentation of the adequate facilities and well maintained civic amenities for the comfort of the patients and attendants
- 15.1.3 Measures to limit access to exposure areas of the clinical laboratory department
- 15.1.4 Separate change rooms for males and females
- 15.1.5 Clean toilets / washrooms with bolts, preferably separate for males and females
- 15.1.6 Facilitated toilets for disabled patients are available
- 15.1.7 Disabled patients are facilitated for the diagnostic tests
- 15.1.8 Directional arrows pointing towards various important areas for patients are displayed
- 15.1.9 **Dedicated Area for Stretchers and Wheelchairs:**
  - 15.1.9.1 **Area Size:** [Enter square footage of dedicated area]
  - 15.1.9.2 **Accessibility:** [Yes/No – Is the area easily accessible for staff and patients?]
- 15.1.10 **Functional Wheelchairs Availability:**
  - 15.1.10.1 **Total Number of Wheelchairs:** [Enter number of available wheelchairs]
  - 15.1.10.2 **Wheelchairs In Use:** [Number of currently used wheelchairs]
  - 15.1.10.3 **Available Wheelchairs:** [Calculated number of available wheelchairs]
  - 15.1.10.4 **Maintenance Status:** [All functional/Under maintenance]
- 15.1.11 **Functional Stretchers Availability:**
  - 15.1.11.1 **Total Number of Stretchers:** [Enter number of available stretchers]
  - 15.1.11.2 **Stretchers In Use:** [Number of currently used stretchers]
  - 15.1.11.3 **Available Stretchers:** [Calculated number of available stretchers]
  - 15.1.11.4 **Maintenance Status:** [All functional/Under maintenance]
- 15.1.12 **Building Maintenance and Safety:**

- 15.1.12.1 Manage the **maintenance** of clinical department, including **HVAC systems** (heating, ventilation, air conditioning), **plumbing, electrical systems, and lighting**.
- 15.1.12.2 Track **preventive maintenance schedules** to ensure regular inspections and servicing of critical infrastructure like **elevators, water supply systems, and emergency power systems**.
- 15.1.12.3 Record and **manage facility repair requests**, including emergency repairs and routine maintenance tasks.

## **16 Cleanliness and Hygiene – P1**

### **16.1 Electric Wires Sealed:**

- 16.1.1 **Status:** [All sealed/Partial sealing required]
- 16.1.2 **Inspection Date:** [Last inspected date]
- 16.1.3 **Next Inspection:** [Next scheduled inspection]

### **16.2 Anti-Fly/Mosquito/Rodent Measures:**

- 16.2.1 **Measures In Place:** [Pest control schedule or actions]
- 16.2.2 **Last Inspection:** [Date of last inspection]
- 16.2.3 **Next Inspection:** [Date of next inspection]

### **16.3 Availability of Clean Linen:**

- 16.3.1 **Total Clean Linen Available:** [Enter number of linens available]
- 16.3.2 **Clean Linen In Use:** [Number of linens in use]
- 16.3.3 **Available Linen:** [Number of linens available]

### **16.4 Cleanliness of Radiology Rooms:**

- 16.4.1 **Room 1:** [Clean/Needs Attention]
- 16.4.2 **Room 2:** [Clean/Needs Attention]
- 16.4.3 **Room Details:** [Link to ward/room cleanliness records]

### **16.5 Cleanliness of Corridors and Toilets:**

- 16.5.1 **Corridors Clean:** [Yes/No, Date of Last Cleaning]
- 16.5.2 **Toilets Clean:** [Yes/No, Date of Last Cleaning]

## **17 Power and Equipment**

### **17.1 Power and Electricity Supply: - P1**

- 17.1.1 Track and manage the **electricity supply**, including **backup generators, and UPS (Uninterruptible Power Supply)**.
- 17.1.2 Maintain records of **power outages** and monitor the functioning of **emergency generators**.
- 17.1.3 **Energy management:** Integrate systems for managing the **efficient use of energy** across the hospital (e.g., **solar power, LED lighting, smart energy meters**).

### **17.2 Backup Power Supply Checking: - P1**

- 17.2.1 **Status:** [Available/Not Available]
- 17.2.2 **Last Test Date:** [Date of last backup power test]
- 17.2.3 **Next Test Date:** [Scheduled date for next test]

### **17.3 CCTV Camera Installation: - P1**

- 17.3.1 **Total Number of Cameras:** [Enter number of CCTV cameras installed in ED]

17.3.2 **Cameras In Operation:** [Enter number of operational cameras]

17.3.3 **Live Monitoring:** [Link to real-time CCTV monitoring]

## 17.4 Security and Access Control

17.4.1 **Security Systems:** - P2

17.4.1.1 Monitor **security systems**, including **CCTV surveillance, access control, and visitor management systems**.

17.4.1.2 Integrate with **emergency security protocols** such as **lockdown procedures, patient abduction prevention, and staff safety**.

17.4.2 **Access Control and Role-Based Permissions:** - P2

17.4.2.1 Ensure **restricted access** to sensitive areas.

17.4.2.2 Monitor **staff access** based on **role-based permissions** to ensure the right personnel access the right areas (e.g., only **doctors, nurses, and authorized staff** should access patient care areas).

## 18 SOPs / Protocols and Guidelines Repository

### 18.1 Digitized Repository of SOPs / Protocols / Guidelines / Laws & Regulation

18.1.1 SOPs and Protocols Repository: - P1

18.1.1.1 **SOP/Protocol Name:** [List of all available SOPs and protocols]

18.1.1.2 **Document Type:** [Link to PDF/Word document for each SOP]

18.1.1.3 **Date Last Updated:** [MM/DD/YYYY]

18.1.1.4 **Version History:** [Link to historical versions of the SOPs/Protocols]

18.1.1.5 **Search & Filter Options:**

18.1.1.5.1 **Search Bar:** [Allow staff to search for specific SOPs, procedures, or diseases]

18.1.1.5.2 **Filter by Role:** [Filter guidelines by role (e.g., Doctors, Nurses, Support Staff)]

18.1.1.5.3 **Filter by Protocol Type:** [Filter by category like Trauma, Cardiology, Infectious Disease, etc.]

18.1.1.5.4 **Filter by Status:** [Active/Archived]

18.1.2 Documentary evidence of implementation of SOPs / Protocols / Guidelines / Laws & Regulations – P1

### 18.2 Interactive, Searchable Database with Role-Specific Guidelines

18.2.1 Interactive Guidelines Database: - P2

18.2.1.1 **Searchable Database:**

18.2.1.1.1 **Search Criteria:** [Allow users to search for guidelines by key terms, disease, procedure, or staff role]

18.2.1.1.2 **Role-Specific Guidelines:**

- **Doctors:** [Link to guidelines relevant to medical staff, e.g., advanced trauma life support, resuscitation protocols]
- **Nurses:** [Link to nursing guidelines, e.g., infection control protocols, patient monitoring procedures]
- **Support Staff:** [Link to housekeeping, maintenance, and transport protocols]
- **Clinical Guidelines by Procedure:** [List of protocols for procedures like intubation, blood transfusion, etc.]

18.2.2 **Guideline Categories:** - P1

18.2.3 **Real-Time Updates:** - P1

18.2.3.1 **Latest Changes:** [Display recent updates and modifications to any guideline]

18.2.3.2 **Change Log:** [Link to a log detailing who made the changes and the reason for the update]

## 18.3 Training and Education

18.3.1 **E-Learning Modules:** - P2

18.3.1.1 **Training Course Title:**

18.3.1.2 **Course Description:** [Brief overview of the course]

18.3.1.3 **Module Content:**

18.3.1.3.1 **Videos:** [Training videos covering various emergency care protocols, donning/doffing PPE, etc.]

18.3.1.3.2 **Interactive Quizzes:** [Quizzes to assess understanding of the protocol content]

18.3.1.3.3 **Case Studies:** [Real-life case study simulations for application of protocols]

18.3.1.4 **Staff Progress Tracking:**

18.3.1.4.1 **Completion Status:** [Track who has completed the training module]

18.3.1.4.2 **Passing Criteria:** [Display minimum score to pass e-learning courses]

18.3.1.4.3 **Progress Tracking:** [Allow staff to see their own training progress]

18.3.1.4.4 **Certificates:** [Automated certification upon completion of a training module]

18.3.1.5 **Training Module Library:**

18.3.1.5.1 **Available Courses:** [List of available e-learning modules]

18.3.1.5.2 **Module Duration:** [Time required to complete each module]

18.3.1.5.3 **Review Status:** [Indicates when the module was last reviewed and updated]

## 19 Dashboard (P-2)

### 19.1 Standard Reports

The HMIS should include a variety of **predefined reports** for both laboratory and hospital management. These reports must cover a range of performance, compliance, and operational aspects:

19.1.1 Test Results Reports

19.1.1.1 **Test result summary reports** for patients, with options to filter by:

19.1.1.1.1 Patient name, ID, department, or time range

19.1.1.1.2 Test type (e.g., blood tests, microbiology, histology)

19.1.1.1.3 Test status (pending, completed, critical)

19.1.1.2 **Batch results** for groups of patients (e.g., health check-up or screening results)

19.1.2 Turnaround Time (TAT) Reports

19.1.2.1 Report the time taken from sample collection to result reporting.

19.1.2.2 Provide **department-level breakdown and individual test performance**.

19.1.2.3 Identify bottlenecks in workflow or device malfunction that cause delays.

19.1.3 Test Volume Reports

19.1.3.1 Summarize the number of tests conducted by:

19.1.3.1.1 Type of test (e.g., hematology, biochemistry)

19.1.3.1.2 Instrument or analyzer

19.1.3.1.3 Department or lab section

19.1.3.2 Track trends over time (daily, weekly, monthly, and quarterly).

19.1.4 Quality Control (QC) and Calibration Reports

19.1.4.1 Detailed analysis of **QC results** to ensure compliance with set thresholds.

19.1.4.2 Show **calibration checks** for devices, ensuring accurate results.

19.1.4.3 Provide **trending of QC data** for error detection.

19.1.5 Inventory Reports

19.1.5.1 Track reagent usage, expiry dates, and stock levels.

19.1.5.2 Highlight **low stock alerts and reorder status**.

19.1.5.3 Report **reagent consumption trends** per test or department.

19.1.6 Financial & Billing Reports

19.1.6.1 **Cost per test** analysis and **billable amount** calculations.

19.1.6.2 **Reimbursement status** from insurance or patients.

19.1.6.3 **Revenue generation** from different lab tests and panels.

19.1.7 Compliance & Accreditation Reports

19.1.7.1 Track adherence to regulatory standards (e.g., **CLIA, ISO 15189, CAP**).

19.1.7.2 Include audit trails for internal or external accreditation.

## 19.2 Customizable Reports

19.2.1 **User-defined filters** to customize the content, format, and layout of reports.

19.2.1.1 Examples: test categories, patient demographics, clinician-specific data

19.2.2 Ability to save custom report templates for frequent use.

19.2.3 Allows lab managers or clinicians to generate reports based on specific needs.

### **19.3 Data Visualization & Dashboards(P-2)**

19.3.1 **Interactive dashboards** for real-time data visualization:

19.3.1.1 Show key metrics, such as test volume trends, turnaround times, and critical results alerts.

19.3.1.1.1 Display device performance and system utilization metrics.

19.3.1.1.2 Provide a visual summary of lab performance indicators (KPIs) such as:

- Test result turnarounds
- Daily sample volume
- Error rates
- Critical result frequencies

19.3.2 Dashboards should include:

19.3.2.1 **Bar charts**

19.3.2.2 **Line graphs**

19.3.2.3 **Pie charts**

19.3.2.4 **Heat maps**

19.3.3 Supports **real-time decision-making** and gives lab managers a quick overview of operations.

### **19.4 Advanced Analytics(P-3)**

19.4.1 Leverage **data mining and statistical methods** to uncover insights from lab data.

19.4.2 Enable the following **advanced analytics** capabilities:

19.4.2.1 **Predictive analytics** for identifying potential test failures or quality issues (e.g., instrument downtime prediction).

19.4.2.2 **Trend analysis** for test results (e.g., lab performance over time, patient outcomes).

19.4.2.3 **Benchmarking** against industry standards or historical performance data.

19.4.2.4 **Outlier detection** for abnormal or erroneous test results.

19.4.2.5 **Patient outcome analytics** based on lab results (e.g., correlations between lab data and treatment outcomes).

19.4.3 Helps improve quality assurance, reduce costs, and optimize laboratory performance.

### **19.5 Real-time Alerts & Notifications(P-2)**

19.5.1 Generate **real-time alerts** based on specific thresholds or triggers:

- 19.5.1.1 **Critical results** for immediate clinician action.

- 19.5.1.2 **Test result delays** exceeding expected turnaround times.

- 19.5.1.3 **Device malfunctions or QC failures.**

- 19.5.2 **Push notifications** to appropriate stakeholders (e.g., lab technician, pathologist, doctor, nurse).

- 19.5.3 Ensures that users are notified instantly of any critical issues or deviations.

## **19.6 Automated Report Generation & Scheduling(P-2)**

- 19.6.1 Enable **automated report generation** at predefined times (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly).

- 19.6.2 Schedule regular reports:

- 19.6.2.1 E.g., daily test volume report for the lab manager

- 19.6.2.2 Weekly TAT analysis for operational efficiency review

- 19.6.2.3 Monthly inventory and reagent consumption reports for procurement planning

- 19.6.3 Automate the **delivery of reports** to the relevant users, such as:

- 19.6.3.1 Lab supervisors

- 19.6.3.2 Clinicians

- 19.6.3.3 Hospital administrators

- 19.6.4 Saves time by reducing manual report generation and ensures timely delivery.

## **19.7 Audit Trail & Data Integrity(P-2)**

- 19.7.1 Track and log all changes to reports, including:

- 19.7.1.1 Report creation, modification, and deletion.

- 19.7.1.2 **User access logs** for report viewing and editing.

- 19.7.1.3 Maintain a secure **audit trail** to meet regulatory requirements.

- 19.7.2 Ensure **data integrity** by validating that report data is accurate and up-to-date.

- 19.7.3 Essential for compliance with regulatory and accreditation bodies.

## **19.8 User Role-based Access to Reports(P-2)**

- 19.8.1 Define access levels based on **user roles**:

- 19.8.1.1 **Lab Technicians:** View test volumes, QC data, and basic test results.

- 19.8.1.2 **Pathologists:** Access detailed test reports, patient trends, and analytics.

- 19.8.1.3 **Lab Managers:** View performance reports, inventory, and financial data.

- 19.8.1.4 **Admin / IT:** Configure system settings and manage report generation.

- 19.8.1.5 **Clinicians:** Access patient-specific test results and analytics related to their patients.

- 19.8.2 Ensures that sensitive information is only accessible to authorized users, improving security and compliance.

## **19.9 Mobile Access to Reports(P-2)**

- 19.9.1 Provide **mobile access** to reports for:

19.9.1.1 Lab managers and clinicians to access data from anywhere

19.9.1.2 Mobile dashboards to track performance in real-time

19.9.1.3 Mobile notifications for urgent alerts (e.g., critical result)

19.9.2 Ensures flexibility and responsiveness in decision-making, especially for on-the-go hospital staff.

## **19.10 Export & Sharing Capabilities(P-2)**

19.10.1 Allow users to **export reports** in various formats:

19.10.1.1 PDF, Excel, CSV, Word, etc.

19.10.2 Enable users to share reports via:

19.10.2.1 **Email** (with encrypted attachment)

19.10.2.2 **Secure download links**

19.10.2.3 **Shared network drives or cloud storage** (with appropriate security)

19.10.3 Facilitates easy sharing of reports among stakeholders or external auditors.

19.10.4 Quality Indicators for Laboratory Excellence

## **20 Pre-Analytical Quality Indicators**

*(Sample Collection, Handling, and Processing Errors)*

| <b>Indicator</b>                        | <b>Measurement</b>                                     |
|-----------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| <b>Incorrect/mislabeled samples</b>     | % of rejected samples due to labeling errors           |
| <b>Sample hemolysis/lipemia/icterus</b> | % of unsuitable samples due to poor quality            |
| <b>Wrong container/anticoagulant</b>    | % of samples rejected for improper collection          |
| <b>Insufficient sample volume</b>       | % of tests canceled due to inadequate quantity         |
| <b>Order entry errors</b>               | % of test requests with incorrect patient/test details |
| <b>Test request form errors</b>         | Missing/incomplete requisition forms                   |

## 21 Analytical Quality Indicators

*(Testing Accuracy, Instrument Performance, and QC Compliance)*

| <b>Indicator</b>                            | <b>Measurement</b>                          |
|---------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------|
| <b>QC failures</b>                          | % of runs outside acceptable control limits |
| <b>Calibration failures</b>                 | Frequency of recalibration needed           |
| <b>Instrument downtime</b>                  | Hours of unexpected analyzer outages        |
| <b>Proficiency testing (PT) performance</b> | % of PT failures (CLIA-required)            |
| <b>Turnaround time (TAT) compliance</b>     | % of tests reported within defined TAT      |
| <b>Critical value reporting delays</b>      | Time from result to clinician notification  |

## 22 Post-Analytical Quality Indicators

*(Reporting, Interpretation, and Follow-up Errors)*

| <b>Indicator</b>                     | <b>Measurement</b>                                              |
|--------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
| <b>Critical value reporting rate</b> | % of critical results communicated within required time         |
| <b>Report transcription errors</b>   | % of reports with manual entry mistakes                         |
| <b>Delta check failures</b>          | % of results flagged for significant variation from prior tests |
| <b>Amended/corrected reports</b>     | Frequency of report modifications post-issuance                 |

| <b>Indicator</b>                           | <b>Measurement</b>                          |
|--------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------|
| <b>Physician satisfaction with reports</b> | Survey-based feedback on clarity/timeliness |

## **23 Operational & Safety Indicators**

*(Lab Efficiency, Compliance, and Risk Management)*

| <b>Indicator</b>                                   | <b>Measurement</b>                       |
|----------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------|
| <b>Incident reports (near-misses, errors)</b>      | Number of documented lab incidents       |
| <b>Specimen storage compliance</b>                 | Adherence to retention policies          |
| <b>Employee competency assessments</b>             | % of staff meeting training requirements |
| <b>Biosafety &amp; chemical exposure incidents</b> | Number of safety violations              |
| <b>Test utilization review</b>                     | % of unnecessary/redundant tests ordered |

## Radiology Department Module

![A radiologist reviewing CT scan images on a computer monitor in a radiology department, with a CT scanner and a patient in the background.](09e20531558819de932d1e53f83f7bc0_img.jpg)

A photograph of a radiology department. In the foreground, a radiologist with dark hair tied back, wearing a white lab coat and a purple apron, is seated at a workstation. They are looking at a computer monitor displaying a transverse CT scan of a human torso. To the left of the main monitor is a smaller screen showing a grid of multiple scan images. On the desk in front of the radiologist are a keyboard, a mouse, and a calendar. In the background, a large white CT scanner is visible. A patient is lying on a table inside the machine's gantry. A man in a brown jacket and a face mask stands nearby, observing the procedure. The wall behind the scanner is light green, and the overall lighting is bright and clinical.
