## [RADIOLOGY MODULE]

### 1 Patient Information Management

#### 1.1 Demographic Data Integration

- 1.1.1 Automatic population of patient details from central HMIS: - P1
  - 1.1.1.1 Unique Hospital ID (UHID)
  - 1.1.1.2 Name, gender, age/DOB
  - 1.1.1.3 Contact information (phone, email, address)
  - 1.1.1.4 CNIC #
  - 1.1.1.5 Insurance details/payer info
  - 1.1.1.6 Guardian/Next of kin details (for pediatrics or dependent patients)

#### 1.2 Visit/Encounter Data

- 1.2.1 Retrieve and associate current visit/encounter information: - P1
  - 1.2.1.1 Inpatient (IPD), Outpatient (OPD), or Emergency (ER)
  - 1.2.1.2 Department or unit
  - 1.2.1.3 Referring physician
  - 1.2.1.4 Admission/visit number
  - 1.2.1.5 Admission date/time
  - 1.2.1.6 Bed/ward details (for inpatients)

#### 1.3 Clinical Context

- 1.3.1 Access and attach clinical notes: - P1
  - 1.3.1.1 Presenting complaints
  - 1.3.1.2 Provisional diagnosis
  - 1.3.1.3 Relevant history or previous imaging
  - 1.3.1.4 Lab results or previous findings if relevant
- 1.3.2 Link to active problems list from EMR - P1

#### 1.4 Previous Radiology Records

- 1.4.1 Fetch historical radiology records and images for comparative analysis: - P1
  - 1.4.1.1 Prior studies across all modalities
  - 1.4.1.2 Previous reports (with timestamp, interpreting radiologist)
  - 1.4.1.3 Direct link to associated images via PACS viewer

#### 1.5 Patient Movement Tracking

- 1.5.1 Integration with patient flow system: - P2
  - 1.5.1.1 Auto-update when a patient moves from one department to another
  - 1.5.1.2 Track when a patient is sent to radiology and returns
  - 1.5.1.3 Status like "waiting for imaging", "imaging in progress", "report pending"

#### 1.6 Unique Patient Identifiers & Labeling

- 1.6.1 Barcode/QR code support for: - P1
  - 1.6.1.1 Patient wristbands
  - 1.6.1.2 Radiology requisition forms

- 1.6.1.3 Sample labels or films (if printed)

## **1.7 Consent & Documentation**

- 1.7.1 Situations requiring informed consent are defined - P1
- 1.7.2 Who can give consent when patient is incapable of independent decision-making is also defined - P1
- 1.7.3 Track consent status: - P1
  - 1.7.3.1 Informed consent for imaging procedures (especially contrast studies, MRI)
  - 1.7.3.2 Digital signatures or scanned consent forms
- 1.7.4 Alerts for special considerations: - P1
  - 1.7.4.1 Pregnancy
  - 1.7.4.2 Allergies (e.g., contrast agents)
  - 1.7.4.3 Claustrophobia, implanted devices (for MRI)

## **1.8 Patient Portal Integration**

- 1.8.1 Make basic demographic and radiology appointment information available to the patient: - P2
  - 1.8.1.1 Scheduled tests
  - 1.8.1.2 Prep instructions (e.g., fasting, hydration)
  - 1.8.1.3 Completed tests and report availability
  - 1.8.1.4 Report download/view access (with privacy controls)

# **2 Radiology Order Management**

## **2.1 Order Initiation**

- 2.1.1 Order Entry Points: - P1
  - 2.1.1.1 Physician Order Entry from EMR
  - 2.1.1.2 Inpatient, Outpatient, Emergency Department modules
  - 2.1.1.3 Pre-authorized scheduled procedures from clinics
- 2.1.2 Order Details Must Include: - P1
  - 2.1.2.1 Patient demographics and visit details (auto-populated)
  - 2.1.2.2 Ordering physician and department
  - 2.1.2.3 Clinical indications / Provisional diagnosis
  - 2.1.2.4 Requested modality & study (e.g., Chest X-ray, MRI Brain)
  - 2.1.2.5 Special instructions (e.g., sedation required, contrast use)

## **2.2 Order Validation & Approval Workflow**

- 2.2.1 Checks before submission: - P2
  - 2.2.1.1 Duplicate study alerts
  - 2.2.1.2 Contraindication warnings (e.g., allergy to contrast, pacemaker for MRI)
  - 2.2.1.3 Pregnancy or age-related warnings
- 2.2.2 Approval Mechanisms: - P2
  - 2.2.2.1 Automatic or manual review depending on imaging type
  - 2.2.2.2 Radiologist consultation or second-level approval for high-risk procedures

## **2.3 Order Prioritization & Categorization**

- 2.3.1 Ability to mark orders with priority: - P1
  - 2.3.1.1 Routine, Urgent, STAT Emergency
- 2.3.2 Assign triage protocols for handling bulk emergency orders – P2

## 2.4 Scheduling Integration

- 2.4.1 Send orders directly to scheduling interface – P3
- 2.4.2 Check availability of machines, technicians, and patients – P3
- 2.4.3 Integration with appointment system to assign time slots – P3
- 2.4.4 Flags for pre-scan requirements (e.g., fasting, hydration, lab tests) – P3

## 2.5 Order Tracking & Lifecycle Management

- 2.5.1 Each order should pass through defined statuses:

Ordered → Verified → Scheduled → In Progress → Performed → Reported → Completed/Archived

- 2.5.2 Visual status dashboards for all users – P2
- 2.5.3 Timestamps for each status change – P2
- 2.5.4 Real-time updates for patient tracking and clinical staff – P2

## 2.6 Order Modifications / Cancellations

- 2.6.1 Order editing with reason capturing (by authorized users) – P2
- 2.6.2 Cancellation with mandatory reason selection – P2
- 2.6.3 Full audit trail for every change (who, what, when) – P2

## 2.7 Alerts & Notifications

- 2.7.1 Auto-alerts to: - P2
  - 2.7.1.1 Radiology staff (new orders, STAT cases)
  - 2.7.1.2 Technologists (schedule updates)
  - 2.7.1.3 Referring physician (completed report)
- 2.7.2 SMS/email/portal notifications to patients for scheduled tests – P2

## 2.8 Compliance & Safety Checks

- 2.8.1 Allergy check (e.g., iodine for contrast-enhanced CT) – P2
- 2.8.2 Safety checklist verification (implants, pregnancy, claustrophobia) – P2
- 2.8.3 Adherence to imaging appropriateness criteria – P2

# 3 Radiology Appointment

## 3.1 Appointment Scheduling and Management

- 3.1.1 Patient Appointment Booking: - P1
  - 3.1.1.1 **Radiology appointments** should be easily bookable via various channels (e.g., **online portal, phone calls, walk-in**, or through **referring clinicians**).
  - 3.1.1.2 Patients should be able to select appointment types based on modality (e.g., **MRI, CT Scan, X-ray, Ultrasound**). – P1
  - 3.1.1.3 The system should capture **essential patient details** including: - P1
    - 3.1.1.3.1 **Patient ID, name, age, gender.**
    - 3.1.1.3.2 **Procedure type** (e.g., X-ray, MRI, CT).
    - 3.1.1.3.3 **Preferred time and date** for the procedure.
    - 3.1.1.3.4 **Referring physician details.**

3.1.1.3.5 **Clinical indications** (reason for imaging).

3.1.2 Integrated Calendar View: - P1

3.1.2.1 The system should display a **real-time calendar** showing available and booked slots for each imaging modality and room.

3.1.2.2 Allow staff to schedule appointments and avoid overbooking or double-booking of resources.

3.1.2.3 The calendar should allow for **viewing by modality, equipment, room, or staff availability**.

3.1.3 Automated Scheduling: - P2

3.1.3.1 **Automated scheduling algorithms** can suggest the most appropriate appointment times based on the **modality, patient needs, and availability of resources** (e.g., equipment, radiologists, or imaging rooms).

3.1.3.2 Allow for **adjustments to schedules** in real time to accommodate urgent or emergency cases.

3.1.4 Patient Appointment Confirmation: - P1

3.1.4.1 Once the appointment is scheduled, an automated confirmation (via email, SMS, or app notification) should be sent to the patient with:

3.1.4.1.1 Appointment **date, time, and location**.

3.1.4.1.2 **Preparation instructions** (e.g., fasting, contrast agent administration).

3.1.4.1.3 **Contact details** for rescheduling or inquiries.

3.1.5 Appointment Rescheduling and Cancellation: - P2

3.1.5.1 The system should allow for **easy rescheduling or cancellation** of appointments by both patients and hospital staff, with automatic updates to the system and notifications to the patient.

3.1.5.2 Track and capture **rescheduling reasons** (e.g., patient unavailability, machine failure).

### **3.2 Patient Information and Clinical Data Integration**

3.2.1 Integration with EMR (Electronic Medical Records): - P1

3.2.1.1 **Patient demographic and clinical information** from the EMR should be automatically pulled into the radiology appointment system for seamless data transfer.

3.2.1.2 **Clinical indication and reason for imaging** should be displayed to assist in selecting the correct imaging modality.

3.2.1.3 Integrate patient **medical history, allergies, and previous imaging studies** to avoid redundant or conflicting procedures.

3.2.2 Patient Screening and Safety Checks: - P2

3.2.2.1 The system should integrate patient **safety protocols**, including allergy checks and risk assessments (e.g., pregnancy test before contrast-enhanced imaging).

- 3.2.2.2 The system should automatically flag any contraindications or required preparations based on patient history, and ensure these are verified before the procedure.

### 3.3 Modality & Equipment Availability

- 3.3.1 Real-Time Equipment Scheduling: - P1
  - 3.3.1.1 The system should display the **availability** of various **imaging equipment** (e.g., MRI, CT scanner, ultrasound machines) and **imaging rooms**.
  - 3.3.1.2 Appointments should be scheduled according to the **availability** of the modality, taking into account the room, equipment, and technician's availability.
- 3.3.2 Maintenance Scheduling: - P2
  - 3.3.2.1 **Maintenance schedules** for imaging equipment (e.g., **CT scanners, MRI machines**) should be integrated into the appointment scheduling process to prevent conflicts between maintenance and patient appointments.
  - 3.3.2.2 **Automatic notifications** should be sent to staff about scheduled or unscheduled maintenance that could affect patient appointments.
- 3.3.3 Appointment Priority Management: - P2
  - 3.3.3.1 The system should allow for **prioritization of appointments** based on urgency, such as **STAT orders**, inpatient imaging, or routine outpatient imaging.
  - 3.3.3.2 A **priority flagging system** should ensure that urgent imaging needs are handled first and that appropriate resources (e.g., radiologist or equipment) are allocated.

### 3.4 Patient Preparation & Pre-Procedure Instructions

- 3.4.1 Automated Instructions Based on Procedure Type: - P1
  - 3.4.1.1 The system should send **automated pre-procedure instructions** to patients based on the type of imaging ordered (e.g., fasting instructions for CT with contrast, hydration for MRI).
  - 3.4.1.2 **Pre-procedure reminders** should be sent to patients closer to the appointment time (e.g., 24 hours or 1 hour before).
- 3.4.2 Patient Notifications: - P2
  - 3.4.2.1 Provide **notifications** to patients about what they need to bring to the appointment (e.g., previous imaging films, physician referrals, identification documents).
  - 3.4.2.2 Send reminders for specific **preparation tasks** (e.g., stopping medications, drinking contrast material).
  - 3.4.2.3 Provide the option to **confirm** or **reschedule** the appointment via **SMS** or **email**.
- 3.4.3 Patient Check-In: - P3
  - 3.4.3.1 The system should notify staff of the patient's arrival, and automatically confirm if the patient is properly prepared for the procedure.

### 3.5 Appointment Follow-up and Communication

- 3.5.1 Post-Procedure Communication: - P1
  - 3.5.1.1 After the radiology appointment, the system should notify the **referring physician and patient** about the completion of the study.
  - 3.5.1.2 The **referring physician** should be notified via the **EMR or RIS** when the radiology report is ready, so they can review the results promptly.
- 3.5.2 Follow-up Appointments: - P1
  - 3.5.2.1 The system should support **follow-up appointments** if further imaging is required (e.g., follow-up MRI after initial CT scan).
  - 3.5.2.2 **Automated reminders** should be sent to the patient for any subsequent imaging appointments based on physician instructions.
- 3.5.3 Patient Feedback: - P2
  - 3.5.3.1 Option for **patients to provide feedback** on their experience, including waiting times, staff professionalism, and overall satisfaction with the imaging procedure.

## 4 Modality Scheduling

### 4.1 Modality Configuration

- 4.1.1 Maintain an up-to-date inventory of all radiology equipment/modalities: - P1
  - 4.1.1.1 X-ray, CT, MRI, Ultrasound, PET, Mammography, etc.
- 4.1.2 Parameters to define: - P1
  - 4.1.2.1 Machine ID, modality type, location
  - 4.1.2.2 Operating hours and maintenance schedules
  - 4.1.2.3 Time slot duration (e.g., 15/30/60 mins)
  - 4.1.2.4 Downtime blocks (planned or unplanned)
- 4.1.3 Assign machines to specific technicians or teams

### 4.2 Scheduling Rules & Constraints

- 4.2.1 Slot duration customization per modality and study type – P2
- 4.2.2 Built-in buffers for cleaning, reporting, or emergencies – P2
- 4.2.3 Rules based on patient category: - P2
  - 4.2.3.1 Outpatient, Inpatient, Emergency
- 4.2.4 Conflict detection and resolution: - P2
  - 4.2.4.1 Prevent double-booking of machines or staff

### 4.3 Automated & Manual Scheduling

- 4.3.1 Auto-scheduling: - P2
  - 4.3.1.1 System suggests earliest available slot based on modality availability and patient constraints
- 4.3.2 Manual override: - P2
  - 4.3.2.1 Admin/radiology staff can override auto suggestions
  - 4.3.2.2 Manual reassignment of patient slots
- 4.3.3 Slot lock/reservation before final confirmation – P2

### 4.4 Integration with Order Management

- 4.4.1 Direct scheduling from the Order Management module – P1
- 4.4.2 Link order → scheduled slot → technician assignment – P1
- 4.4.3 Block slots only after order is validated (to prevent dummy bookings) – P1

#### **4.5 Technician and Staff Assignment**

- 4.5.1 Assign radiology technologists and supporting staff to: - P1
  - 4.5.1.1 Modalities
  - 4.5.1.2 Specific slots or procedures
- 4.5.2 Notify staff of changes in daily schedule – P1
- 4.5.3 Track technician shift timings and overlap – P1

#### **4.6 Patient-Centric Scheduling Features**

- 4.6.1 Select slot based on: - P1
  - 4.6.1.1 Patient availability
  - 4.6.1.2 Special needs (e.g., mobility, isolation, anesthesia)
  - 4.6.1.3 Preferences (e.g., gender-specific technologist)
- 4.6.2 Notify patient of appointment via: - P1
  - 4.6.2.1 SMS / Email / Mobile App / Patient Portal
- 4.6.3 Auto-generate appointment card (digital or print) – P1

#### **4.7 Calendar Views & Dashboards**

- 4.7.1 Multiple views: - P2
  - 4.7.1.1 Daily, weekly, per modality, per technician
  - 4.7.1.2 Color-coded slots (available, booked, emergency, downtime)
- 4.7.2 Filters: - P2
  - 4.7.2.1 By location, machine, date, shift, patient type

#### **4.8 Appointment Modifications**

- 4.8.1 Rescheduling, cancellation, or swapping of slots – P2
- 4.8.2 Alert triggers for: - P2
  - 4.8.2.1 Rescheduled slots
  - 4.8.2.2 Last-minute cancellations
  - 4.8.2.3 Delayed patient arrivals
- 4.8.3 Audit trail for all changes (who made the change, when, and why) – P2

#### **4.9 Alerts & Notifications**

- 4.9.1 To patient: - P1
  - 4.9.1.1 Confirmation, reminders (e.g., 24 hrs and 1 hr before)
  - 4.9.1.2 Pre-scan instructions (e.g., fasting, hydration)
- 4.9.2 To radiology staff: - P1
  - 4.9.2.1 New bookings, cancellations, overbook warnings
- 4.9.3 To referring physician: - P1
  - 4.9.3.1 Optional alerts for scheduled/unscheduled delays

#### **4.10 Walk-in and Emergency Handling**

- 4.10.1 Walk-in slot allocation for OPD – P1
- 4.10.2 Dedicated emergency buffers in daily plan – P1

### **5 Radiographer Acknowledgement**

#### **5.1 Procedure Understanding and Acknowledgment**

- 5.1.1 Procedure-Specific Acknowledgment: - P1

5.1.1.1 The system should ensure that the radiographer **acknowledges** the specific radiology procedure being performed, including:

- **Procedure name** (e.g., MRI, CT, X-ray, Ultrasound).
- **Patient preparation requirements**, including fasting, contrast administration, or hydration.
- **Safety protocols** (e.g., removal of metal objects before MRI, contrast checks, radiation safety).

5.1.2 Patient Identification and Consent: - P1

5.1.2.1 The radiographer should confirm that the **patient has been correctly identified** (e.g., name, DOB, and patient ID) to ensure the correct procedure is being performed.

5.1.2.2 **Confirmation that patient consent** has been obtained, especially for invasive procedures, use of contrast, or sedation, should be included in the acknowledgment process.

5.1.3 Radiographer Responsibilities: - P2

5.1.3.1 The system should include a **checklist** or acknowledgment form for the radiographer to confirm their understanding of their responsibilities for the procedure, such as:

- **Positioning the patient** correctly for imaging.
- **Selecting the appropriate imaging settings** based on the patient's condition and the procedure.
- **Following radiation safety guidelines** (e.g., lead shielding, proper exposure limits).

## 5.2 Safety Protocols and Compliance Acknowledgment

5.2.1 Radiation Safety Protocols: - P1

5.2.1.1 The radiographer must **acknowledge** the following **radiation safety protocols** before performing procedures involving radiation (e.g., X-rays, CT scans):

- Proper **dose calculation** and adherence to **ALARA (As Low As Reasonably Achievable)** principles.
- Use of **personal protective equipment (PPE)**, such as lead aprons and thyroid shields.
- Ensuring that **pregnant patients** or **patients of childbearing age** are appropriately screened and informed about the risks of radiation exposure.
- **Ensuring adequate shielding** for patients and staff, especially when multiple exposures are involved.

5.2.2 Infection Control and Hygiene: - P1

5.2.2.1 The radiographer should **acknowledge** understanding of **infection control protocols** when handling imaging equipment, contrast agents, and patient contact:

- **Cleaning** of equipment and imaging rooms before and after use.
- **Hand hygiene** and **glove usage** requirements.

5.2.3 Contrast Administration: - P1

5.2.3.1 If the procedure involves contrast agents, the radiographer must confirm understanding of:

- **Preparation of the contrast material.**
- **Patient allergy screening and reaction protocols.**
- **Proper handling and disposal** of contrast agents.

5.2.4 Emergency Protocols: - P1

5.2.4.1 The system should ensure that the radiographer **acknowledges** the proper **emergency protocols** in case of adverse reactions to contrast agents, radiation accidents, or patient discomfort during the procedure.

- This includes confirming that the radiographer knows how to handle **contrast reactions, medical emergencies, or equipment malfunctions.**

### **5.3 Radiographer Workflow and Procedure Documentation**

5.3.1 Radiographer Workflow Acknowledgment: - P2

5.3.1.1 The radiographer must **acknowledge** their role in the **workflow** for each imaging procedure. This includes:

- **Setting up the imaging equipment** and ensuring all settings are optimized for the patient's needs.
- **Documenting relevant details** (e.g., imaging technique used, exposure parameters, contrast agents).
- **Ensuring patient comfort and safety** during the procedure.
- **Communicating with the radiologist** regarding any special considerations for the imaging study.

5.3.2 Image Quality Control: - P1

5.3.2.1 The system should include a checkbox or **acknowledgment form** for the radiographer to confirm that they will perform quality checks on the images (e.g., ensuring that the images are of acceptable quality, verifying patient positioning, and that the appropriate views are captured).

5.3.2.2 The radiographer should acknowledge any **repeat imaging** required due to suboptimal results, and the necessary actions to mitigate any delays in the patient's procedure.

5.3.3 Documentation of Completion: - P1

- 5.3.3.1 After the procedure, the radiographer must confirm that the procedure has been completed and the images have been uploaded to the PACS system for review.
- 5.3.3.2 An **electronic signature or acknowledgment timestamp** should be recorded in the system for procedural and legal purposes.

## 5.4 Patient Interaction and Communication

- 5.4.1 Communication with Patients: - P2
  - 5.4.1.1 Radiographers must confirm that they have **properly communicated with the patient** regarding the imaging procedure, including explaining what to expect and addressing any concerns.
  - 5.4.1.2 If the patient has **special requirements** (e.g., pediatric, geriatric.), the radiographer must confirm that they have addressed these appropriately.
- 5.4.2 Patient Privacy and Confidentiality: - P2
  - 5.4.2.1 Radiographers should **acknowledge** understanding of **patient confidentiality** protocols and their responsibility to protect patient information, in compliance with privacy regulations.

## 5.5 Emergency and Critical Situations

- 5.5.1 Emergency Acknowledgment: - P2
  - 5.5.1.1 Radiographers must acknowledge emergency protocols for handling critical situations during imaging (e.g., contrast reactions, patient distress, or equipment malfunction).
  - 5.5.1.2 The system should record their acknowledgment of emergency preparedness and steps to follow in such situations.

# 6 Integration with PACS & Modalities

## 6.1 PACS Integration

- 6.1.1 Send Radiology Orders to PACS: - P2
  - 6.1.1.1 Include patient demographics, study details, referring physician, modality, clinical notes
- 6.1.2 Receive Completed Studies from PACS: - P2
  - 6.1.2.1 Auto-link DICOM images to patient's radiology order
  - 6.1.2.2 Receive DICOM SR (Structured Reports), JPEGs, or PDF attachments
- 6.1.3 View Images: - P2
  - 6.1.3.1 Integrate PACS viewer directly into HMIS or provide viewer link
  - 6.1.3.2 Role-based image access (Radiologist, Referring Physician, Technician)
- 6.1.4 Report Storage: - P2
  - 6.1.4.1 Option to store finalized reports in both PACS (for clinical imaging archive) and HMIS.

## 6.2 Image Routing & Archiving

- 6.2.1 Route images to: - P2
  - 6.2.1.1 PACS for long-term storage
  - 6.2.1.2 Local cache/viewer servers for fast access
- 6.2.2 Support for **tiered storage**: - P2

- 6.2.2.1 Fast-access for recent studies
- 6.2.2.2 Cloud/offline/archive storage for older studies

### **6.3 Image & Report Viewer Features**

- 6.3.1 Embedded or integrated viewer in HMIS: - P2
  - 6.3.1.1 Access to images from patient EMR
  - 6.3.1.2 Radiologist-grade tools (zoom, pan, measurement, annotations)
  - 6.3.1.3 Compare with previous studies (side-by-side view)
- 6.3.2 For clinicians: - P2
  - 6.3.2.1 Quick view with basic navigation
  - 6.3.2.2 Mobile/tablet compatible viewer

### **6.4 Reconciliation and Error Handling**

- 6.4.1 Patient/study reconciliation: - P2
  - 6.4.1.1 Resolve mismatches in demographics, modality data
- 6.4.2 Alerts for: - P2
  - 6.4.2.1 Missing or delayed image transfer
  - 6.4.2.2 Unmatched studies
  - 6.4.2.3 DICOM tag inconsistencies

## **7 Image Viewing & Interpretation**

### **7.1 Image Viewer Integration**

- 7.1.1 Embedded PACS Viewer: - P2
  - 7.1.1.1 Seamlessly accessible from within the HMIS interface
  - 7.1.1.2 Zero-footprint viewer preferred (browser-based, no local installation)
- 7.1.2 Viewer Access Points: - P2
  - 7.1.2.1 From radiology workstation
  - 7.1.2.2 From patient EMR
  - 7.1.2.3 From finalized report (linked image)
- 7.1.3 Cross-platform compatibility: - P2
  - 7.1.3.1 Desktop (Windows), tablets, mobile (optional)

### **7.2 Image Navigation & Tools**

- 7.2.1 Standard DICOM Viewer Functions: - P2
  - 7.2.1.1 Zoom, pan, rotate, flip
  - 7.2.1.2 Window leveling (brightness/contrast presets)
  - 7.2.1.3 Cine loop (for dynamic series like USG or MRI)
  - 7.2.1.4 Scroll through image slices (CT/MRI)
- 7.2.2 Measurement Tools: - P2
  - 7.2.2.1 Linear measurement
  - 7.2.2.2 Area/volume estimation
  - 7.2.2.3 Angle measurement
  - 7.2.2.4 Hounsfield unit (CT)
- 7.2.3 Annotation Tools: P 2

- 7.2.3.1 Text labels
- 7.2.3.2 Arrows, shapes
- 7.2.3.3 Structured lesion markers

### **7.3 Prior Study Comparison**

- 7.3.1 Retrieve and display prior studies for the same patient: - P2
  - 7.3.1.1 Auto-matching by body part/modality
  - 7.3.1.2 Side-by-side comparison (current vs previous)
  - 7.3.1.3 Highlight changes in annotations/reports

### **7.4 Export, Share & Archive**

- 7.4.1 Export selected images or annotated screenshots – P2
- 7.4.2 Burn to CD/DVD with embedded viewer – P2
- 7.4.3 Link image references directly in radiology reports – P2
- 7.4.4 Archiving integration with PACS for long-term storage – P2

## **8 Radiology Reporting**

### **8.1 Reporting Workflow Support**

- 8.1.1 Clearly defined statuses for each report: - P1
  - 8.1.1.1 Draft → In Review → Finalized → Verified → Sent to Physician → Archived
- 8.1.2 Worklists to manage reporting queues: - P1
  - 8.1.2.1 Filter by: Unreported, STAT cases, modality, radiologist, department
- 8.1.3 Assignment rules: - P1
  - 8.1.3.1 Manual or auto-assignment of studies to radiologists
  - 8.1.3.2 Peer review or second-opinion routing (optional)

### **8.2 Structured and Free-Text Reporting**

- 8.2.1 Support for both:
  - 8.2.1.1 Structured Reports: - P1
    - Based on standard templates
    - Drop-downs, radio buttons, and structured findings
  - 8.2.1.2 Free-Text Reports: - P1
    - Rich text editor with formatting (bold, bullet, tables)
    - Auto-complete, spelling/grammar tools

### **8.3 Image & Report Linking**

- 8.3.1 Allow linking specific findings in the report to key images or annotations – P1
- 8.3.2 Enable radiologists to mark findings with references in the image viewer (e.g., "See Fig. 1") – P1

### **8.4 Report Approval & Sign-off**

- 8.4.1 Multi-stage validation workflow: - P1

- 8.4.1.1 Preliminary (Resident)
- 8.4.1.2 Finalized (Consultant)
- 8.4.1.3 Verified/Signed-off (Senior Consultant)
- 8.4.2 Electronic signatures with date/time stamps – P1
- 8.4.3 Lock reports after finalization to prevent unauthorized edits – P1
- 8.4.4 Ability to **amend** finalized reports with addenda and full audit trail – P1

## **8.5 Critical Findings & Alert Mechanism**

- 8.5.1 Highlight critical/urgent findings (flag system) – P1
- 8.5.2 Automatic alerts to: - P1
  - 8.5.2.1 Referring physician
  - 8.5.2.2 Emergency department (if applicable)
- 8.5.3 Acknowledgment tracking: - P1
  - 8.5.3.1 Confirm if clinician viewed the report/alert
  - 8.5.3.2 Escalation alerts if not acknowledged within defined time

## **8.6 Quality Control & Audit Trail**

- 8.6.1 Peer review and discrepancy tracking – P2
- 8.6.2 Inter-observer variability logs (if used for teaching or training) – P2
- 8.6.3 Full audit trail for: - P2
  - 8.6.3.1 Report creation
  - 8.6.3.2 Edits
  - 8.6.3.3 Approvals
  - 8.6.3.4 Viewing by clinicians

# **9 Result Communication**

## **9.1 Automated Report Delivery**

- 9.1.1 Seamless routing of finalized reports to: - P1
  - 9.1.1.1 Referring physician
  - 9.1.1.2 Treating physician / department
  - 9.1.1.3 Electronic Medical Record (EMR)
- 9.1.2 Auto-notification when a report is: - P1
  - 9.1.2.1 Finalized
  - 9.1.2.2 Flagged as critical/urgent
- 9.1.3 Reports should be accessible: - P1
  - 9.1.3.1 Within the patient's chart
  - 9.1.3.2 From the physician's dashboard/worklist

## **9.2 Multichannel Notification System**

- 9.2.1 Support for multiple notification channels: - P1
  - 9.2.1.1 In-system alerts/popup
  - 9.2.1.2 Email notifications (with or without attachment)
  - 9.2.1.3 SMS alerts (summary/availability notice)
  - 9.2.1.4 Mobile app push notifications (if available)

## **9.3 Critical Result Communication Workflow**

- 9.3.1 Dedicated flow for critical, urgent, or unexpected findings: - P1

- 9.3.1.1 Auto-escalation rules for high-priority results
- 9.3.1.2 Require **acknowledgment receipt** from recipient
- 9.3.1.3 Record of communication: date/time, mode (call, message), and receiver
- 9.3.1.4 Alert escalation to second-tier staff (if no response in defined timeframe)

#### 9.4 Physician Worklist Integration

- 9.4.1 Auto-populate a **physician's worklist** with: - P1
  - 9.4.1.1 New reports for review
  - 9.4.1.2 Reports awaiting acknowledgment (critical)
  - 9.4.1.3 Comparison with previous studies
- 9.4.2 Filter options: unread, abnormal, critical, date range, modality – P1

#### 9.5 Patient Notification and Access

- 9.5.1 Patient portal integration to: - P1
  - 9.5.1.1 Notify patient that imaging results are ready
  - 9.5.1.2 Allow secure viewing/download of report
  - 9.5.1.3 Allow viewing of selected key images or screenshots
  - 9.5.1.4 Delay release if clinically sensitive (configurable hold period)

#### 9.6 Documentation & Acknowledgment Tracking

- 9.6.1 Log all report deliveries: - P1
  - 9.6.1.1 Who received the report
  - 9.6.1.2 When it was viewed
  - 9.6.1.3 Confirmation/acknowledgment (especially for critical reports)
- 9.6.2 Track unsuccessful communication attempts – P1
- 9.6.3 Provide summary report of unacknowledged critical results – P1

#### 9.7 Communication with Inpatient/ED Teams

- 9.7.1 Automatic notification to: - P1
  - 9.7.1.1 Nurse Station
  - 9.7.1.2 Ward Doctor
- 9.7.2 Integration with inpatient dashboards

#### 9.8 Interdepartmental Communication Support

- 9.8.1 Share reports with: - P2
  - 9.8.1.1 Multidisciplinary teams (Tumor Board, ICU, Surgery)
  - 9.8.1.2 Anesthesia or surgical planning teams (e.g., pre-op imaging review)
- 9.8.2 Support for tagging/co-notifying multiple recipients – P2

### 10 Administrative & Workflow Management

#### 10.1 Radiology Department Scheduling & Resource Allocation

- 10.1.1 Staff and Equipment Scheduling: - P1
  - 10.1.1.1 Qualified Radiologist is heading the radiology department
  - 10.1.1.2 Qualified sub-section heads are appointed
  - 10.1.1.3 **Radiology Department** should have the ability to schedule radiologists, technologists, and support staff for shifts, ensuring coverage based on patient volumes and study types.

10.1.1.4 Schedule imaging modalities (e.g., MRI, CT, X-ray) and imaging rooms based on availability, workload, and priority.

10.1.1.5 **Automatic conflict resolution** to prevent over-scheduling or double-booking of staff, rooms, or machines.

10.1.1.6 Enable **real-time updates** to schedules in response to emergencies, patient arrivals, or cancellations.

#### 10.1.2 Equipment & Room Management: - P1

10.1.2.1 **Booking and availability management** for imaging equipment (e.g., MRI, CT scanners) and imaging rooms.

10.1.2.2 Integration with **maintenance schedules** for equipment downtime, ensuring no scheduled studies conflict with routine maintenance or repairs.

10.1.2.3 Ability to track usage statistics for imaging equipment (e.g., usage hours, peak usage times).

### 10.2 Radiology Study Workflow Management

#### 10.2.1 Study Order and Assignment: - P2

10.2.1.1 Once a radiology order is placed (via the **RIS or EMR**), it should be automatically assigned to the appropriate technician based on skill set, workload, and modality.

10.2.1.2 **Workflow prioritization** for STAT, urgent, or scheduled studies, ensuring time-sensitive cases are handled immediately. – P2

10.2.1.3 The ability for **technologists to acknowledge** and update the status of orders (e.g., "started," "completed"). – P2

### 10.3 Patient Flow & Coordination

#### 10.3.1 Patient Check-In & Queue Management: - P1

10.3.1.1 Patients should be checked in upon arrival, and their information should be available to technologists, radiologists, and other healthcare providers.

10.3.1.2 **Queue management system** for imaging studies that can prioritize patient flow based on urgency (e.g., STAT studies, inpatient vs. outpatient).

10.3.1.3 Support for **tracking patient status** at various stages (e.g., in waiting room, in procedure, report pending).

#### 10.3.2 Patient Tracking: - P1

10.3.2.1 Real-time visibility of patient location (e.g., waiting for imaging, in procedure, or in recovery).

10.3.2.2 Integration with **EMR** to access and update patient records as they move through the radiology workflow.

10.3.2.3 Automated **notifications** for patients when their procedure is about to start, delays occur, or additional instructions are needed.

### 10.4 Communication and Coordination Across Teams

#### 10.4.1 Internal Communication Tools: - P1

10.4.1.1 Real-time communication between radiologists, technologists, and administrative staff through the **radiology module** for efficient workflow (e.g., instant messaging, task notifications).

10.4.1.2 Notifications of critical cases, abnormal findings, or urgent tasks to relevant team members.

#### 10.4.2 Multidisciplinary Collaboration: - P2

10.4.2.1 The ability for **radiologists to collaborate** with referring physicians, oncologists, surgeons, or specialists for case review and diagnosis.

10.4.2.2 Tools for **multidisciplinary meetings** where radiologists can present cases to other specialists for diagnosis and treatment planning.

### 10.5 Task Assignment & Tracking

#### 10.5.1 Task Creation and Assignment: - P2

10.5.1.1 Administrative and radiology managers can create and assign tasks (e.g., follow-ups, equipment checks, study reviews) to staff members.

10.5.1.2 Tasks should be categorized by priority (high, medium, low) and tracked through completion.

#### 10.5.2 Task Notifications: - P2

10.5.2.1 Notifications for new tasks, reminders, and updates should be sent to the relevant individuals (e.g., for equipment maintenance, patient follow-ups, or study prioritization).

10.5.2.2 **Escalation management** to automatically alert senior staff if tasks are not completed within set timeframes.

### 10.6 Performance Monitoring and Reporting

#### 10.6.1 Staff Performance Analytics: - P2

10.6.1.1 Track individual staff performance in terms of:

- **Task completion times** (e.g., imaging study completion, report finalization).
- **Image quality and accuracy** (e.g., rejection rates for poor-quality images).
- **Turnaround time (TAT)** for imaging procedures and reporting.
- **Diagnostic accuracy** (e.g., false positives/negatives, discrepancies between preliminary and final reports).

#### 10.6.2 Departmental Performance Reports: - P2

10.6.2.1 Monthly, weekly, or real-time performance reports, including metrics such as:

- Imaging volumes by modality.
- Average waiting times for imaging studies.
- Turnaround times for reports.
- Resource utilization (e.g., imaging equipment, rooms, staff).
- Error rates, such as rejected or repeated imaging studies.

#### 10.6.3 Operational Efficiency: - P2

10.6.3.1 Monitor and report on equipment utilization, room occupancy, and staff efficiency.

10.6.3.2 Identify bottlenecks in the radiology workflow (e.g., areas where delays occur) and generate recommendations for improvement.

## **10.7 Inventory and Supply Chain Management**

10.7.1 Inventory Tracking: - P1

10.7.1.1 Monitor and manage inventory levels of consumables (e.g., contrast media, film, gloves) and imaging equipment.

10.7.1.2 Automated inventory alerts when supplies are running low and need to be replenished.

10.7.1.3 Integration with **hospital supply chain management** systems for seamless ordering and restocking.

10.7.2 Equipment Lifecycle Management: - P2

10.7.2.1 Track equipment usage, maintenance schedules, and end-of-life for imaging equipment.

10.7.2.2 Schedule regular preventive maintenance and calibration based on manufacturer guidelines and internal usage data.

10.7.2.3 Monitor warranties and service contracts for imaging machines.

## **10.8 Compliance & Documentation Management**

10.8.1 Regulatory Compliance Tracking: - P1

10.8.1.1 Valid PNRA License

10.8.1.2 Ensure that all workflows comply with healthcare regulations, such as **radiation safety standards**.

10.8.1.3 Track and log **compliance checks** for equipment maintenance, staff certifications, and imaging protocols.

10.8.2 Documentation and Reporting: - P1

10.8.2.1 Store and manage necessary documentation for quality audits, accreditation, and certifications.

10.8.2.2 Ensure audit trails are available for all radiology activities, including imaging procedures, report generation, and task completion.

## **10.9 Emergency & STAT Case Management**

10.9.1 Emergency Case Prioritization: - P2

10.9.1.1 Flag **emergency/STAT cases** for immediate attention, ensuring that urgent studies are prioritized in the workflow.

10.9.1.2 Automatically allocate resources (e.g., imaging equipment, staff) to STAT cases.

10.9.1.3 Provide a **tracking mechanism** for STAT case handling to ensure that all steps are completed promptly.

10.9.2 Escalation Protocols: - P2

10.9.3 Define workflows for escalating critical issues (e.g., system downtimes, equipment failure, delays in report delivery) to the appropriate staff.

10.9.4 Real-time alerts for critical delays or anomalies in the workflow, ensuring rapid problem resolution.

10.9.5 Quality Control

10.9.10 Record issues (e.g., motion artifacts, incorrect positioning, under/overexposure)

10.9.24 Department

10.9.25 Include image reject analysis and corrective notes - P1

10.9.27 Monitor: - P2

10.9.28 Scan success rate (1st-time correct scan)

10.9.29 Quality rating of images

10.9.30 Compliance with safety protocols and checklists

10.9.31 Enable peer review of image acquisition techniques - P2

10.9.32 Radiologist Reporting Quality

10.9.33 Peer review system for report accuracy: - P2

10.9.34 Random or targeted case reviews

10.9.35 Discrepancy flagging (minor/major discrepancies)

10.9.36 Track agreement levels between radiologists

10.9.37 Escalation path for: - P2

10.9.38 Clinically significant errors

10.9.39 Incomplete or delayed reports

10.9.40 Equipment Performance Monitoring

10.9.41 Record: - P1

10.9.42 Calibration logs

10.9.43 Preventive maintenance schedules

10.9.44 Downtime logs (planned and unplanned)

10.9.45 Imaging artifacts linked to specific devices

10.9.46 Alerts for overdue maintenance or frequent errors

10.9.47 Checklist Compliance Monitoring

10.9.48 Track whether pre-scan and safety checklists were completed: - P2

10.9.49 Patient ID confirmation

10.9.50 Contrast allergy checks

- 10.9.51 Pregnancy screening
- 10.9.52 Policies and procedures guide the care providers for safe exposure of patients to radiation – P2
- 10.9.53 Policies and procedures guide the safe handling, proper labelling, exposures and processing of x-ray films – P2
- 10.9.54 Policies and procedures guide the safe disposal of films & reagents – P2
- 10.9.55 Compliance dashboards for departments or individual users – P2

### **Radiation Safety – P1**

- 10.9.56 Digital radiation safety SOPs are available
- 10.9.57 Documentation of the communication of the radiation safety SOPs to the employees
- 10.9.58 Details of a certified professional for ensuring radiation safety activities
- 10.9.59 Regular radiation safety monitoring reports are generated
- 10.9.60 There are appropriate equipment and facilities to ensure radiation safety
- 10.9.61 Regular testing of dosimeters of all staff is documented
- 10.9.62 Proper waiting areas protected from radiation for waiting patients and attendants are available
- 10.9.63 Entry of attendants is restricted inside the exposure room
- 10.9.64 Attendants inside the exposure room are protected
- 10.9.65 All incident reports are documented
- 10.9.66 Required decontamination facilities are available / accessible

### **Radioactive Material Safety – P1**

- 10.9.67 Only authorized persons are permitted to enter radioactive material storage area
- 10.9.68 Any transportation of radioactive material is properly recorded

### **10.10 Audit Trails & Traceability**

- 10.10.1 Full logs for: - P1
  - 10.10.1.1 Who performed each scan
  - 10.10.1.2 Who finalized reports
  - 10.10.1.3 Quality ratings and comments
- 10.10.2 Timestamped actions with user ID and role – P1
- 10.10.3 Non-editable history of QC feedback – P1

### **10.11 Corrective Action Workflow**

- 10.11.1 Allow quality flags to trigger: - P2
  - 10.11.1.1 Supervisor review
  - 10.11.1.2 Staff re-training requests
  - 10.11.1.3 Equipment re-calibration
- 10.11.2 Documentation of actions taken and outcomes – P2

### **10.12 Standardization & Protocol Compliance**

- 10.12.1 Monitor use of: - P1

10.12.1.1 Standardized protocols per modality (e.g., brain MRI protocol)

10.12.1.2 Structured reporting templates

10.12.2 Alerts for protocol deviation or outdated templates – P2

## 10.13 External Quality Assurance

10.13.1 EQA of the Radiological Diagnostic Center is ensured through external assessment of nationally / internationally recognized bodies. – P1

10.13.2 Record of EQA reports is maintained – P1

## 11 Films, Reagents, and Consumables Management

### 11.1 Inventory Management

11.1.1 Real-Time Tracking: - P1

11.1.1.1 The system should allow for **real-time tracking** of all consumables, reagents, and films used in the radiology department. This includes tracking **quantities, usage rates, and stock levels** for each item (e.g., X-ray films, MRI contrast agents, ultrasound gel).

11.1.1.2 Inventory levels should be updated automatically after each procedure, and the system should provide **alerts** when stock levels are low or about to expire.

11.1.2 Barcode Integration: - P2

11.1.2.1 The system should support **barcode technology** to scan and track items as they are used, moved, or replenished. This improves the accuracy of inventory records and reduces manual entry errors.

11.1.2.2 Each item (film, reagent, consumable) should have a **unique identifier** (e.g., barcode tag) to facilitate seamless tracking.

11.1.3 Batch and Expiry Management: - P1

11.1.3.1 The system should track the **batch number and expiry dates** of consumables, reagents, and films. This is particularly important for medical reagents, contrast agents, and other time-sensitive materials.

11.1.3.2 **Alerts** should be generated for items approaching expiration, and expired items should be flagged for removal from inventory.

11.1.4 Automatic Replenishment: - P2

11.1.4.1 The system should support **automatic reorder points** for consumables and reagents based on historical usage and predefined thresholds. When stock levels fall below the threshold, the system should automatically generate purchase orders or alerts for reordering.

11.1.4.2 For critical items (e.g., contrast agents, films), **low stock alerts** should be set to trigger immediately to avoid any disruptions in procedures.

### 11.2 Consumption and Usage Tracking

11.2.1 Linking Supplies to Procedures: - P2

11.2.1.1 The system should allow the **linking of consumables** to specific radiology procedures. For instance, when an X-ray or MRI is performed, the system should automatically log the **type and quantity** of films, reagents, or other consumables used during the procedure.

11.2.1.2 This helps track **usage patterns** for different types of procedures and assists in **budgeting and cost analysis**.

11.2.2 Consumable Usage by Patient: - P2

11.2.2.1 The system should allow for tracking the consumable usage per patient so that accurate **billing** can be done for procedures involving consumables like contrast agents, films, and other materials.

11.2.2.2 This tracking should be **integrated** with the **patient's electronic health record (EHR)** to ensure accurate billing and inventory reconciliation.

11.2.3 Consumable Waste Management: - P2

11.2.3.1 The system should monitor **wastage rates** of consumables and provide alerts when usage exceeds normal levels (e.g., high levels of unused or discarded films, reagents, or contrast agents).

11.2.3.2 The system should allow staff to record **reasons for wastage** (e.g., defective reagents, incorrect procedure, expired consumables) to help identify patterns and mitigate waste.

## 11.3 Procurement and Supplier Management

11.3.1 Supplier Database Integration: - P2

11.3.1.1 The system should include a **supplier management feature** that stores information about suppliers of radiology consumables, reagents, and films. This information includes contact details, price lists, lead times, and order histories.

11.3.1.2 The system should support **automatic generation of purchase orders** to suppliers based on stock levels and usage predictions.

11.3.2 Purchase Order Management: - P2

- The system should support **automated purchase orders** for replenishing consumables and reagents when stock levels fall below a certain threshold. The system should also allow for manual purchase orders and provide approval workflows for purchases to ensure that orders are approved by authorized personnel.

11.3.3 Order Tracking: - P2

11.3.3.1 The system should track the **status** of orders (e.g., **pending, shipped, delivered**) and update inventory upon the receipt of goods.

11.3.3.2 It should also track **delivery times**, and provide alerts if the delivery is delayed or if there is an issue with the order (e.g., incorrect quantity or defective products).

## 11.4 Cost Control and Budgeting

11.4.1 Cost Tracking: - P3

11.4.1.1 The system should track the **cost of consumables**, reagents, and films used per procedure or patient. This allows for accurate **costing** of imaging procedures and ensures that departmental budgets are adhered to.

11.4.1.2 The system should generate **cost reports** that show the total consumption and costs of different types of consumables (e.g., films, contrast agents) over a given period, helping in budgeting and cost control.

11.4.2 Consumable Usage Analysis: - P3

11.4.2.1 The system should allow for **detailed reporting and analysis** of consumable usage trends. This can help identify areas of **high consumption, wastage, or underutilization**.

11.4.2.2 It should also enable **comparison of suppliers' prices**, helping in cost optimization by sourcing consumables from the most cost-effective suppliers.

11.4.3 Inventory Value Calculation: - P3

11.4.3.1 The system should calculate the current value of the consumables in stock based on the purchase cost and quantities available. This will help in financial forecasting and budget **planning**.

## 11.5 Compliance and Regulatory Tracking

11.5.1 Regulatory Compliance for Reagents: - P3

11.5.1.1 Reagents used for diagnostic purposes must be tracked for **traceability**, ensuring that in case of any product recalls or issues, the specific batches can be traced.

11.5.2 Film and Reagent Labeling: - P2

11.5.2.1 For each film or reagent used, the system should allow for **automated labeling** (e.g., batch number, expiration date, lot number) to ensure traceability for compliance, quality control, and patient safety.

11.5.2.2 The system should support **audit trails**, ensuring that every action related to films, reagents, or consumables (e.g., usage, wastage, or procurement) is logged with a time stamp and user information.

## 12 Integration with Other Modules:

### 12.1 Integration with EMR (Electronic Medical Records)

12.1.1 Patient Information Synchronization: - P2

12.1.1.1 Automatically sync patient demographics (e.g., name, age, gender, medical record number) between **HMIS** and **EMR** to ensure up-to-date patient data.

12.1.1.2 Retrieve and display patient history from the **EMR** for contextual information (e.g., allergies, medications, past imaging studies) when processing imaging orders.

12.1.2 Radiology Results in EMR: - P2

12.1.2.1 Once the report is completed, the results should be automatically transferred to the **EMR**, making it available for the attending physician.

12.1.2.2 Integration of structured reports (e.g., diagnostic summaries, findings, and recommendations) into the patient's electronic chart.

12.1.3 Clinical Decision Support: - P3

- 12.1.3.1 Radiology-related decision support tools (e.g., guidelines for imaging, alerts for contraindications such as allergies or pregnancy) should be integrated into the EMR for real-time decision-making.

## 12.2 **Integration with Laboratory Module**

- 12.2.1 Clinical Lab Data for Imaging: - P2
  - 12.2.1.1 Access laboratory results (e.g., renal function tests) within the radiology module to determine eligibility for imaging with contrast media.
  - 12.2.1.2 Automatically update radiology orders based on lab results (e.g., contraindications for contrast if kidney function is abnormal).
- 12.2.2 Data Exchange for Diagnostics: - p2
  - 12.2.2.1 Sync diagnostic findings or imaging-related laboratory data (e.g., imaging biomarkers) between the **Laboratory and Radiology Module**.

## 12.3 **Integration with HMIS**

- 12.3.1 Patient Admission and Discharge Information: - P2
  - 12.3.1.1 Integrate with **HMIS** to pull patient admission/discharge data for radiology workflows.
  - 12.3.1.2 Real-time updates from **HMIS** for patient status changes that could affect imaging schedules or protocols.
- 12.3.2 Radiology Billing Integration: - P2
  - 12.3.2.1 Synchronize billing data between **HMIS and Radiology Module** to ensure proper charges are applied for imaging services.
  - 12.3.2.2 Enable cost tracking, including labor and equipment usage, and integrate charges with the patient's financial record in HIS.
- 12.3.3 Clinical Alerts & Notifications: - P1
  - 12.3.3.1 HIS should send alerts to the Radiology Department for **critical findings** or special imaging orders, such as STAT or emergency imaging.

## 12.4 **Integration with Billing Systems**

- 12.4.1 Radiology Billing: - P2
  - 12.4.1.1 Sync billing data between **HMIS and Billing Systems** to automatically generate invoices for radiology services (e.g., imaging study, technician time, contrast media).
  - 12.4.1.2 Integration should allow for automated billing based on completed imaging studies, including any modifications for special procedures (e.g., contrast-enhanced imaging).
- 12.4.2 Insurance Claims: - P2
  - 12.4.2.1 Integration to allow submission of insurance claims directly from the **Radiology Module** for procedures performed, ensuring accurate coding and fast processing.
- 12.4.3 Financial Reporting: - P2
  - 12.4.3.1 Share billing data with the hospital's financial system for revenue generation and auditing.

## 12.5 **Integration with Reporting Systems**

- 12.5.1 Integration for Report Distribution: - P1

12.5.1.1 Allow finalized radiology reports to be sent to various hospital departments (e.g., referring physician, attending physician, nursing, emergency department).

12.5.1.2 Reports should be viewable in real-time by authorized personnel (e.g., radiologists, physicians, nurses).

## **12.6 Integration with Workflow and Resource Management Systems**

12.6.1 Scheduling & Resource Allocation: - P1

12.6.1.1 Sync with hospital-wide scheduling and resource management systems to ensure that imaging resources (e.g., machines, rooms, staff) are properly allocated for all imaging studies.

12.6.1.2 Integration with **staffing systems** to optimize workload distribution for technologists and radiologists.

12.6.2 Stat & Emergency Case Management: - P1

- Enable automatic prioritization and resource allocation for stat and emergency cases across all connected systems (e.g., RIS, HIS, PACS).

## **13 Infrastructure and Civic Amenities Maintenance – P1**

13.1.1 Documentation of the dedicated and separate areas for administrative and technical areas

13.1.2 Documentation of the adequate facilities and well maintained civic amenities for the comfort of the patients and attendants

13.1.3 Measures to limit access to exposure areas of the radiology department

13.1.4 Separate change rooms for males and females

13.1.5 clean toilets/ washrooms with bolts, preferably separate for males and females

13.1.6 Facilitated toilets for disabled patients are available

13.1.7 Disabled patients are facilitated for the diagnostic tests

13.1.8 Directional arrows pointing towards various important areas for patients are displayed

13.1.9 **Dedicated Area for Stretchers and Wheelchairs:**

13.1.9.1 **Area Size:** [Enter square footage of dedicated area]

13.1.9.2 **Accessibility:** [Yes/No – Is the area easily accessible for staff and patients?]

13.1.10 Functional Wheelchairs Availability:

13.1.10.1 **Total Number of Wheelchairs:** [Enter number of available wheelchairs]

13.1.10.2 **Wheelchairs In Use:** [Number of currently used wheelchairs]

13.1.10.3 **Available Wheelchairs:** [Calculated number of available wheelchairs]

13.1.10.4 **Maintenance Status:** [All functional/Under maintenance]

13.1.11 Functional Stretchers Availability:

- 13.1.11.1 **Total Number of Stretchers:** [Enter number of available stretchers]
- 13.1.11.2 **Stretchers In Use:** [Number of currently used stretchers]
- 13.1.11.3 **Available Stretchers:** [Calculated number of available stretchers]
- 13.1.11.4 **Maintenance Status:** [All functional/Under maintenance]
- 13.1.12 Porters Availability:
  - 13.1.12.1 **Number of Porters Per Shift:** [Enter number of porters assigned per shift]
  - 13.1.12.2 **Porter Schedule:** [Link to digital shift schedule of porters]
- 13.1.13 **Building Maintenance and Safety:**
  - 13.1.13.1 Manage the **maintenance** of radiology department, including **HVAC systems** (heating, ventilation, air conditioning), **plumbing, electrical systems, and lighting**.
  - 13.1.13.2 Track **preventive maintenance schedules** to ensure regular inspections and servicing of critical infrastructure like **elevators, water supply systems, and emergency power systems**.
  - 13.1.13.3 Record and **manage facility repair requests**, including emergency repairs and routine maintenance tasks.

#### **Registration and Reception – P1**

- 13.1.14 Sufficient Registration Counters Available:
  - 13.1.14.1 **Total Number of Counters:** [Enter number of counters available]
  - 13.1.14.2 **Counters In Use:** [Enter number of counters actively used]
  - 13.1.14.3 **Counters Available:** [Calculated number of available counters]
  - 13.1.14.4 **Counters Segregated by Gender:** [Male/Female counters available]
- 13.1.15 Reception/Registration Desk Staffing:
  - 13.1.15.1 **Number of Staff per Shift:** [Enter number of reception staff per shift]
  - 13.1.15.2 **Staff Schedule:** [Link to reception staff duty roster]

#### **Cleanliness and Hygiene – P1**

- 13.1.16 Electric Wires Sealed:
  - 13.1.16.1 **Status:** [All sealed/Partial sealing required]
  - 13.1.16.2 **Inspection Date:** [Last inspected date]
  - 13.1.16.3 **Next Inspection:** [Next scheduled inspection]
- 13.1.17 Anti-Fly/Mosquito/Rodent Measures:
  - 13.1.17.1 **Measures In Place:** [Pest control schedule or actions]
  - 13.1.17.2 **Last Inspection:** [Date of last inspection]
  - 13.1.17.3 **Next Inspection:** [Date of next inspection]
- 13.1.18 Availability of Clean Linen:

13.1.18.1 **Total Clean Linen Available:** [Enter number of linens available]

13.1.18.2 **Clean Linen In Use:** [Number of linens in use]

13.1.18.3 **Available Linen:** [Number of linens available]

13.1.19 Cleanliness of Radiology Rooms:

13.1.19.1 **Room 1:** [Clean/Needs Attention]

13.1.19.2 **Room 2:** [Clean/Needs Attention]

13.1.19.3 **Room Details:** [Link to ward/room cleanliness records]

13.1.20 Cleanliness of Corridors and Toilets:

13.1.20.1 **Corridors Clean:** [Yes/No, Date of Last Cleaning]

13.1.20.2 **Toilets Clean:** [Yes/No, Date of Last Cleaning]

## 13.2 Power and Equipment

13.2.1 **Power and Electricity Supply:** - P1

13.2.1.1 Track and manage the **electricity supply**, including **backup generators**, and **UPS (Uninterruptible Power Supply)**.

13.2.1.2 Maintain records of **power outages** and monitor the functioning of **emergency generators**.

13.2.1.3 **Energy management:** Integrate systems for managing the **efficient use of energy** across the hospital (e.g., **solar power, LED lighting, smart energy meters**).

13.2.2 Backup Power Supply Checking: - P1

13.2.2.1 **Status:** [Available/Not Available]

13.2.2.2 **Last Test Date:** [Date of last backup power test]

13.2.2.3 **Next Test Date:** [Scheduled date for next test]

13.2.3 CCTV Camera Installation: - P1

13.2.3.1 **Total Number of Cameras:** [Enter number of CCTV cameras installed in ED]

13.2.3.2 **Cameras in Operation:** [Enter number of operational cameras]

13.2.3.3 **Live Monitoring:** [Link to real-time CCTV monitoring]

## 13.3 Security and Access Control

13.3.1 **Security Systems:** - P2

13.3.1.1 Monitor **security systems**, including **CCTV surveillance, access control, and visitor management systems**.

13.3.1.2 Integrate with **emergency security protocols** such as **lockdown procedures, patient abduction prevention, and staff safety**.

13.3.2 **Access Control and Role-Based Permissions:** - P2

13.3.2.1 Ensure **restricted access** to sensitive areas.

13.3.2.2 Monitor **staff access** based on **role-based permissions** to ensure the right personnel access the right areas (e.g., only **doctors, nurses, and authorized staff** should access patient care areas).

## 14 SOPs / Protocols and Guidelines Repository

### 14.1 Digitized Repository of SOPs / Protocols / Guidelines / Laws & Regulation

14.1.1 SOPs and Protocols Repository: - P1

14.1.1.1 **SOP/Protocol Name:** [List of all available SOPs and protocols]

14.1.1.2 **Document Type:** [Link to PDF/Word document for each SOP]

14.1.1.3 **Date Last Updated:** [MM/DD/YYYY]

14.1.1.4 **Version History:** [Link to historical versions of the SOPs/Protocols]

14.1.1.5 **Search & Filter Options:**

- **Search Bar:** [Allow staff to search for specific SOPs, procedures, or diseases]
- **Filter by Role:** [Filter guidelines by role (e.g., Doctors, Nurses, Support Staff)]
- **Filter by Protocol Type:** [Filter by category like Trauma, Cardiology, Infectious Disease, etc.]
- **Filter by Status:** [Active/Archived]

14.1.2 Documentary evidence of implementation of SOPs / Protocols / Guidelines / Laws & Regulations – P1

## 14.2 **Interactive, Searchable Database with Role-Specific Guidelines**

14.2.1 Interactive Guidelines Database: - P2

14.2.1.1 **Searchable Database:**

- **Search Criteria:** [Allow users to search for guidelines by key terms, disease, procedure, or staff role]
- **Role-Specific Guidelines:**
  - **Doctors:** [Link to guidelines relevant to medical staff, e.g., advanced trauma life support, resuscitation protocols]
  - **Nurses:** [Link to nursing guidelines, e.g., infection control protocols, patient monitoring procedures]
  - **Support Staff:** [Link to housekeeping, maintenance, and transport protocols]
  - **Clinical Guidelines by Procedure:** [List of protocols for procedures like intubation, blood transfusion, etc.]

14.2.2 **Guideline Categories:** - P1

14.2.3 **Real-Time Updates:** - P1

14.2.3.1 **Latest Changes:** [Display recent updates and modifications to any guideline]

14.2.3.2 **Change Log:** [Link to a log detailing who made the changes and the reason for the update]

## 14.3 **Training and Education**

14.3.1 **e-Learning Modules:** - P2

14.3.1.1 **Training Course Title:** [e.g., “Basic Trauma Management,” “Infection Control Practices”]

14.3.1.2 **Course Description:** [Brief overview of the course]

#### 14.3.1.3 Module Content:

- **Videos:** [Training videos covering various emergency care protocols, donning/doffing PPE, etc.]
- **Interactive Quizzes:** [Quizzes to assess understanding of the protocol content]
- **Case Studies:** [Real-life case study simulations for application of protocols]

#### 14.3.1.4 Staff Progress Tracking:

- **Completion Status:** [Track who has completed the training module]
- **Passing Criteria:** [Display minimum score to pass e-learning courses]
- **Progress Tracking:** [Allow staff to see their own training progress]
- **Certificates:** [Automated certification upon completion of a training module]

#### 14.3.1.5 Training Module Library:

- **Available Courses:** [List of available e-learning modules]
- **Module Duration:** [Time required to complete each module]
- **Review Status:** [Indicates when the module was last reviewed and updated]

### 15 Reporting & Analytics

#### 15.1 Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) for Radiology Operations – PI

##### 15.1.1 Scan Volume:

- 15.1.1.1 Total number of imaging studies performed daily/weekly/monthly
- 15.1.1.2 Breakdown by modality (e.g., X-ray, CT, MRI, Ultrasound)

##### 15.1.2 Turnaround Time (TAT):

- 15.1.2.1 Time from study order to scan completion
- 15.1.2.2 Time from scan completion to report finalization
- 15.1.2.3 Time from report finalization to delivery

##### 15.1.3 Report Discrepancy Rate:

- 15.1.3.1 Percentage of cases with discrepancies between preliminary and final reports
- 15.1.3.2 Flag discrepancies based on severity (e.g., minor vs. major)

##### 15.1.4 Patient Wait Times:

- 15.1.4.1 Average time from patient arrival to imaging completion
- 15.1.4.2 Wait time for urgent/STAT studies

##### 15.1.5 Technologist Performance:

- 15.1.5.1 Scan quality (first-time accuracy)
- 15.1.5.2 Image rejection rates by technologist and modality

15.1.5.3 Compliance with safety protocols and procedures

15.1.6 Radiologist Performance:

15.1.6.1 Report accuracy rate (e.g., error rates, discrepancies)

15.1.6.2 Report turnaround times

15.1.6.3 Number of cases reviewed per day

## 15.2 Operational Efficiency & Workflow Analytics

15.2.1 Utilization of Modalities: - P2

15.2.1.1 Measure how often each modality is in use (e.g., MRI, CT scanner uptime)

15.2.1.2 Idle times for equipment

15.2.1.3 Equipment downtime due to maintenance or malfunction

15.2.2 Study Completion Rate: - P2

15.2.2.1 Percentage of studies completed versus scheduled

15.2.2.2 Breakdown by modality, location, and technician

15.2.3 Room/Workstation Utilization: - P2

15.2.3.1 Utilization of imaging rooms or stations

15.2.3.2 Peak usage times and bottlenecks

15.2.4 Queue Management: - P2

15.2.4.1 Number of studies in queue for each modality or department

15.2.4.2 Priority studies, STAT studies, and backlogs

15.2.5 Appointment Tracking Reports: - P2

15.2.5.1 **Radiology managers** should have access to **reports** on scheduled, completed, and canceled appointments.

15.2.5.2 **Real-time dashboards** should show booking statistics, modality usage, and department capacity.

15.2.6 No-Show and Cancellations Tracking: - P2

15.2.6.1 Track **no-shows** and **last-minute cancellations** to help improve appointment scheduling efficiency.

15.2.6.2 The system should generate **alerts or reports** to help identify patterns and enable follow-up actions (e.g., reminders for patients who have missed appointments).

## 15.3 Quality Control & Patient Safety Reporting

15.3.1 Repeat/Rejected Studies: - P1

15.3.1.1 Track repeat or rejected studies by modality and reason (e.g., motion artifacts, poor positioning)

15.3.1.2 Monitor repeat rates per technician or radiologist

15.3.2 Incident Reporting: - P1

15.3.2.1 Log and categorize incidents (e.g., contrast reactions, falls, imaging complications)

15.3.2.2 Track actions taken and resolution status

15.3.3 Adverse Events and Critical Findings: - P1

- 15.3.3.1 Number and type of adverse events (e.g., allergic reactions to contrast)

- 15.3.3.2 Percentage of critical findings acknowledged within the specified timeframe

- 15.3.4 Compliance with Standards: - P1

- 15.3.4.1 Monitor adherence to safety protocols (e.g., radiation safety checks, informed consent)

- 15.3.4.2 Regulatory compliance for imaging procedures (e.g., ACR, FDA)

## **15.4 Financial & Resource Utilization Reporting**

- 15.4.1 Cost Analytics: - P2

- 15.4.1.1 Cost per imaging study (including staff, equipment, and consumables)

- 15.4.1.2 Revenue per study (for private practice or hospital billing)

- 15.4.1.3 Cost of contrast media and consumables used

- 15.4.2 Billing & Reimbursement Analytics: - P2

- 15.4.2.1 Number of billed studies and revenue generated per modality

- 15.4.2.2 Reimbursement rates for different insurance providers or government programs

- 15.4.2.3 Payment status and outstanding balances for imaging services

- 15.4.3 Resource Utilization: - P2

- 15.4.3.1 Equipment utilization rates (e.g., number of MRI scans performed per day)

- 15.4.3.2 Staffing levels and costs per modality

- 15.4.3.3 Inventory usage and management (e.g., consumables, contrast agents)

## **15.5 Customizable Reporting & Dashboards**

- 15.5.1 User-Defined Reports: - P2

- 15.5.1.1 Allow users (radiologists, administrators, department heads) to customize reports based on specific parameters (e.g., date range, modality, department, staff performance)

- 15.5.1.2 Predefined templates for common reports (e.g., monthly operational summary, patient wait time analysis)

- 15.5.2 Interactive Dashboards: - P2

- 15.5.2.1 Visualize KPIs and metrics in real-time (e.g., patient volume, modality utilization, TAT)

- 15.5.2.2 Interactive charts (e.g., bar graphs, pie charts, heat maps)

- 15.5.2.3 Drill-down functionality to view detailed data by technician, radiologist, or modality

- 15.5.3 Data Export & Sharing: - P2

- 15.5.3.1 Export reports in various formats (e.g., Excel, PDF, CSV) for analysis or sharing

- 15.5.3.2 Automated email delivery of key reports to relevant stakeholders (e.g., department heads, quality officers)

## **15.6 Advanced Analytics & Predictive Reporting**

- 15.6.1 Predictive Analytics: - P3

15.6.1.1 Forecast demand for imaging services based on historical data (e.g., volume trends, seasonal variations)

15.6.1.2 Predict equipment maintenance needs or breakdowns based on usage patterns

15.6.2 Machine Learning Integration: - P3

15.6.2.1 Use AI/ML to detect patterns (e.g., predict patient no-shows, identify high-risk areas for operational improvement)

15.6.2.2 Performance improvement suggestions based on historical data analysis

15.6.3 Clinical Outcomes Reporting: - P3

15.6.3.1 Correlate imaging results with patient outcomes (e.g., diagnostic accuracy, treatment outcomes, post-imaging patient care)

15.6.3.2 Report on missed diagnoses or delayed diagnoses linked to imaging

**15.7 Performance Reviews & Staff Analytics**

15.7.1 Technologist and Radiologist Reviews: - P2

15.7.1.1 Track and generate performance reports for each staff member

15.7.1.2 Include performance metrics such as accuracy, efficiency, and quality of work

15.7.1.3 Set goals and track performance improvements over time

15.7.2 Workforce Allocation: - P2

15.7.2.1 Analyze staffing levels and allocate resources based on workload (e.g., peak hours for certain modalities)

15.7.2.2 Track overtime and labor costs
