## [PHARMACY MODULE]

### 1 Drug Inventory Management

#### 1.1 Item Master Management: - P1

##### 1.1.1 Centralized Drug Catalog (Hospital Formulary approved by P&TC)

- 1.1.1.1 Drug name (generic + brand)
- 1.1.1.2 3 brands for one generic item
- 1.1.1.3 Dosage form (tablet, injection, syrup, etc.)
- 1.1.1.4 Strength (500mg, 1g, etc.)
- 1.1.1.5 Barcode
- 1.1.1.6 Storage requirements (cold chain, room temp, etc.)
- 1.1.1.7 Controlled substance flag
- 1.1.1.8 Consignment items
- 1.1.1.9 Restricted drugs list (Antibiotics like Ertapenem, Tiecoplanin etc. & Anticancer drugs etc. Approval by ID & Oncologist or Consultant only)
- 1.1.1.10 Drug brands PAR levels (min & max levels)

##### 1.1.2 Manufacturer & Vendor Linkage: - P3

- 1.1.2.1 Preferred vendors for each item /generic drug
- 1.1.2.2 Manufacturer information (Drug license + CGMP)
- 1.1.2.3 Vendor contract pricing +supply timeline+ expiries

#### 1.1.3 Stock Inward (Receiving)

##### 1.1.4 PO Generation: - P2

- 1.1.4.1 Generate PO on drug min PAR level
- 1.1.4.2 Manager Pharmacy edit/approve it
- 1.1.4.3 Review/Approved by Chief Pharmacist + MS
- 1.1.4.4 Sent to Purchase department to issue PO to approved vendor

##### 1.1.5 Purchase Order Integration: - P3

- 1.1.5.1 Match PO with received stock
- 1.1.5.2 Partial/complete delivery tracking
- 1.1.5.3 Auto-update of inventory upon Goods Receipt Note (GRN)

##### 1.1.6 Batch & Expiry Tracking: - P1

- 1.1.6.1 Batch number entry
- 1.1.6.2 Expiry date capture (mandatory)
- 1.1.6.3 MRP / cost per unit
- 1.1.6.4 Barcode generation on receipt

##### 1.1.7 Quality Check Workflow: - P2

- 1.1.7.1 Accept/reject items based on QC/Recall
- 1.1.7.2 Record reason for rejections
- 1.1.7.3 Recall drugs –Report to Health Authorities (DRAP/CDC)

#### 1.2 Stock Management

##### 1.2.1 Inventory by Location – P1

- 1.2.1.1 Central pharmacy store
  - 1.2.1.2 Departmental/sub-pharmacy stores
  - 1.2.1.3 ICU/OT/ward-specific stocks
- 1.2.2 Stock Transfer – P2**
  - 1.2.2.1 Internal transfers (main → sub-store)
  - 1.2.2.2 Transfer requests and approvals
  - 1.2.2.3 Transfer reject if expiry less than 6 months
  - 1.2.2.4 Real-time update of both locations
- 1.2.3 Stock Adjustment – P3**
  - 1.2.3.1 Manual/software adjustment entries
  - 1.2.3.2 Categories: Damaged, Expired, Lost, Theft, recall
  - 1.2.3.3 Audit trail and approval workflow
- 1.2.4 Stock Return – P2**
  - 1.2.4.1 Return to vendor (due to expiry, damage, recall etc.)
  - 1.2.4.2 Return from departments (unused/recall drugs)
  - 1.2.4.3 Track credits/debits with finance module
- 1.3 Dispensing-Based Stock Outward**
  - 1.3.1 OPD/IPD Dispensing Impact – P3**
    - 1.3.1.1 Automatic deduction on dispensing
    - 1.3.1.2 Real-time inventory update
  - 1.3.2 Stock Reservation – P3**
    - 1.3.2.1 For scheduled surgeries/procedures
    - 1.3.2.2 Hold stock for specific patients or units
  - 1.3.3 FIFO/LIFO (FEFO)Policy – P2**
    - 1.3.3.1 System-controlled stock issuing based on expiry or policy
- 1.4 Reorder Management**
  - 1.4.1 Stock Thresholds – P1**
    - 1.4.1.1 Minimum/maximum/reorder levels per item/location
    - 1.4.1.2 Safety stock alerts
  - 1.4.2 Automated Purchase Requests – P3**
    - 1.4.2.1 Trigger PR generation when stock falls below reorder level
    - 1.4.2.2 Multi-level approval workflows for PR
  - 1.4.3 Fast-Moving/Slow-Moving Analysis – P3**
    - 1.4.3.1 Insights for optimizing procurement and inventory holding
- 1.5 Expiry Management**
  - 1.5.1 Expiry Alerts – P1**
    - 1.5.1.1 Notifications for items nearing expiry
    - 1.5.1.2 Configurable time frame (e.g., 3 months before expiry)
  - 1.5.2 Expiry Handling – P2**

- 1.5.2.1 Blocking of expired stock from issue (check on batch no/expiry date)
- 1.5.2.2 Auto-flagging for return/disposal
- 1.5.2.3 Reports on upcoming expiries

## **2 Prescription Management**

### **2.1 Integration with Clinical Systems**

#### **2.1.1 Real-time sync of prescriptions from: - P3**

- 2.1.1.1 Outpatient (OPD) consultations
- 2.1.1.2 Inpatient (IPD) rounds
- 2.1.1.3 Emergency department
- 2.1.1.4 Daycare/specialty clinics

### **2.2 Prescription Entry & View**

#### **2.2.1 Electronic Prescription (eRx) Capture**

- 2.2.1.1 Doctor- enter the patient medical record no. MR#
- 2.2.1.2 Patient data showed-name, age, wt, height, gender
- 2.2.1.3 Doctor-entered orders (generic + brand)
- 2.2.1.4 Free-text + standard drug catalog entry
- 2.2.1.5 Dose, Frequency, dosage, route of administration
- 2.2.1.6 Duration (e.g., x days or until discontinued)
- 2.2.1.7 Instructions/comments (e.g., take after food)
- 2.2.1.8 Restricted drugs entry – view as red to doctor & pharmacist which needs approval before dispensing

#### **2.2.2 Prescription Viewer (for Pharmacists) – P2**

- 2.2.2.1 Separate dashboard for Emergency department patient medicines for priority dispensing
- 2.2.2.2 Chronological view of prescriptions per patient
- 2.2.2.3 Prescription intervention module- check button to suggest the dose adjustment etc
- 2.2.2.4 Filter by status: New / Partially dispensed / Dispensed / Cancelled/Restricted drugs/prescription intervention
- 2.2.2.5 Color-coding for priority (e.g., stat orders for admitted patients)

#### **2.2.3 Templates & Shortcuts – P3**

- 2.2.3.1 Refill orders button for long term therapies
- 2.2.3.2 Review Prescription intervention (accept/reject) to pharmacist/pharmacy

### **2.3 Drug Decision Support**

#### **2.3.1 Drug Interaction Alerts – P2**

- 2.3.1.1 Contraindications, duplicate therapies, allergies

#### **2.3.2 Dosage Checks – P2**

- 2.3.2.1 Age/weight-based dose validation (especially pediatrics)

#### **2.3.3 Formulary Compliance – P2**

- 2.3.3.1 Suggest alternatives if non-formulary drug prescribed

#### **2.3.4 Allergy Check – P2**

- 2.3.4.1 Automatic cross-reference with known allergies

## **2.4 Order Lifecycle Management**

### **2.4.1 Prescription Statuses – P2**

- 2.4.1.1 Draft / Finalized / Modified / Cancelled

### **2.4.2 Edit & Cancel Logic – P3**

- 2.4.2.1 Only permitted before dispensing (or with override/approval)
- 2.4.2.2 Audit log of all changes

### **2.4.3 Re-prescription / Repeat Orders – P2**

- 2.4.3.1 Auto-clone previous prescription
- 2.4.3.2 Mark drugs for long-term therapy

## **2.5 Dispensing Management**

### **2.5.1 Queue Management – P1**

- 2.5.1.1 OPD, IPD, and Emergency queues by time and priority

### **2.5.2 Partial Dispensing – P1**

- 2.5.2.1 Allow partial issue (due to stock shortage or patient preference)
- 2.5.2.2 Allow alternate brand (same generic) option on dispensing to avoid unfulfilled drug Rx
- 2.5.2.3 Record unfulfilled drugs

### **2.5.3 Real-time Inventory Check – P1**

- 2.5.3.1 Show stock status at pharmacist's end
- 2.5.3.2 Suggest alternatives if out-of-stock

## **2.6 Prescription to Billing Integration**

### **2.6.1 Charge Capture – P3**

- 2.6.1.1 Real-time billing upon dispensing
- 2.6.1.2 Real-time billing upon return
- 2.6.1.3 Differentiation: Paid / Credit / Insurance/return

### **2.6.2 Link to Patient Bill – P3**

- 2.6.2.1 Itemized drug charges with quantity, batch, and expiry
- 2.6.2.2 Itemized drug return with quantity, batch and expiry

## **2.7 Regulatory & Compliance Requirements**

### **2.7.1 Audit Trail – P3**

- 2.7.1.1 Track who entered, modified(intervene), approved, or cancelled each order

### **2.7.2 Controlled Substances – P3**

- 2.7.2.1 Tag and track narcotic/psychotropic prescriptions separately
- 2.7.2.2 Mandatory dual-authentication for such drugs (optional)

### **2.7.3 Prescription Printout – P2**

- 2.7.3.1 Printable formats (for patients or manual fallback)
- 2.7.3.2 QR code/barcode for digital verification

# **3 Dispensing Workflow**

## **3.1 Prescription Retrieval & Validation**

### **3.1.1 Source Integration – P2**

- 3.1.1.1 Pull finalized prescriptions from EMR/EHR

- 3.1.1.2 Support OPD, IPD, emergency, and day care prescriptions

### 3.1.2 **Filtering & Queuing – P3**

- 3.1.2.1 Auto-sorting prescriptions based on:

- 3.1.2.1.1 Patient type (OPD/IPD)

- 3.1.2.1.2 Urgency (STAT, routine)

- 3.1.2.1.3 Department/ward

### 3.1.3 **Validation Checks – P2**

- 3.1.3.1 Check for:

- 3.1.3.1.1 Prescription status (approved/finalized)

- 3.1.3.1.2 Patient details (ID, age, wt, allergies)

- 3.1.3.1.3 Doctor authorization

- 3.1.3.2 Highlight potential issues (e.g., allergy conflicts, duplicate therapy)

## 3.2 **Inventory Check**

- Real-time stock check at dispensing location – P1

- 3.2.1.1 Item availability, batch number, expiry date

- 3.2.1.2 Flag items with:

- 3.2.1.2.1 Near expiry

- 3.2.1.2.2 Quarantined or expired

- 3.2.1.2.3 Alternative suggestions (if original drug is unavailable)

## 3.3 **Dispensing Queue Management**

### 3.3.1 **Queue System – P2**

- 3.3.1.1 Multi-window support (e.g., OPD counter, IPD, ER)

- 3.3.1.2 FIFO dispensing or priority-based for emergency

- 3.3.1.3 Token-based or patient ID-based tracking

### 3.3.2 **User Dashboard – P2**

- 3.3.2.1 Pharmacist dashboard showing:

- 3.3.2.1.1 Pending prescriptions

- 3.3.2.1.2 Patient wait time

- 3.3.2.1.3 Prescription status (new, in-progress, dispensed, refill)

## 3.4 **Preparation and Verification**

### 3.4.1 **Labeling – P1**

- 3.4.1.1 Auto-generate and print:

- 3.4.1.1.1 Patient details

- 3.4.1.1.2 Drug name (generic+ brand), dosage, batch, expiry

- 3.4.1.1.3 Instructions (from prescription)

- 3.4.1.1.4 High alert, LASA, storage conditions

- 3.4.1.1.5 Dilution

- 3.4.1.1.6 Dispensed by sign., filled by sign.

- 3.4.1.1.7 Barcode/QR code

### 3.4.2 **Double-Check Protocol – P1**

3.4.2.1 Option for dual-verification (technician + pharmacist)

3.4.2.2 Check for correct:

3.4.2.2.1 Drug

3.4.2.2.2 Dose

3.4.2.2.3 Route

3.4.2.2.4 Frequency

3.4.2.2.5 Duration

3.4.2.2.6 Quantity

### **3.5 Partial / Complete Dispensing**

#### **3.5.1 Partial Dispensing Support – P3**

3.5.1.1 Allow issue of available quantity

3.5.1.2 Log remaining undispensed items

3.5.1.3 Auto-flag for next refill or follow-up

#### **3.5.2 Controlled Drug Handling – P1**

3.5.2.1 Dual authentication if configured

3.5.2.2 Logbook entry + digital record

#### **3.5.3 Dispense by Batch – P2**

3.5.3.1 Auto-select based on FIFO or shortest expiry

3.5.3.2 Allow override with justification (Rx orders dispensing within 72hrs)

### **3.6 Patient Handover / Ward Transfer**

#### **3.6.1 OPD/Day Care – P1**

3.6.1.1 Direct handover with patient acknowledgment

3.6.1.2 Optional digital or biometric signature

#### **3.6.2 IPD/Ward Patients – P1**

3.6.2.1 Handover to ward nurse (linked to administration log)

3.6.2.2 Integration with Medication Administration Record (MAR)

### **3.7 Billing Integration**

#### **3.7.1 Real-Time Billing – P2**

3.7.1.1 Drugs auto-pushed to billing system post-dispensing

3.7.1.2 Itemized details (name, quantity, rate, tax)

#### **3.7.2 Billing Status Visibility – P2**

3.7.2.1 Paid/ Credit / Insurance linked

3.7.2.2 Blocking/alert for unapproved/unauthorized credit

### **3.8 Returns Management**

#### **3.8.1 Allow returns of unused/partially used meds from: - P2**

3.8.1.1 Patients (with patient billing adjustment)

3.8.1.2 Wards (IPD drug return)

3.8.1.3 Update inventory on return approval – P2

3.8.1.4 Flag damaged/opened items separately – P2

## **4 Purchase and Supplier Management**

### **4.1 Supplier (Vendor) Master Management**

**4.1.1 Vendor Database – P3**

- 4.1.1.1 Vendor name, contact details, email, GSTIN/PAN
- 4.1.1.2 Drug license and registration details
- 4.1.1.3 Vendor category (Local / National / Govt. supplier / Direct company)

**4.1.2 Vendor Classification – P3**

- 4.1.2.1 Preferred vs. backup vendors
- 4.1.2.2 Vendor rating system (based on delivery time, price, quality)

**4.1.3 Document Management – P3**

- 4.1.3.1 License upload, compliance docs, contracts
- 4.1.3.2 Alert on license/contract expiry/Filer, Non filer status-Tax)

**4.2 Drug Purchase Requisition (PR)****4.2.1 PR Generation Triggers – P2**

- 4.2.1.1 Manual requisition by store manager/pharmacist
- 4.2.1.2 Auto-triggered based on:
  - 4.2.1.2.1 Reorder level
  - 4.2.1.2.2 Consumption rate
  - 4.2.1.2.3 Minimum stock threshold

**4.2.2 Requisition Details – P2**

- 4.2.2.1 Drug name (generic + brand), strength, dosage form
- 4.2.2.2 Required quantity
- 4.2.2.3 Priority level (Routine / Urgent)

**4.2.3 Approval Workflow – P2**

- 4.2.3.1 Configurable multi-level approval based on drug type or value
- 4.2.3.2 Requisition tracking (pending, approved, rejected)

**4.3 Purchase Order (PO) Management****4.3.1 PO Creation – P2**

- 4.3.1.1 Auto-create from approved PR
- 4.3.1.2 Manual PO with direct vendor selection

**4.3.2 PO Details – P2**

- 4.3.2.1 Vendor, items, quantity, rate, taxes
- 4.3.2.2 Delivery location, terms & conditions, delivery schedule

**4.3.3 PO Approval Workflow – P2**

- 4.3.3.1 Authorization based on total PO value or user roles

**4.3.4 PO Status Tracking – P2**

- 4.3.4.1 PO sent / partially fulfilled / completed / closed / canceled

**4.4 Goods Receipt Note (GRN) / Stock Inward****4.4.1 Receiving Against PO – P2**

- 4.4.1.1 Match received quantity vs. ordered quantity
- 4.4.1.2 Batch number, expiry date, MRP, rate entry
- 4.4.1.3 Barcode/RFID tag generation (if applicable)

**4.4.2 Quality Check Workflow – P2**

4.4.2.1 Accept / reject partial delivery

4.4.2.2 Hold/Quarantine non-compliant drugs

#### **4.4.3 GRN Approval -P2**

4.4.3.1 Record discrepancies and reasons

4.4.3.2 Link GRN with invoice for finance handover

### **4.5 Invoice & Payment Integration**

#### **4.5.1 Invoice Matching – P3**

4.5.1.1 PO → GRN → Invoice linkage

4.5.1.2 Capture invoice number, amount, date

#### **4.5.2 Finance System Integration – P3**

4.5.2.1 Push approved invoice to finance/accounts

4.5.2.2 Tax details (GST, VAT)

#### **4.5.3 Payment Tracking –P3**

4.5.3.1 Mark payment status (Pending / Paid / On Hold/Partial)

### **4.6 Returns & Rejections Management**

#### **4.6.1 Return to Vendor Workflow – P3**

4.6.1.1 Due to excess quantity, expired, damaged, or QC failure/Recall

#### **4.6.2 Credit Note Tracking – P3**

4.6.2.1 Against returned goods or future adjustment

#### **4.6.3 Supplier Performance Analytics**

4.6.3.1 Vendor rating dashboard: - P3

- Delivery timelines
- Quality issues
- Pricing trend

4.6.3.2 Blacklist / suspend vendor – P3

4.6.3.3 Monthly and quarterly performance reviews – P3

## **5 Billing Integration**

### **5.1 Real-Time Integration with Billing System**

#### **5.1.1 Automatic sync of drug charges with the hospital's central billing system at the moment of: - P3**

5.1.1.1 Dispensing (OPD)

5.1.1.2 Drug issue to ward/patient (IPD)

5.1.1.3 Emergency dispensing

#### **5.1.2 Zero-latency update to ensure that patient bill reflects latest pharmacy charges – P3**

### **5.2 Patient Type-Specific Billing Logic**

#### **5.2.1 OPD Patients – P3**

5.2.1.1 Immediate charge posting to patient account

5.2.1.2 Option for cash/card payment before dispensing

#### **5.2.2 IPD Patients – P3**

- 5.2.2.1 Charges posted to patient's running account (bed-side billing)
    - 5.2.2.2 Linked to admission number and episode ID
  - 5.2.3 Emergency Patients – P3**
    - 5.2.3.1 Fast-track dispensing with deferred billing (based on hospital policy)
  - 5.2.4 Daycare/Surgery Units – P3**
    - 5.2.4.1 Package vs. itemized billing control
  - 5.3 Payment Mode and Pricing Logic**
    - 5.3.1 Support for multiple billing modes: - P3**
      - 5.3.1.1 Cash
      - 5.3.1.2 Credit
      - 5.3.1.3 Insurance
      - 5.3.1.4 Government schemes
    - 5.3.2 Dynamic Pricing Support – P3**
      - 5.3.2.1 Pricing variation based on:
        - 5.3.2.1.1 Payer type (cash vs. insurance)
        - 5.3.2.1.2 In-house vs. outsourced pharmacy
        - 5.3.2.1.3 Generic vs. branded drug
  - 5.4 Discounts and Approvals**
    - 5.4.1 Configurable Discount Types – P3**
      - 5.4.1.1 Staff discount
      - 5.4.1.2 Senior citizen discount
      - 5.4.1.3 Special approvals (e.g., charity, doctor-authorized)
    - 5.4.2 Approval Workflow – P3**
      - 5.4.2.1 Define thresholds and roles for discount approval
      - 5.4.2.2 Digital audit trail of who applied and approved
  - 5.5 Return and Refund Handling**
    - 5.5.1 Return from Patient – P3**
      - 5.5.1.1 Update billing: Adjust/refund based on item condition and policy
      - 5.5.1.2 Auto-credit to patient account
    - 5.5.2 Return from Ward (IPD) – P3**
      - 5.5.2.1 Reverse entry in patient bill
      - 5.5.2.2 Trigger notification to nursing or billing team
  - 5.6 Billing Adjustment for Package Plans**
    - 5.6.1 Recognize drugs included in procedure or surgery packages – P3**
    - 5.6.2 Auto-apply package billing logic: - P3**
      - 5.6.2.1 Hide itemized billing for covered items
      - 5.6.2.2 Bill only excluded drugs or excess quantity
- 
- 6 Expiry and Batch Management**
  - 6.1 Batch Management at Stock Entry**
    - 6.1.1 Mandatory Batch Details During Stock Inward (GRN) – P1**

- 6.1.1.1 Batch number
    - 6.1.1.2 Manufacturing date
    - 6.1.1.3 Expiry date
    - 6.1.1.4 MRP, cost price
    - 6.1.1.5 Vendor/manufacturer details
  - 6.1.2 Multiple Batches Per Drug – P1**
    - 6.1.2.1 Enable handling of multiple active batches of the same drug in inventory
    - 6.1.2.2 Auto-prioritize based on expiry (FIFO)
  - 6.1.3 Auto Barcode/QR Generation – P2**
    - 6.1.3.1 Batch-linked barcode for tracking and scanning
    - 6.1.3.2 Integration with dispensing and return workflows
  - 6.2 Expiry Tracking and Monitoring**
    - 6.2.1 Expiry Alerts – P1**
      - 6.2.1.1 System alerts for:
        - 6.2.1.1.1 Drugs nearing expiry (configurable window, e.g., 90, 60, 30 days)
        - 6.2.1.1.2 Expired drugs in active stock
    - 6.2.2 Batch Locking – P2**
      - 6.2.2.1 Auto-block expired batches from:
      - 6.2.2.2 Dispensing
        - 6.2.2.2.1 Transfers
        - 6.2.2.2.2 Returns to patient/ward
  - 6.2.3 Quarantine Mechanism – P2**
    - 6.2.3.1 Expired/near-expiry drugs moved to a separate quarantine status (non-issuable)
- 6.3 Stock Issue Based on Expiry (FIFO)**
  - 6.3.1 FEFO Logic - P2**
    - 6.3.1.1 System auto-selects the **First Expiry, First Out** batch for dispensing
    - 6.3.1.2 Override option with justification and access control
  - 6.3.2 Batch Selection During Dispensing – P2**
    - 6.3.2.1 Allow manual selection if multiple batches are available
    - 6.3.2.2 Show expiry date during batch selection to alert pharmacist
- 6.4 Expiry Reports and Logs**
  - 6.4.1 Upcoming Expiry Report – P1**
    - 6.4.1.1 Drugs expiring within a set timeframe (e.g., next 3 months)
    - 6.4.1.2 By department, location, category
  - 6.4.2 Expired Drug Report – P1**
    - 6.4.2.1 Items expired but not yet returned/disposed
  - 6.4.3 Batch-Wise Stock Report – P1**
    - 6.4.3.1 Current stock by batch, expiry, location
- 6.5 Expired Stock Disposal Workflow**
  - 6.5.1 Return to Vendor (If Applicable) – P2**

- 6.5.1.1 Capture return notes, batch, and expiry
- 6.5.1.2 Track credit note or replacement receipt

## **6.5.2 In-House Disposal – P2**

- 6.5.2.1 Capture disposal approval
- 6.5.2.2 Record method of disposal (e.g., incineration)
- 6.5.2.3 Maintain disposal certificate logs

## **6.5.3 Audit Trail – P2**

- 6.5.3.1 Who marked the stock expired
- 6.5.3.2 Who approved return/disposal
- 6.5.3.3 Timestamped entries

# **6.6 Batch-Wise Inventory Valuation**

## **6.6.1 Maintain separate valuation per batch for: - P3**

- 6.6.1.1 FIFO / LIFO / Weighted Average costing
- 6.6.1.2 Finance module reporting
- 6.6.1.3 Stock audit and reconciliation

# **6.7 Returns & Expiry Adjustment**

## **6.7.1 Patient Returns – P2**

- 6.7.1.1 Allow return only if batch is not expired
- 6.7.1.2 Expiry validation before refund

## **6.7.2 Ward Returns – P2**

- 6.7.2.1 Similar check during IPD stock return from wards

# **7 Formulary & Drug Catalog Management**

## **7.1 Central Drug Master (Catalog)**

### **7.1.1 Standardized Drug Information Fields – P1**

- 7.1.1.1 Generic name
- 7.1.1.2 Brand name(s)
- 7.1.1.3 Strength (e.g., 500 mg)
- 7.1.1.4 Dosage form (tablet, injection, syrup, etc.)
- 7.1.1.5 Route of administration (oral, IV, topical, etc.)
- 7.1.1.6 Drug code / SKU / HSN code
- 7.1.1.7 ATC classification / therapeutic class
- 7.1.1.8 Pharmacological category

### **7.1.2 Multiple UOMs (Units of Measure) – P1**

- 7.1.2.1 Tablet, strip, bottle, vial, ampoule, drips, cap, tubes, surgical items
- 7.1.2.2 UOM conversion logic for billing and inventory

## **7.2 Hospital Formulary Management**

### **7.2.1 Formulary Drug List – P1**

7.2.1.1 Flag drugs as:

7.2.1.1.1 Formulary

7.2.1.1.2 Non-formulary

7.2.1.1.3 Restricted (requires special approval)

**7.2.2 Department-Specific Formularies - P1**

7.2.2.1 Custom lists for ICU, Pediatrics, Oncology, etc.

7.2.2.2 Filter drugs based on specialty needs

**7.2.3 Formulary Versioning – P2**

7.2.3.1 Maintain historical changes and updates

7.2.3.2 Track drugs added, removed, or modified

**7.2.4 Drug Equivalence Mapping – P2**

7.2.4.1 Map brand names to generic equivalents

7.2.4.2 Suggest alternatives if prescribed drug is non-formulary

**7.3 Clinical and Safety Information**

**7.3.1 Drug Monograph Integration – P3**

7.3.1.1 Description, indications, contraindications

7.3.1.2 Dosage and administration guidelines

7.3.1.3 Side effects, interactions, precautions

**7.3.2 Allergy & Alert Settings –P3**

7.3.2.1 Flag drugs for known patient allergies

7.3.2.2 Black box warnings and clinical alerts

**8 Integration Requirements**

**8.1 Integration with the Electronic Medical Record (EMR)**

**8.1.1 Prescription Order Interface – P2**

8.1.1.1 Enable **doctors** to prescribe drugs directly from the **formulary** within the EMR.

8.1.1.2 Automatic transfer of prescriptions from the EMR to the pharmacy module for dispensing.

8.1.1.3 Flag non-formulary drugs or restricted drugs during prescription.

8.1.1.4 Real-time **drug allergy checks** against patient data.

**8.1.2 Patient Profile Sync – P2**

8.1.2.1 Retrieve patient demographics, medical history, and current medications.

8.1.2.2 Ensure **medication reconciliation**: Cross-check prescribed drugs with patient's current drug list to avoid duplications, interactions, or allergies.

**8.1.3 Clinical Decision Support System (CDSS) Integration – P2**

8.1.3.1 Alert for drug interactions, contraindications, and renal/hepatic adjustments when prescribing or dispensing drugs.

**8.2 Integration with Inventory Management Module**

**8.2.1 Stock Updates – P2**

8.2.1.1 Sync inventory levels in real-time. When a drug is dispensed, stock levels are immediately updated in the inventory.

8.2.1.2 Alert when stock falls below the reorder level, triggering automatic purchase order creation or manual review.

#### **8.2.2 Batch and Expiry Management – P1**

8.2.2.1 Track drug batches and expiry dates, syncing them with the inventory system.

8.2.2.2 Alerts for near-expiry drugs and expiration of critical stock.

#### **8.2.3 Stock Transfer Between Pharmacy Locations – P1**

8.2.3.1 If the hospital operates multiple pharmacy locations (main, ward, ICU), enable **real-time stock transfer** between locations, with an automatic update in the inventory system.

#### **8.2.4 Pharmacy Stock Replenishment – P2**

8.2.4.1 Integration with inventory to monitor **stock levels** and automatically trigger purchase orders when stock reaches a predefined threshold.

### **8.3 Integration with Billing / Finance Module**

#### **8.3.1 Drug Billing – P3**

8.3.1.1 **Real-time integration** between the pharmacy module and the billing system for automatic posting of drug charges to patient accounts.

8.3.1.2 Track patient type (e.g., OPD, IPD, Emergency) and adjust billing accordingly, whether it's a full charge, insurance claim, or government-sponsored.

#### **8.3.2 Price Management – P3**

8.3.2.1 Sync pricing information from the pharmacy catalog (MRP, discount, insurance, etc.) with the billing module.

8.3.2.2 Ensure correct pricing for branded and generic versions of drugs.

#### **8.3.3 Insurance – P3**

8.3.3.1 Link drug charges with patient insurance records for automatic **insurance claim generation**.

#### **8.3.4 Refund & Return Management – P3**

8.3.4.1 Sync the return of medications (from patient or ward) with the billing system to ensure accurate **credit note issuance or adjustments** in the patient's bill.

### **8.4 Integration with Purchase & Supplier Management Module**

#### **8.4.1 Supplier Data Sync – P3**

8.4.1.1 Maintain an up-to-date list of **suppliers/vendors** and their **contact details**, including delivery times, lead times, and contract terms.

#### **8.4.2 Purchase Orders (PO) – P3**

8.4.2.1 Generate **purchase orders** directly from the pharmacy system based on stock levels, usage rates, and reorder thresholds.

8.4.2.2 Sync approved POs with the purchasing module to facilitate order tracking and invoicing.

#### **8.4.3 Goods Receipt and Inventory Update – P3**

- 8.4.3.1 On the receipt of drugs, automatically update the **inventory** and link received goods with **purchase order records**.
- 8.4.4 **Vendor Performance Management – P3**
  - 8.4.4.1 Track **delivery timelines, quality of drugs, and discrepancies** in received items. This can be used to evaluate supplier performance and make data-driven decisions about reordering.
- 8.5 **Integration with Laboratory and Radiology Modules**
  - 8.5.1 **Medication for Lab/Procedure Orders – P2**
    - 8.5.1.1 Automatically synchronize drug orders for tests or treatments prescribed in the **Lab and Radiology modules**.
    - 8.5.1.2 For example, drugs needed for contrast agents in radiology or pre/post-procedure medication in the lab can be automatically generated as part of the procedure order.
  - 8.5.2 **Clinical Pathways / Protocols Integration – P3**
    - 8.5.2.1 Drugs linked to **specific clinical pathways or protocols** are updated and synchronized between the pharmacy and clinical departments to ensure accurate medication administration.
- 8.6 **Integration with Patient Administration / Registration Module**
  - 8.6.1 **Patient Demographic Sync – P2**
    - 8.6.1.1 Automatically pull patient demographics (e.g., ID, contact, insurance details) and synchronize with the pharmacy system to **link prescriptions** to patient records.
  - 8.6.2 **Admission / Discharge Notifications – P2**
    - 8.6.2.1 Sync pharmacy records with **patient admission and discharge** information to track medications during hospital stays and ensure discharge prescriptions are accurately generated.
  - 8.6.3 **Ward/Bed Information – P2**
    - 8.6.3.1 Enable automatic medication orders from the pharmacy module based on **ward and bed assignments** for inpatients.

## 9 Medicine Storage Conditions

- 9.1 **Medicine Storage Condition Mapping in Drug Master**
  - 9.1.1 **Mandatory Fields in Drug Catalog: - P1**
    - 9.1.1.1 Storage temperature range (e.g., 2–8°C, 15–25°C)
    - 9.1.1.2 Humidity control requirement
    - 9.1.1.3 Light sensitivity (e.g., store in a dark place)
    - 9.1.1.4 Freezing restrictions (e.g., "Do not freeze")
    - 9.1.1.5 Flammable / hazardous tags
    - 9.1.1.6 Special instructions (e.g., store upright, avoid shaking)
  - 9.1.2 **Storage Type Classification: - P1**

- 9.1.2.1 Room temperature
- 9.1.2.2 Refrigerated
- 9.1.2.3 Freezer
- 9.1.2.4 Controlled substances cabinet
- 9.1.2.5 Cytotoxic drug cabinet
- 9.1.2.6 Narcotic vaults

#### **9.1.3 Flagging of Special-Handling Drugs: - P1**

- 9.1.3.1 Vaccines, biologics, insulins, chemotherapeutics, etc.

### **9.2 Storage Location Management**

#### **9.2.1 Link Drugs to Physical Storage Locations: - P1**

- 9.2.1.1 Main pharmacy store
- 9.2.1.2 Cold storage unit
- 9.2.1.3 Ward pharmacy cabinets
- 9.2.1.4 Emergency trolleys
- 9.2.1.5 ICU medication fridges

#### **9.2.2 Multi-location Storage Tracking: - P1**

- 9.2.2.1 Keep inventory counts and conditions tracked per location
- 9.2.2.2 Identify non-compliance in remote or ward-based stores

### **9.3 Alerts & Notifications**

#### **9.3.1 Deviation Alerts: - P2**

- 9.3.1.1 Real-time notification to pharmacy/store in-charge when:
  - 9.3.1.1.1 Temperature exceeds storage range
  - 9.3.1.1.2 Fridge door left open
  - 9.3.1.1.3 Power outage in refrigeration units
  - 9.3.1.1.4 **Escalation Matrix: - P2**
- 9.3.1.2 Configurable escalation to next-level staff if alert is not resolved in a defined time

### **9.4 Inventory Movement Based on Storage Conditions**

#### **9.4.1 Storage-Specific Batching: P3**

- 9.4.1.1 Restrict movement of refrigerated drugs only to appropriate cold-chain-enabled locations
- 9.4.1.2 Alert if an attempt is made to transfer a cold-chain item to a non-compliant store

#### **9.4.2 Cold Chain Integrity During Issue: - P2**

- 9.4.2.1 Ensure delivery/transfer notes include:
  - 9.4.2.1.1 Time of removal from cold storage
  - 9.4.2.1.2 Expected time of delivery
  - 9.4.2.1.3 Condition upon receipt

### **9.5 Expiry Risk and Quality Assurance**

#### **9.5.1 Stability Monitoring: - P3**

9.5.1.1 Flag batches that have experienced a **temperature excursion**

9.5.1.2 Prevent dispensing of compromised drugs until QA clearance

**9.5.2 Return Handling: - P3**

9.5.2.1 Record storage condition compliance at the time of return (e.g., from wards)

9.5.2.2 Segregate returns that may have been exposed to incorrect storage conditions

## [HRM MODULE]

### 1. Employee Management

#### 1.1 Employee Information Management: P1

- 1.1.1 Personal details (name, contact, address, emergency contact)
- 1.1.2 Professional details (designation, department, qualifications)
- 1.1.3 Employment type (permanent, contractual, on-call)
- 1.1.4 Identity documentation (CNIC, licenses, certifications)
- 1.1.5 Signed Job Descriptions
- 1.1.6 Photograph and digital signature storage
- 1.1.7 Departmental Mapping:
  - 1.1.7.1 Role-based assignment (e.g., Nurse, Doctor, Admin Staff)
  - 1.1.7.2 Department (e.g., Surgery, Cardiology, IT, Housekeeping)
  - 1.1.7.3 Reporting hierarchy and supervisors
- 1.1.8 Disciplinary back ground (record of any disciplinary action taken in the past, if any)
- 1.1.9 Record of health status of the employee

#### 1.2 Employee Lifecycle Management: P1

- 1.2.1 Recruitment tracking and onboarding workflows
- 1.2.2 Probation period tracking and confirmation
- 1.2.3 Role and responsibility assignment
- 1.2.4 Transfer, promotion, demotion, and resignation tracking
- 1.2.5 Exit process with clearance documentation

#### 1.3 Attendance & Shift Management: P1

- 1.3.1 Biometric based attendance integration
- 1.3.2 Shift scheduling (morning, evening, night)
- 1.3.3 Shift rotation and planning
- 1.3.4 Absenteeism tracking
- 1.3.5 Leave management (casual, sick, annual, maternity, etc.)

#### 1.4 Payroll Integration: P1

- 1.4.1 Salary structure management
- 1.4.2 Tax and statutory deduction configuration
- 1.4.3 Overtime, bonus, and incentive calculation
- 1.4.4 Salary slips generation
- 1.4.5 Bank transfer/Direct deposit integration

#### 1.5 Training and Development: P1

- 1.5.1 Training schedule and participation tracking
- 1.5.2 Certification and license renewals
- 1.5.3 Skill matrix and performance tracking
- 1.5.4 Feedback and evaluation mechanisms

#### 1.6 Performance Management: P1

- 1.6.1 Appraisal cycles and KPIs
- 1.6.2 Goal setting and review tracking

- 1.6.3 Feedback (360-degree, peer, manager)
- 1.6.4 Documentation of disciplinary actions, warnings, or commendations

## **1.7 Document Management: P1**

- 1.7.1 Store and manage employee-related documents securely
- 1.7.2 Auto-alerts for expiring licenses or documents
- 1.7.3 Controlled access to sensitive documents

## **1.8 Compliance and Regulatory Requirements: P1**

- 1.8.1 Ensure compliance with labor laws and healthcare regulations
- 1.8.2 Defined process exist to validate the credentials of professionals
- 1.8.3 Evidence of verification of documents from the primary source like regulatory councils/universities and or institution etc.
- 1.8.4 Registrations/certifications of all the practitioners with the professional council or body are valid

# **2 Recruitment & Onboarding**

## **2.1 Job Requisition & Approval Workflow: P1**

- 2.1.1 Department-level job requisition initiation
- 2.1.2 Position details (role, qualifications, department, number of vacancies)
- 2.1.3 Automated approval hierarchy (HOD → HR → Admin)
- 2.1.4 Budget check and justification workflow

## **2.2 Job Posting & Applicant Sourcing: P1**

- 2.2.1 Integration with hospital website/career page
- 2.2.2 Multi-channel job posting (job portals, internal bulletin, social media)
- 2.2.3 Internal job posting for internal movement/promotion
- 2.2.4 Status tracking of job postings

## **2.3 Applicant Tracking System (ATS): P1**

- 2.3.1 Centralized candidate database
- 2.3.2 Resume parsing and profile creation
- 2.3.3 Applicant screening, shortlisting, and tagging
- 2.3.4 Interview scheduling and communication
- 2.3.5 Stage-wise tracking (Applied → Shortlisted → Interviewed → Selected)

## **2.4 Interview Management: P1**

- 2.4.1 Interview panel assignment (Doctor, HR, Admin)
- 2.4.2 Interview round scheduling (Telephonic, Technical, Final)
- 2.4.3 Feedback forms and rating matrix
- 2.4.4 Interview outcome documentation
- 2.4.5 Notification/reminders to panel members

## **2.5 Selection & Offer Management: P1**

- 2.5.1 Selection status updates
- 2.5.2 Offer letter generation (auto-template)
- 2.5.3 Approval-based offer release
- 2.5.4 Salary and benefits proposal
- 2.5.5 Candidate offer acceptance and joining date capture

## **2.6 Background Verification & Compliance: P1**

- 2.6.1 Reference checks and employment history

- 2.6.2 Education and certification verification
- 2.6.3 License validation for clinical staff (e.g., medical council registration)
- 2.6.4 Document uploads (ID proof, certificates, previous employment letters)

## **2.7 Onboarding Workflow: P1**

- 2.7.1 Digital joining form
- 2.7.2 Welcome email and joining kit assignment
- 2.7.3 Induction session scheduling (HR, Admin, IT, Departmental)
- 2.7.4 Allocation of employee code, ID card, login credentials
- 2.7.5 Policy acceptance tracking (leave, confidentiality, dress code, etc.)

# **3 Attendance & Leave Management**

## **3.1 Employee Attendance Tracking: P1**

- 3.1.1 Integration with Biometric/Face Recognition systems
- 3.1.2 Real-time sync of clock-in/clock-out data
- 3.1.3 Manual attendance entry (with authorization workflow)
- 3.1.4 Attendance exception handling (missed punches, manual overrides)
- 3.1.5 Holiday and weekend recognition per staff category

## **3.2 Shift Management: P1**

- 3.2.1 Multiple shift configurations (Morning, Evening, Night, Rotational)
- 3.2.2 Department-wise shift assignment
- 3.2.3 Auto shift roster generation and publishing
- 3.2.4 Shift swap and override with supervisor approval
- 3.2.5 Nurse and doctor-specific shift scheduling support

## **3.3 Leave Management System: P1**

- 3.3.1 Customizable leave types (Casual, Sick, Earned, Maternity, Paternity, etc.)
- 3.3.2 Leave eligibility based on role, tenure, and policy
- 3.3.3 Online leave application with approval workflow
- 3.3.4 Leave balance tracking and accrual automation
- 3.3.5 Compensatory off (comp-off) request and approval

## **3.4 Policy Configuration: P1**

- 3.4.1 Organization-specific rules for attendance and leave
  - 3.4.1.1 Late coming/early going thresholds
  - 3.4.1.2 Half-day/full-day rules
  - 3.4.1.3 Sandwich rule (leave + holiday handling)
- 3.4.2 Leave encashment and carry-forward rules
- 3.4.3 Grace time settings and absence auto-marking

## **3.5 Alerts and Notifications: P1**

- 3.5.1 Notifications for:
  - 3.5.1.1 Leave approval/rejection
  - 3.5.1.2 Upcoming leave balance low
  - 3.5.1.3 Missed punch alerts
- 3.5.2 SMS/Email integration for shift reminders and alerts

## **3.6 Integration with Payroll & HR: P1**

- 3.6.1 Sync attendance and leave data for payroll processing

- 3.6.2 Deduction logic for absenteeism or leave without pay
- 3.6.3 Real-time leave balance sync for employee portal
- 3.6.4 Attendance and leave mapping for performance and compliance tracking

### **3.7 Mobile & Self-Service Access: P1**

- 3.7.1 Employee portal/mobile app to:
  - 3.7.1.1 View shift roster
  - 3.7.1.2 Mark attendance (if geo-fencing enabled)
  - 3.7.1.3 Apply/cancel leave
  - 3.7.1.4 View leave balances
- 3.7.2 Supervisor dashboard for team attendance and leave status

### **3.8 Audit & Compliance: P1**

- 3.8.1 Full audit trail of attendance modifications
- 3.8.2 Medical staff compliance reporting for regulatory bodies
- 3.8.3 Auto backup and secure storage of attendance logs
- 3.8.4 Data retention as per local labor and health department laws

## **4 Payroll & Compensation**

### **4.1 Employee Payroll Setup: P1**

- 4.1.1 Define salary structure templates (fixed + variable components)
- 4.1.2 Grade/position-based salary mapping
- 4.1.3 Bank account and payment method capture
- 4.1.4 Applicable tax regimes and exemptions (based on employee type and location)
- 4.1.5 Integration with Employee Master Data (designation, department, shift, etc.)

### **4.2 Earnings & Deductions Configuration: P1**

- 4.2.1 Fixed components:
  - 4.2.1.1 Basic Pay, DA, Medical Allowance, Special Pay etc.
- 4.2.2 Variable/incentive components:
  - 4.2.2.1 Overtime, Night Duty Allowance, On-call duty
  - 4.2.2.2 Performance Bonuses, Holiday Work Pay etc.
- 4.2.3 Deductions:
  - 4.2.3.1 Tax (TDS), Provident Fund (PF), Professional Tax etc.
  - 4.2.3.2 Loan/advance repayments
  - 4.2.3.3 Leave without Pay (LWP) deductions

### **4.3 Automated Payroll Processing: P1**

- 4.3.1 Monthly payroll processing based on:
  - 4.3.1.1 Attendance & leave records
  - 4.3.1.2 Shift differentials
  - 4.3.1.3 Extra duty hours
- 4.3.2 Support for different payroll cycles (monthly, bi-weekly)
- 4.3.3 Auto-calculation of gross, net salary, and deductions
- 4.3.4 Handling of arrears, reimbursements, and adjustments

### **4.4 Leave & Attendance Integration: P1**

- 4.4.1 Real-time sync with attendance/leave modules
- 4.4.2 LOP (Loss of Pay) deductions auto-applied

- 4.4.3 Overtime and compensatory off considered in salary

#### **4.5 Disbursement & Integration: P1**

- 4.5.1 Bank statement/bulk salary transfer file generation
- 4.5.2 Direct integration with bank payment gateways (if supported)
- 4.5.3 Pay slip generation (PDF/online/mobile access)
- 4.5.4 Integration with accounting/finance module for ledger posting

#### **4.6 Loans, Advances & Reimbursements: P1**

- 4.6.1 Loan/advance request and approval workflows
- 4.6.2 EMI auto-deduction configuration
- 4.6.3 Reimbursement claims (travel, uniform, medical) processing and payout
- 4.6.4 Settlement calculation during resignation/retirement/termination

#### **4.7 Final Settlement (Full & Final - FnF): P1**

- 4.7.1 Auto-calculation of:
  - 4.7.1.1 Notice period pay
  - 4.7.1.2 Leave encashment
  - 4.7.1.3 Pending salary
  - 4.7.1.4 Recoveries (asset loss, penalties)
- 4.7.2 Approval and clearance workflows
- 4.7.3 FnF report and payslip generation

### **5 Performance Management**

#### **5.1 Goal Setting & Alignment: P1**

- 5.1.1 Role-based KPI templates (for doctors, nurses, admin, paramedics, etc.)
- 5.1.2 Individual and departmental goal setting
- 5.1.3 Goal alignment with hospital objectives (e.g., patient satisfaction, infection control rates)
- 5.1.4 Goal library with customizable metrics
- 5.1.5 Goal weightage and scoring configuration

#### **5.2 Performance Appraisal Workflow: P1**

- 5.2.1 Well defined performance appraisal system
- 5.2.2 Support for different appraisal types:
  - 5.2.2.1 Annual
  - 5.2.2.2 Mid-year/Quarterly
  - 5.2.2.3 360-degree feedback (peers, managers, subordinates)
  - 5.2.2.4 Self-assessment
- 5.2.3 Appraisal schedule and timeline management
- 5.2.4 Appraisal as per defined schedule
- 5.2.5 Customizable review forms based on role/category
- 5.2.6 Automated notifications/reminders

#### **5.3 Multi-Rater Feedback: P2**

- 5.3.1 Manager, peer, self, and subordinate evaluations
- 5.3.2 Anonymous feedback (if enabled)
- 5.3.3 Feedback templates for clinical and non-clinical staff
- 5.3.4 Rating scales, comments, and evidence uploads

## Score Calculation & Rating: P1

- 5.3.5 Weighted scoring mechanism
- 5.3.6 Auto-calculated final scores
- 5.3.7 Performance bands (e.g., Outstanding, Meets Expectations, Below Expectations)
- 5.3.8 Calibration tools for fair and consistent ratings

## 5.4 Performance History & Analytics: P1

- 5.4.1 Historical performance records for each employee
- 5.4.2 Graphical performance trends (e.g., over multiple cycles)
- 5.4.3 Department and role-based performance analytics
- 5.4.4 Attrition and performance correlation analysis

## 5.5 Integration with Other HR Functions: P2

- 5.5.1 Link with:
  - 5.5.1.1 **Payroll** (for performance-linked bonuses/increments)
  - 5.5.1.2 **Training** (for skill gap identification)
  - 5.5.1.3 **Succession Planning** (for leadership pipeline)
- 5.5.2 Performance-based access to internal job postings

## 5.6 Training & Development Recommendations: P1

- 5.6.1 Auto-suggest training based on appraisal feedback
- 5.6.2 Skill-gap analysis
- 5.6.3 Assign learning plans and track completion

## 5.7 Promotion & Career Progression Support: P1

- 5.7.1 Automated eligibility check for promotions
- 5.7.2 Track readiness for higher roles
- 5.7.3 Performance inputs for internal mobility and transfer decisions

## 5.8 Disciplinary and Reward Tracking: P2

- 5.8.1 Document rewards, recognitions, and commendations
- 5.8.2 Track warnings, disciplinary actions, and PIPs (Performance Improvement Plans)
- 5.8.3 Link performance ratings with disciplinary records

## 5.9 Employee Self-Service Access: P2

- 5.9.1 Employees can:
  - 5.9.1.1 View performance objectives
  - 5.9.1.2 Fill self-assessment
  - 5.9.1.3 View feedback and final ratings
  - 5.9.1.4 Track appraisal history
- 5.9.2 Manager dashboard for team performance

# 6 Training & Development

## 6.1 Staff Orientation at Induction: P1

- 6.1.1 Mission and goals
- 6.1.2 The hospital / Departments / services
- 6.1.3 Relevant department/unit/service/program policies and procedures
- 6.1.4 Patient rights and responsibilities
- 6.1.5 His/her rights and responsibilities

- 6.1.6 Performance appraisal system
- 6.1.7 Fire safety
- 6.1.8 Infection prevention and control
- 6.1.9 CQI
- 6.1.10 The assigned department/section
- 6.1.11 The specific job within the department

## **6.2 Training Needs Identification: P1**

- 6.2.1 Performance appraisal-linked skill gap identification
- 6.2.2 Departmental training needs assessment
- 6.2.3 Compliance-based training (e.g., fire safety, infection control, BLS/ACLS)
- 6.2.4 Role-based mandatory training (nurses, lab techs, surgeons, etc.)

## **6.3 Training Program Management: P1**

- 6.3.1 Create and manage training programs:
  - 6.3.1.1 Internal (in-house faculty, HR trainers)
  - 6.3.1.2 External (medical colleges, certified trainers)
- 6.3.2 Define:
  - 6.3.2.1 Training type (online, classroom, hands-on)
  - 6.3.2.2 Target audience (specific roles or departments)
  - 6.3.2.3 Duration, schedule, location
- 6.3.3 Course material uploads (PPTs, PDFs, videos)

## **6.4 Training Calendar & Scheduling: P1**

- 6.4.1 Monthly/quarterly training calendar setup
- 6.4.2 Auto-notification to nominated participants
- 6.4.3 Session reminders (email/SMS/app)
- 6.4.4 Room/resource booking integration (projectors, halls)

## **6.5 Participant Nomination & Enrollment: P1**

- 6.5.1 Employee self-nomination
- 6.5.2 Manager/HR nomination workflows
- 6.5.3 Enrollment limits and waitlist management
- 6.5.4 Attendance tracking (manual or biometric integration)

## **6.6 Assessment & Certification: P1**

- 6.6.1 Pre- and post-training assessments
- 6.6.2 Online quizzes or practical evaluations
- 6.6.3 Scoring system with pass/fail criteria
- 6.6.4 Certificate generation and issuance (auto-generated)
- 6.6.5 Certificate expiry tracking (for renewals)

## **6.7 Training Effectiveness & Feedback: P1**

- 6.7.1 Participant feedback collection
- 6.7.2 Trainer evaluation
- 6.7.3 Training effectiveness survey (Kirkpatrick Model – optional)
- 6.7.4 Report on knowledge gain vs. pre-assessment

## **6.8 Employee Training History: P1**

- 6.8.1 Maintain complete training records for every employee
- 6.8.2 View certifications completed, due, or expired

- 6.8.3 Integration with performance module for up skilling analysis
- 6.8.4 Required during audits, promotions, or departmental reviews

## **6.9 Self-Service & Mobile Access: P1**

- 6.9.1 Employee portal for:
  - 6.9.1.1 Viewing training calendar
  - 6.9.1.2 Enrolling in sessions
  - 6.9.1.3 Downloading course materials
  - 6.9.1.4 Taking online training modules or assessments
- 6.9.2 Manager dashboard to monitor team training status

# **7 Employee Health & Safety (Critical in Hospitals)**

## **7.1 Occupational Health Records: P1**

- 7.1.1 Maintain health profiles for all employees:
  - 7.1.1.1 Vaccination records (Hepatitis B, COVID-19, etc.)
  - 7.1.1.2 Pre-employment medical screening reports
  - 7.1.1.3 Periodic health check-up results
- 7.1.2 Chronic illness or disability records (if disclosed)
- 7.1.3 Confidential handling of medical data with secure access

## **7.2 Immunization & Medical Surveillance: P1**

- 7.2.1 Role-based immunization requirements (clinical, lab, waste handlers)
- 7.2.2 Auto-alerts for due or expired vaccines
- 7.2.3 Scheduling and tracking of routine health check-ups
- 7.2.4 Occupational exposure surveillance (TB, COVID, needle-stick injuries)

## **7.3 Medical Checkups & Surveillance: P1**

- 7.3.1 Annual health check tracking
- 7.3.2 TB screening, mental health assessments

## **7.4 Infection Exposure & Incident Reporting: P1**

- 7.4.1 Real-time reporting of:
  - 7.4.1.1 Needle-stick/sharps injuries
  - 7.4.1.2 Chemical spills
  - 7.4.1.3 Exposure to infectious diseases
- 7.4.2 Incident documentation:
  - 7.4.2.1 Time, location, staff involved
  - 7.4.2.2 Initial response and follow-up actions
- 7.4.3 Automatic alert to Infection Control and HR

## **7.5 Workplace Safety Audits: P1**

- 7.5.1 Safety audit schedules and tracking
- 7.5.2 Digital checklists for safety inspections (labs, wards, OTs)
- 7.5.3 Hazard identification and risk assessment documentation
- 7.5.4 Assign corrective actions to responsible teams
- 7.5.5 Compliance status dashboards

## **7.6 Emergency Response Management: P1**

- 7.6.1 Fire drills and mock code blue training schedules
- 7.6.2 First aid team assignment and training history

- 7.6.3 Emergency contact database
- 7.6.4 Location-wise emergency preparedness plans

## **7.7 Mental Health & Well-being Support: P2**

- 7.7.1 Employee Assistance Program (EAP) integration
- 7.7.2 Stress and burnout assessment tools (optional)
- 7.7.3 Counseling session scheduling
- 7.7.4 Confidentiality and privacy protocols for mental health records

## **7.8 Policy Management: P1**

- 7.8.1 Upload and share:
  - 7.8.1.1 Health & safety policy documents
  - 7.8.1.2 SOPs for incident handling
  - 7.8.1.3 COVID-19 and pandemic protocols
- 7.8.2 Digital acknowledgment by employees

# **8 Employee Self-Service Portal**

## **8.1 Secure Login & Access: P2**

- 8.1.1 Role-based secure access (doctor, nurse, paramedic, admin staff, etc.)
- 8.1.2 Integration with hospital SSO (Single Sign-On), biometric login, or OTP
- 8.1.3 Mobile-friendly interface or dedicated app access

## **8.2 Profile Management: P2**

- 8.2.1 View and update personal details (address, contact info, emergency contacts)
- 8.2.2 View employment details (designation, department, employee ID)
- 8.2.3 Upload personal documents (certificates, ID proof)

## **8.3 Attendance & Leave: P2**

- 8.3.1 Daily attendance view (real-time or historical)
- 8.3.2 Apply for leave (casual, sick, earned, maternity, etc.)
- 8.3.3 View leave balance and history
- 8.3.4 Track leave approval status
- 8.3.5 Regularization requests (for missed punches, incorrect entries)

## **8.4 Shift & Roster Management: P2**

- 8.4.1 View assigned shifts and duty rosters
- 8.4.2 Request shift changes (subject to manager approval)
- 8.4.3 Notifications for shift changes or weekly schedules
- 8.4.4 View on-call or emergency duty assignments

## **8.5 Pay slip & Compensation: P2**

- 8.5.1 Access monthly pay slips
- 8.5.2 View salary structure and components
- 8.5.3 Download income tax statements
- 8.5.4 Submit tax declarations and investment proofs

## **8.6 Training & Development: P2**

- 8.6.1 View assigned and available training programs
- 8.6.2 Enroll in sessions and take online courses
- 8.6.3 Complete pre- and post-training assessments
- 8.6.4 Download training certificates
- 8.6.5 View personal training history and certifications

## **8.7 Performance & Appraisals: P2**

- 8.7.1 Access performance appraisal forms
- 8.7.2 Fill out self-appraisals
- 8.7.3 View feedback and final ratings
- 8.7.4 Submit peer/manager reviews (if 360° feedback is enabled)

## **8.8 Requests & Helpdesk: P2**

- 8.8.1 Submit queries or requests to HR (e.g., experience letters, NOC)
- 8.8.2 Track status of submitted requests
- 8.8.3 Access HR helpdesk and knowledge base (policies, FAQs)

## **8.9 Policy Acknowledgments & Updates: P2**

- 8.9.1 View and acknowledge updated HR policies and guidelines
- 8.9.2 Notifications for mandatory reads (e.g., safety protocols, compliance updates)

## **8.10 Document & Certificate Access: P2**

- 8.10.1 Download:
  - 8.10.1.1 Appointment letter
  - 8.10.1.2 Service certificate
  - 8.10.1.3 Promotion or transfer letters
  - 8.10.1.4 HR policy documents

## **8.11 Exit & Resignation Workflow: P2**

- 8.11.1 Submit resignation request online
- 8.11.2 Track exit formalities and clearance status
- 8.11.3 View final settlement details

## **8.12 Notifications & Alerts: P2**

- 8.12.1 Real-time alerts for:
  - 8.12.1.1 Leave approval/rejection
  - 8.12.1.2 Shift changes
  - 8.12.1.3 Training schedules
  - 8.12.1.4 Compliance deadlines (license renewal, health checks)
- 8.12.2 SMS, Email, or In-app notifications

# **9 HRM Dashboard**

## **9.1 Workforce Overview: P1**

- 9.1.1 Total number of employees (clinical & non-clinical)
- 9.1.2 Distribution by department (e.g., nursing, admin, doctors)
- 9.1.3 Staff by facility/location
- 9.1.4 Gender and age breakdown
- 9.1.5 Employment type (full-time, part-time, contractual, etc.)

## **9.2 Staff Attendance & Availability: P1**

- 9.2.1 Daily, weekly, and monthly attendance reports
- 9.2.2 Absenteeism trends by department/staff category
- 9.2.3 On-leave staff summary
- 9.2.4 Roster & shift schedules
- 9.2.5 Real-time staff availability
- 9.2.6 Leave utilization reports

- 9.2.7 Roster adherence and shift coverage analytics
- 9.2.8 Leave forecast and planning support for department heads

### **9.3 Recruitment & Vacancies: P1**

- 9.3.1 Open positions by department/facility
- 9.3.2 Status of recruitment processes
- 9.3.3 Time-to-fill and time-to-hire metrics
- 9.3.4 Applicant tracking summary
- 9.3.5 Source effectiveness (e.g., internal vs. job portal)
- 9.3.6 Offer-to-join ratio
- 9.3.7 Recruitment funnel analysis
- 9.3.8 New hire satisfaction survey data

### **9.4 Training & Capacity Building: P1**

- 9.4.1 Number of trained staff per category
- 9.4.2 Upcoming and completed training programs
- 9.4.3 Certification and re-certification tracking
- 9.4.4 Training effectiveness feedback
- 9.4.5 Training completion rates by department/role
- 9.4.6 Certification compliance dashboards

### **9.5 Performance & Appraisal: P1**

- 9.5.1 Key performance indicators (KPIs) by role
- 9.5.2 Recent appraisals completed
- 9.5.3 High-performing vs low-performing staff
- 9.5.4 Staff promotion and disciplinary actions summary
- 9.5.5 Appraisal status tracking reports
- 9.5.6 Department-wise performance comparison
- 9.5.7 Role-specific dashboards (HR, Managers, Executives)

### **9.6 Payroll & Benefits: P1**

- 9.6.1 Salary disbursement status
- 9.6.2 Deductions and benefits summary
- 9.6.3 Overtime and incentives tracking
- 9.6.4 Department-wise salary reports
- 9.6.5 Cost-to-company (CTC) analysis
- 9.6.6 Variance reports (monthly/yearly)
- 9.6.7 Payroll audit logs and salary revision history
- 9.6.8 Budget forecasting tools

### **9.7 Compliance & Licensing: P1**

- 9.7.1 Staff licenses and certification status
- 9.7.2 Upcoming renewal dates
- 9.7.3 Compliance with national staffing norms (e.g., doctor-to-patient ratio)

### **9.8 Turnover & Retention: P1**

- 9.8.1 Staff attrition rate (monthly/yearly)
- 9.8.2 Exit reasons analysis
- 9.8.3 Retention rate trends

### **9.9 Health Worker Well-being: P1**

- 9.9.1 Reported incidents (stress, burnout, etc.)

- 9.9.2 Access to mental health resources
- 9.9.3 Employee feedback or engagement metrics
- 9.9.4 Occupational health report for audits
- 9.9.5 Injury and illness logs
- 9.9.6 Health & safety incident frequency and severity analysis

#### **9.10 Custom Reports & Exports: P1**

- 9.10.1 Ability to generate custom reports (PDF/Excel)
- 9.10.2 Filters by location, cadre, and date range, etc.
- 9.10.3 Scheduled report delivery via email or SMS

# **EQUIPMENT MANAGEMENT MODULE**

**NOTE: This module will be implemented in all clinical and patient areas of the hospital**

## **1 Equipment Inventory Management**

### **1.1 Equipment Master Data Management**

- 1.1.1 Create and maintain master data for each equipment item: P1
  - 1.1.1.1 Unique Equipment ID (auto-generated or manual)
  - 1.1.1.2 Equipment name and category
    - 1.1.1.2.1 E.g., MRI machine, ECG monitor, infusion pump
  - 1.1.1.3 Equipment type (Diagnostic, Surgical, Therapeutic, etc.)
  - 1.1.1.4 Manufacturer and model
  - 1.1.1.5 Equipment specifications (power, capacity, software version)
  - 1.1.1.6 Equipment classification (critical/non-critical)
- 1.1.2 Upload supporting documents: P2
  - 1.1.2.1 User manuals, brochures, technical sheets
- 1.1.3 Add new equipment with details: P1
  - 1.1.3.1 Equipment ID, name, type (diagnostic, therapeutic, surgical, etc.)
  - 1.1.3.2 Manufacturer and model
  - 1.1.3.3 Purchase date, warranty, and expected lifecycle
  - 1.1.3.4 Location (department, floor, room)
  - 1.1.3.5 Serial numbers, barcodes, or RFID tags
- 1.1.4 View/search/filter/sort equipment list - P1

### **1.2 Procurement & Acquisition Tracking**

- 1.2.1 Record procurement details: P1
  - 1.2.1.1 Purchase Order (PO) number
  - 1.2.1.2 Vendor/supplier details
  - 1.2.1.3 Purchase date, delivery date
  - 1.2.1.4 Invoice number and cost
- 1.2.2 Link to procurement module or ERP – P2
- 1.2.3 Capture installation and commissioning details – P2

### **1.3 Location and Department Assignment**

- 1.3.1 Tag equipment to: P1
  - 1.3.1.1 Hospital facility, building, floor, and room
  - 1.3.1.2 Assigned department/unit (e.g., Radiology, ICU, OT)
  - 1.3.1.3 Assigned responsible person/technician
- 1.3.2 Transfer equipment between locations – P1
- 1.3.3 Support for asset movement tracking (transfer from one department to another) – P1

### **1.4 Status and Availability Tracking**

- 1.4.1 Track real-time status: P1

- 1.4.1.1 Available, In Use, Under Maintenance, Reserved, Decommissioned
- 1.4.2 Maintain utilization logs: P2
  - 1.4.2.1 Timestamp-based logging of when the equipment was used and by which department

- 1.4.3 Enable alerts for idle or underutilized equipment – P2

## **1.5 Barcode Tagging and Tracking**

- 1.5.1 Generate and print barcode tags for each equipment – P1
- 1.5.2 Enable scanning for quick location, check-in/check-out, and audit – P1
- 1.5.3 Integration with mobile devices for field scanning – P1

## **1.6 Asset Valuation and Depreciation**

- 1.6.1 Maintain initial purchase cost, warranty terms, and depreciation method (e.g., straight-line) – P2
- 1.6.2 Integration with Finance module to: P2
  - 1.6.2.1 Calculate annual depreciation
  - 1.6.2.2 Support asset valuation for insurance and budgeting

## **1.7 Inventory Reconciliation & Audit**

- 1.7.1 Support periodic inventory audits – P2
  - 1.7.1.1 Physical verification logs
  - 1.7.1.2 Reconciliation reports (physical vs system)
- 1.7.2 Discrepancy tracking and justification – P2
- 1.7.3 Auto-flag missing or moved equipment – P2

## **1.8 End-of-Life Management**

- 1.8.1 Track end-of-life (EOL) status – P2
- 1.8.2 Flag equipment for decommissioning/disposal based on: - P2
  - 1.8.2.1 Age, maintenance cost, risk category, or performance
- 1.8.3 Record disposal method and date – P2
- 1.8.4 Support regulatory compliance (e-waste disposal records, etc.) – P2

# **2 Maintenance and Servicing**

## **Preventive Maintenance (PM) Scheduling**

- 2.1.1 Define PM schedules based on: P1
    - 2.1.1.1 Manufacturer recommendations
    - 2.1.1.2 Equipment usage hours/cycles
    - 2.1.1.3 Hospital-specific maintenance policies
  - 2.1.2 Create recurring maintenance plans (monthly, quarterly, annually) – P1
  - 2.1.3 Auto-generate maintenance work orders – P2
  - 2.1.4 Notify assigned technician or maintenance team via email/SMS/in-app – P2
  - 2.1.5 Link PM schedule to calendar with alerts for upcoming due dates – P1
- ## **2.2 Corrective Maintenance (CM) & Breakdown Management**
- 2.2.1 Log equipment breakdowns: P2

- 2.2.1.1 Source: Staff report, system-generated, sensor alert
  - 2.2.1.2 Capture symptoms, error codes, and urgency
- 2.2.2 Assign technician based on: P2
  - 2.2.2.1 Equipment type
  - 2.2.2.2 Technician's workload and specialization
- 2.2.3 Track repair process: P2
  - 2.2.3.1 Start and completion times
  - 2.2.3.2 Troubleshooting steps
  - 2.2.3.3 Spare parts used
- 2.2.4 Monitor downtime duration and impact on services – P2
- 2.3 Work Order Management**
- 2.3.1 Create, assign, and track work orders for all maintenance tasks – P2
- 2.3.2 Work order components: - P2
  - 2.3.2.1 Equipment ID, location
  - 2.3.2.2 Type: Preventive / Corrective / Emergency
  - 2.3.2.3 Task checklist
  - 2.3.2.4 Priority and status (Open, In Progress, Closed, etc.)
  - 2.3.2.5 Technician remarks and service notes
- 2.3.3 Include time spent, labor cost, and parts used – P3
- 2.4 Spare Parts and Consumables Management**
- 2.4.1 Maintain inventory of spare parts and consumables – P1
  - 2.4.1.1 Track stock levels, reorder alerts
  - 2.4.1.2 Link parts to specific equipment types
- 2.4.2 Log parts used during each maintenance job – P1
- 2.4.3 Integrate with inventory/warehouse module for real-time updates – P2
- 2.5 Vendor and AMC/CMC Management**
- 2.5.1 Maintain vendor database: P2
  - 2.5.1.1 Contact info, service history, certifications
- 2.5.2 Track AMC/CMC contracts: P2
  - 2.5.2.1 Equipment under contract
  - 2.5.2.2 Coverage details (labor only, full, warranty extension)
  - 2.5.2.3 Expiry dates, renewal reminders
- 2.5.3 Log external vendor visits, service details, and invoices – P2
- 2.6 Calibration and Compliance Management**
- 2.6.1 Schedule calibration for equipment needing precision (e.g., lab devices, monitors) – P1
- 2.6.2 Maintain calibration certificates and logs – P1
- 2.6.3 Set automated alerts for upcoming or overdue calibration – P1
- 2.7 Downtime and Risk Analysis**
- 2.7.1 Monitor Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) – P3
- 2.7.2 Track Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) – P3
- 2.7.3 Record downtime start/end timestamps – P3
- 2.7.4 Root Cause Analysis (RCA) logs for repeated issues – P3

- 2.7.5 Generate risk scores for critical care equipment – P3

### **3 Equipment Lifecycle Management**

#### **3.1 Equipment Acquisition**

- 3.1.1 Track how and when each equipment was acquired: - P1
  - 3.1.1.1 Procurement details (purchase, donation, lease)
  - 3.1.1.2 Vendor/supplier info, purchase order reference
  - 3.1.1.3 Delivery and installation dates
  - 3.1.1.4 Initial asset value and invoice data
  - 3.1.1.5 Equipment specifications and documents (manuals, certificates)
- 3.1.2 Integration with Procurement & Finance modules – P2

#### **3.2 Commissioning and Deployment**

- 3.2.1 Record commissioning/installation details: P1
  - 3.2.1.1 Site preparation, power/load testing, environmental checks
  - 3.2.1.2 Installation certificate upload
  - 3.2.1.3 Initial calibration/test reports
- 3.2.2 Assign equipment to: P1
  - 3.2.2.1 Department/ward/unit
  - 3.2.2.2 Specific building/floor/room
  - 3.2.2.3 Responsible staff/biomedical team

#### **3.3 Operational Monitoring**

- 3.3.1 Track real-time usage status: P1
  - 3.3.1.1 Available, In Use, Idle, Under Maintenance
- 3.3.2 Link usage to: - P2
  - 3.3.2.1 Clinical procedures, departments, patient services (if applicable)
- 3.3.3 Flag assets for underutilization or overuse – P2

#### **3.4 Maintenance History**

- 3.4.1 Integrate with maintenance and servicing logs: P1
  - 3.4.1.1 PM/CM schedules
  - 3.4.1.2 Calibration and service certificates
  - 3.4.1.3 Spare parts and repair history
- 3.4.2 Attach digital records: P2
  - 3.4.2.1 AMC/CMC history, breakdown logs, technician notes
- 3.4.3 Highlight high-maintenance or frequently failing assets – P2

#### **3.5 Depreciation and Asset Valuation**

- 3.5.1 Maintain financial depreciation: P2
  - 3.5.1.1 Methods: Straight line, declining balance, custom
  - 3.5.1.2 Integration with Finance module for yearly depreciation
- 3.5.2 Calculate: P2

- 3.5.2.1 Current book value
- 3.5.2.2 Total cost of ownership (TCO)
- 3.5.3 Include cost breakdowns: P2
  - 3.5.3.1 Purchase, maintenance, parts, service contracts

### **3.6 Asset Transfer and Relocation**

- 3.6.1 Enable authorized transfer of equipment between: P1
  - 3.6.1.1 Departments, campuses, buildings
- 3.6.2 Maintain movement log: P1
  - 3.6.2.1 From/to locations
  - 3.6.2.2 Reason for movement
  - 3.6.2.3 Authorized person
- 3.6.3 Generate transfer request and approval workflows – P1

### **3.7 Asset Performance & Utilization Analytics**

- 3.7.1 Equipment usage statistics: P1
  - 3.7.1.1 Operating hours, cycle counts, uptime vs downtime
- 3.7.2 Identify: P1
  - 3.7.2.1 Idle/underused assets
  - 3.7.2.2 Over-utilized or mission-critical equipment
- 3.7.3 Generate performance KPIs: P2
  - 3.7.3.1 MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures)
  - 3.7.3.2 MTTR (Mean Time To Repair)

### **3.8 End-of-Life (EOL) Tracking**

- 3.8.1 Identify assets nearing end of lifecycle: P1
  - 3.8.1.1 Age-based rules, usage limits, condition reports
- 3.8.2 Track disposal or decommissioning process: P1
  - 3.8.2.1 Deactivation date, disposal method (scrap, resale, donation)
  - 3.8.2.2 Regulatory requirements (e-waste rules, hazardous material disposal)
- 3.8.3 Update asset registry and flag for replacement P1

### **3.9 Replacement Planning & Budgeting**

- 3.9.1 Flag assets for replacement based on: P1
  - 3.9.1.1 High maintenance cost
  - 3.9.1.2 Low utilization
  - 3.9.1.3 Obsolescence or safety issues
- 3.9.2 Support capital budgeting for asset replacement – P2
- 3.9.3 Provide procurement suggestions based on lifecycle analysis – P2

## **4 Compliance and Audit**

### **4.1 Regulatory Compliance Tracking**

- 4.1.1 Support compliance with national and international healthcare standards: P2
- 4.1.2 Maintain up-to-date compliance status for each equipment: P2

- 4.1.2.1 Certification status (valid/expired)
    - 4.1.2.2 Next inspection/audit due date
  - 4.1.3 Upload and manage documents: P2
    - 4.1.3.1 Calibration certificates
    - 4.1.3.2 Safety inspection reports
    - 4.1.3.3 Electrical safety test results
    - 4.1.3.4 Service contracts and AMC/CMC agreements
- 4.2 Equipment Audit Trail**
- 4.2.1 Maintain a detailed audit log for each asset: P2
    - 4.2.1.1 All lifecycle status changes (e.g., Installed → Operational → EOL)
    - 4.2.1.2 Maintenance activity logs (PM, CM, calibration)
    - 4.2.1.3 Technician/service personnel involved
    - 4.2.1.4 Asset movement history (location and custodian changes)
  - 4.2.2 Each log entry should include: P2
    - 4.2.2.1 Timestamp
    - 4.2.2.2 Action performed
    - 4.2.2.3 User ID/role
    - 4.2.2.4 Linked documentation, if applicable
- 4.3 Internal and External Inspection Support**
- 4.3.1 Generate pre-inspection checklists: P2
    - 4.3.1.1 Equipment operational status
    - 4.3.1.2 Last calibration and servicing dates
    - 4.3.1.3 AMC/CMC status
  - 4.3.2 Provide inspection-friendly reports: P2
    - 4.3.2.1 Equipment due for inspection
    - 4.3.2.2 Equipment without valid calibration
    - 4.3.2.3 Maintenance compliance reports (PM completion %)
  - 4.3.3 Export logs and documents for offline audits – P2
- 4.4 Incident Reporting and Investigation**
- 4.4.1 Log safety incidents or equipment-related failures – P1
  - 4.4.2 Capture investigation data: - P1
    - 4.4.2.1 Root cause analysis (RCA)
    - 4.4.2.2 CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Actions)
    - 4.4.2.3 Technician/department feedback
  - 4.4.3 Link incidents with audit reports for traceability – P1
  - 4.4.4 Track resolution progress and closure - P1
- 4.5 Compliance Alerts and Notifications**
- 4.5.1 Automated alerts for: P1

- 4.5.1.1 Upcoming calibration due dates
- 4.5.1.2 Expiring certifications (AMC, CMC, ISO, NABH)
- 4.5.1.3 Equipment nearing inspection or audit deadlines
- 4.5.2 Send reminders to: P1
- 4.5.3 Biomedical teams
  - 4.5.3.1 Compliance officers
  - 4.5.3.2 Department heads

#### **4.6 Risk-Based Equipment Classification**

- 4.6.1 Classify equipment by risk category:
  - 4.6.1.1 Critical, semi-critical, non-critical
- 4.6.2 Apply different compliance rules/checklists based on risk class
- 4.6.3 Enable focused audit strategy and reporting

#### **4.7 Checklist and SOP Management**

- 4.7.1 Upload and version-control standard checklists for: P1
  - 4.7.1.1 Equipment calibration
  - 4.7.1.2 Maintenance inspections
  - 4.7.1.3 Commissioning/decommissioning procedures
- 4.7.2 Track completion status for scheduled audits – P1
- 4.7.3 Link SOPs to equipment types and compliance requirements – P1

### **5 Work Order and Ticketing System**

#### **5.1 Request Creation and Logging**

- 5.1.1.1 Allow request creation from multiple sources: P2
- 5.1.1.2 Hospital staff (clinical or technical)
- 5.1.1.3 Maintenance department
- 5.1.1.4 Automated system triggers (e.g., IoT alerts, overdue PM)
- 5.1.1.5 QR/barcode scan from mobile device
- 5.1.1.6 Request details to include: P2
- 5.1.1.7 Equipment ID and name (auto-filled)
- 5.1.1.8 Location (building, floor, room)
- 5.1.1.9 Department and user raising the request
- 5.1.1.10 Nature of issue (dropdown and free text)
- 5.1.1.11 Priority level (Critical, High, Medium, Low)
- 5.1.1.12 Attachment upload (photo, error screenshot, logs)

#### **5.2 Work Order Generation**

- 5.2.1 Auto-generate work orders from: P1
  - 5.2.1.1 Corrective Maintenance requests
  - 5.2.1.2 Preventive Maintenance schedules
- 5.2.2 Unique Work Order (WO) ID – P1
- 5.2.3 Contain: P1

- 5.2.3.1 Linked request ID (if applicable)
- 5.2.3.2 Assigned technician/vendor
- 5.2.3.3 Checklist or SOP for task execution
- 5.2.3.4 Estimated time of resolution

- 5.2.3.5 SLA response and resolution time

### **5.3 Assignment and Dispatch**

- 5.3.1 Assign to: P1
  - 5.3.1.1 In-house technician
  - 5.3.1.2 Vendor (AMC/CMC contractor)
- 5.3.2 Rules-based auto-assignment: P2
  - 5.3.2.1 Based on equipment type, technician specialty, location, availability
- 5.3.3 Manual override for supervisor allocation – P1
- 5.3.4 Notify assigned personnel via email/SMS/in-app – P1

### **5.4 Status Tracking and Lifecycle**

- 5.4.1 Work order/request status should follow a clear lifecycle: P1
  - 5.4.1.1 New → Assigned → In Progress → On Hold → Completed → Closed
- 5.4.2 Track timestamps for: - P1
  - 5.4.2.1 Request creation
  - 5.4.2.2 Acknowledgement
  - 5.4.2.3 Job start and finish times
- 5.4.3 Color-coded indicators for SLA breach

### **5.5 SLA and Priority Management**

- 5.5.1 Define SLA policies by: P1
  - 5.5.1.1 Equipment type
  - 5.5.1.2 Risk category (e.g., life-saving equipment = <4h SLA)
  - 5.5.1.3 Contract terms (vendor SLA)
- 5.5.2 Automatic alerts for: P2
  - 5.5.2.1 SLA nearing breach
  - 5.5.2.2 Breached tickets
- 5.5.3 Generate SLA performance metrics - P2

### **5.6 Work Order Execution and Completion**

- 5.6.1 Technicians/vendors can: P1
  - 5.6.1.1 View and update work order
  - 5.6.1.2 Log repair actions taken
  - 5.6.1.3 Use predefined checklist or upload task-specific report
  - 5.6.1.4 Register spare parts used
- 5.6.2 Capture technician comments and digital signature – P2
- 5.6.3 Attach completion evidence (e.g., test reports, images) – P1

### **5.7 Feedback and Closure**

- 5.7.1 Notify requester on job completion – P1

- 5.7.2 Request user to validate and rate service – P1
- 5.7.3 Option to reopen request if issue persists – P1
- 5.7.4 Auto-close after defined duration if no action from requester – P2

## **5.8 Audit Trail and Documentation**

- 5.8.1 Maintain full history of: P1
  - 5.8.1.1 Every status change
  - 5.8.1.2 Technician assignment
  - 5.8.1.3 Parts used
  - 5.8.1.4 Documents or photos attached
- 5.8.2 Exportable logs for audits – P1

## **6 Integration with Other Modules**

### **6.1 Integration with Procurement Module – P2**

- 6.1.1 Sync purchase orders and delivery details.
- 6.1.2 Auto-create equipment records from procurement data (vendor, model, cost, PO number).
- 6.1.3 Tag equipment with supplier and warranty information directly from procurement.
- 6.1.4 Fetch procurement documents (invoices, POs, GRNs).

### **6.2 Integration with Inventory/Warehouse Module – P2**

- 6.2.1 Link spare part usage during maintenance jobs to inventory records.
- 6.2.2 Auto-deduct stock when parts are used.
- 6.2.3 Raise internal requisitions for parts low in stock.
- 6.2.4 Monitor part availability per location or service team.

### **6.3 Integration with Finance & Accounts Module – P2**

- 6.3.1 Equipment purchase cost and depreciation tracking.
- 6.3.2 AMC/CMC contract payments and invoice matching.
- 6.3.3 Cost logging for repairs, maintenance, and replacements.
- 6.3.4 Link service jobs to vendor invoices.

### **6.4 Integration with Maintenance & Facilities Management – P2**

- 6.4.1 Schedule Preventive Maintenance based on equipment type and usage.
- 6.4.2 Sync work orders, job completion status, and technician logs.
- 6.4.3 Share equipment health status and downtime reports.

### **6.5 Integration with Clinical/Patient Care Modules – P2**

- 6.5.1 Link equipment (e.g., ventilators, monitors) to patient beds or cases.
- 6.5.2 Display equipment availability and status to clinical staff.
- 6.5.3 Alert clinicians about equipment under maintenance.

### **6.6 Integration with Human Resources (HR) / Technician Roster – P2**

- 6.6.1 Assign tickets based on technician skills, shifts, or departments.
- 6.6.2 Fetch technician contact info, role, and availability.
- 6.6.3 Log training or certification history (e.g., biomedical engineers).

### **6.7 Integration with Document Management System (DMS) – P2**

- 6.7.1 Link user manuals, AMC contracts, calibration certificates to equipment records.
- 6.7.2 Maintain version-controlled documents.

## 7 Reports and Dashboards

### 7.1 Real-Time Dashboards

7.1.1 Key Features: P1

7.1.1.1 **Equipment Availability:** Real-time status (Operational, Under Maintenance, Out of Service) and location of critical equipment.

7.1.1.2 **Maintenance Status:** Overview of ongoing, pending, and overdue work orders/tickets.

7.1.1.3 **SLA Compliance:** Visual representation of SLA breaches or adherence (response time, resolution time).

7.1.1.4 **Compliance Status:** Percentage of equipment in compliance with standards (calibration, inspections, certifications).

7.1.1.5 **Maintenance Cost:** Real-time cost dashboard displaying total spent on repairs, AMC, and consumables.

7.1.1.6 **Downtime Analysis:** Monitor and visualize equipment downtime by category, department, or type.

7.1.2 Filters: P2

7.1.2.1 By **location** (e.g., ICU, Radiology)

7.1.2.2 By **equipment category** (e.g., ventilators, imaging)

7.1.2.3 By **department** (e.g., Surgery, Diagnostics)

7.1.2.4 By **time frame** (daily, weekly, monthly)

### 7.2 Customizable Reports

7.2.1 Key Features: P2

7.2.1.1 **Work Order Report:** Detailed report on all active and completed work orders, including assigned technicians, completion times, parts used, and costs.

7.2.1.2 **Preventive Maintenance (PM) Compliance Report:** Tracks completed and overdue PM tasks, including technician names and compliance rates.

7.2.1.3 **Asset Health Report:** Analysis of equipment lifecycle, showing aging equipment, frequency of repairs, and upcoming replacement needs.

7.2.1.4 **Vendor Performance Report:** Evaluates vendor performance based on SLA adherence, response time, repair quality, and feedback ratings.

7.2.1.5 **Cost Analysis Report:** Breakdown of equipment-related costs, including maintenance, repairs, spare parts, and AMC contracts. Allows for cost comparison over time.

7.2.1.6 **Equipment Failure Report:** Lists equipment failures, downtime, root cause analysis, and impact on hospital operations.

7.2.1.7 **Inventory Usage Report:** Tracks parts used for maintenance, including quantities and cost, with insight into stock levels.

7.2.2 Filters: P2

- 7.2.2.1 By equipment type or model
- 7.2.2.2 By department or location
- 7.2.2.3 By date range (monthly, quarterly, yearly)
- 7.2.2.4 By technician or vendor

### 7.3 Compliance and Regulatory Reports

- 7.3.1 Key Features: P1
  - 7.3.1.1 **Calibration & Inspection Status:** Tracks the status of calibration, validation, and safety inspections for each piece of equipment.
  - 7.3.1.2 **Contract Compliance Report:** Monitors compliance with vendor contracts, including AMC/CMC coverage, terms, renewals, and SLA adherence.

### 7.4 Asset Lifecycle and Depreciation Reports

- 7.4.1 Key Features: P2
  - 7.4.1.1 **Asset Lifecycle Report:** Monitors the lifecycle of equipment from purchase to decommissioning, identifying aging equipment and those approaching the end of their useful life.
  - 7.4.1.2 **Depreciation Report:** Tracks the depreciation of assets for financial reporting, tax purposes, and asset replacement planning. Shows asset value over time.
  - 7.4.1.3 **Warranty and AMC Status:** Displays equipment covered under warranty or AMC, including expiry dates and renewal status.

### 7.5 Workforce and Technician Performance Reports

- 7.5.1 Key Features: P2
  - 7.5.1.1 **Technician Performance:** Evaluates technician efficiency based on work orders completed, time taken for repairs, and feedback from users.
  - 7.5.1.2 **Technician Utilization:** Tracks workload distribution among technicians, identifying high-performing technicians and those with high unassigned or idle times.
  - 7.5.1.3 **Training Compliance:** Ensures that technicians are up to date with required certifications and training.

### 7.6 Vendor and Contract Management Reports

- 7.6.1 Key Features: P2
  - 7.6.1.1 **Vendor SLA Compliance:** Shows vendor adherence to contracted SLAs (response and resolution time), including vendor-specific breakdowns.
  - 7.6.1.2 **Contract Renewal Report:** Displays upcoming contract renewals for AMCs, CMCs, and other service contracts.
  - 7.6.1.3 **Cost vs. Contracted Service:** Compares costs incurred for service work versus the agreed contract terms, identifying discrepancies or cost-saving opportunities.

## 8 Suggestions

### 8.1 Maintenance and Service

- 8.1.1 Preventive Maintenance Scheduling

- 8.1.1.1 Task Status: Include the status of assigned tasks (e.g., pending, in-progress, completed).
- 8.1.1.2 PPM Status: Track the status of Preventive Maintenance (PPM) activities, including:
  - 8.1.1.2.1 - In-house or outsourced PPM.
  - 8.1.1.2.2 - Reasons for pending PPM (e.g., resource constraints, equipment availability).
- 8.1.1.3 Task Assignment: Ensure clear assignment of tasks to specific teams or personnel

## **8.2 Corrective Maintenance (CM) & Breakdown Management**

- 8.2.1 Complaint Status: Include the status of complaints (e.g., received, in-progress, resolved).
- 8.2.2 Complaint Resolution: Track the resolution of complaints, including:
  - 8.2.2.1 - In-house or outsourced resolution.
  - 8.2.2.2 - Reasons for pending complaints (e.g., resource constraints, awaiting spare parts).

## **8.3 Complaint Routing: Allow for:**

- 8.3.1 - Forwarding complaints to relevant departments.
- 8.3.2 - Accepting or rejecting complaints based on their nature.

## **8.4 Status Updates: Provide regular status updates on complaint resolution, including:**

- 8.4.1 - In-progress complaints.
- 8.4.2 - Completed complaints.

At the bottom center, there is an icon of a syringe and a small box with a cross, representing medical or pharmaceutical products.

## **INFECTION PREVENTION AND CONTROL MODULE**

**NOTE: This module will be implemented in all clinical and patient areas of the hospital**

### **1. Infection Prevention and Control**

#### **1.1. Protocols and Guidelines Repository: P2**

- 1.1.1. Digitized Repository of Infection Control plan and Policies:
  - 1.1.1.1. IPC committee with TORs
  - 1.1.1.2. Minutes of meetings of IPC committee
  - 1.1.1.3. Notification of IPC team with roles and responsibilities of its members
  - 1.1.1.4. Notification of IPC nurse along with JDs
  - 1.1.1.5. Hand hygiene
  - 1.1.1.6. PPE usage
  - 1.1.1.7. Environmental cleaning
- 1.1.2. Interactive, Searchable Database:
  - 1.1.2.1. Role-specific guidelines
- 1.1.3. Multimedia Resources:
  - 1.1.3.1. Training videos for donning/doffing PPE
  - 1.1.3.2. Visual flowcharts for managing infectious cases
- 1.1.4. Customizable Workflows for Handling:
  - 1.1.4.1. Contact, droplet, and airborne precautions
  - 1.1.4.2. Outbreak scenarios (e.g., COVID-19)

#### **1.2. Training and Education: P1-3**

- 1.2.1. e-Learning modules for:
  - 1.2.1.1. Infection prevention best practices.
  - 1.2.1.2. Case management protocols for infectious diseases.
  - 1.2.1.3. All staff is trained on waste management plan on regular basis
- 1.2.2. Regular push notifications for updated guidelines.

#### **1.3. Hand Hygiene Compliance: P1**

- 1.3.1. Adequate hand washing facilities are available
- 1.3.2. Real-time compliance monitoring
- 1.3.3. Automated reminders to staff via mobile devices or workstations.

#### **1.4. PPE Management: P1**

- 1.4.1. Inventory tracking for PPE supplies
- 1.4.2. Alerts for stock replenishment.
- 1.4.3. Staff training logs for proper PPE usage.

#### **1.5. Environmental Cleaning and Disinfection: P1 & P2**

- 1.5.1. Automated task scheduling for high-touch surface cleaning.
- 1.5.2. Digital logs for completed disinfection cycles.
- 1.5.3. IoT-enabled air quality monitoring:
  - 1.5.3.1. Alerts for ventilation issues in isolation rooms.
- 1.5.4. Automated reminders for disinfection tasks via mobile apps. P2

## 2. Waste Management

2.1.1. Hospital Waste Management Plan

2.1.2. Plan Implementation Status:

- 2.1.2.2. [ ] Not Implemented

- 2.2.2.2.3. General Waste (e.g., packaging)
