### 3. Infection Prevention Workflows

#### 3.1.Patient Management: P2

- 3.1.1.Triage integration flags potential infectious patients
- 3.1.2.Auto-assign isolation rooms for flagged patients based on triage data
- 3.1.3.Digital signage for directing patients with specific symptoms (e.g., fever, cough)
- 3.1.4.Real-time bed status updates in the ED
- 3.1.5.Monitor patient progress and infection status in real time

#### 3.2.Staff Workflow: P2

- 3.2.1.Task assignment and reminders for:
  - 3.2.1.1. PPE checks
  - 3.2.1.2. Compliance audits
- 3.2.2.Follow step-by-step protocols for handling infectious patients
- 3.2.3.Update completed tasks (e.g., isolation setup, disinfection) directly in the system
- 3.2.4.Mobile app notifications for:
  - 3.2.4.1. High-risk patient updates
  - 3.2.4.2. Training sessions or emergency drills

#### 3.3.Visitor Management: P2

- 3.3.1.Visitor tracking system for contact tracing
- 3.3.2.Check-ins with infection prevention instructions
- 3.3.3.Visitor limit alerts during outbreaks

### 4. Dashboard:

#### 4.1.Infection Prevention and Control:

- 4.1.1.Automated Reports: P2
  - 4.1.1.1. Daily infection rates
  - 4.1.1.2. Weekly compliance summaries for hand hygiene and PPE.
  - 4.1.1.3. Isolation room occupancy.
  - 4.1.1.4. Infection surveillance data
  - 4.1.1.5. Real-time PPE stock levels and usage trends
  - 4.1.1.6. Training completion indicators for staff
- 4.1.2.Predictive Analytics: P3
  - 4.1.2.1. Identification of high-risk zones
- 4.1.3.Alerts for: P3
  - 4.1.3.1. Potential outbreaks or clustering of infections.
  - 4.1.3.2. Low compliance with hygiene protocols
- 4.1.4.Incident Reporting: P1
  - 4.1.4.1. Digital forms for exposure or protocol breaches.

# Complaint Management Module

![Illustration of a Complaint Management Module interface.](b7828383df120e2bad175e78ff6e2997_img.jpg)

An illustration depicting a 'Complaint Management Module'. The central element is a large, light blue rounded rectangle resembling a web browser window. Inside this window, the word 'COMPLAINT' is written in bold, black, uppercase letters. To the left of the text is a yellow square containing a person's silhouette with a laptop. To the right is a yellow square with a globe icon. Below the text, a man in a blue jacket and dark pants is shown talking on a mobile phone. Surrounding the central window are several colorful icons: a blue square with a white envelope icon at the top left, a yellow speech bubble at the top right, a pink square with a white smartphone icon at the bottom left, a blue square with a person's silhouette and a laptop at the bottom center, and a pink square with white icons for Instagram, Facebook, and Twitter at the bottom right. The background is light gray with a subtle dot pattern. On the far left, there are vertical colored bars: blue at the top, followed by a beige bar, then a dark blue bar, and a green bar at the bottom.

Illustration of a Complaint Management Module interface.

# [COMPLAINTS MANAGEMENT MODULE]

## 1. Complaints Management

### 1.1. Complaint Mechanism: P1

- 1.1.1. Complaint Mechanism Exists and is Functional:
  - 1.1.1.1. Status: [Functional/Not Functional]
  - 1.1.1.2. Description: [Overview of the complaint handling process]
  - 1.1.1.3. Last System Update: [MM/DD/YYYY]
  - 1.1.1.4. Next System Review: [Scheduled review date]
  - 1.1.1.5. Complaint Handling Process: [Link to detailed process/procedure for handling complaints]

### 1.2. Complaint Registration: P1

- 1.2.1. Online/Offline Registration Options: [Available/Not Available]
- 1.2.2. Complaint Registration Portal Link: [Link to digital complaint registration system]
- 1.2.3. Anonymous Registration Option: [Yes/No]
- 1.2.4. Complaint Acknowledgement: [Automatic or manual acknowledgment of complaint upon entry]

### 1.3. Charters Display

- 1.3.1. Patient Charter Displayed: P1
  - 1.3.1.1. Status: [Displayed/Not Displayed]
  - 1.3.1.2. Location: [Location where the Patient Charter is displayed in the ED]
  - 1.3.1.3. Last Inspection: [Date of last check for charter visibility]
  - 1.3.1.4. Next Inspection: [Date for next inspection]
  - 1.3.1.5. Digital Copy of Patient Charter: [Link to digital copy of Patient Charter]
- 1.3.2. Hospital/HCE Charter Displayed: P1
  - 1.3.2.1. Status: [Displayed/Not Displayed]
  - 1.3.2.2. Location: [Location where the HCE Charter is displayed in the ED]
  - 1.3.2.3. Last Inspection: [Date of last check for charter visibility]
  - 1.3.2.4. Next Inspection: [Date for next inspection]
  - 1.3.2.5. Digital Copy of HCE Charter: [Link to digital copy of HCE Charter]

### 1.4. Complaint Entry Record

- 1.4.1. Complaint Entry Record Available: P1
  - 1.4.1.1. Complaint ID: [Unique identifier for each complaint]
  - 1.4.1.2. Complaint Type: [General, Medical, Billing, Behavior, etc.]
  - 1.4.1.3. Date & Time of Entry: [MM/DD/YYYY, HH:MM AM/PM]
  - 1.4.1.4. Patient/Visitor Name: [Patient or Visitor who lodged the complaint]
  - 1.4.1.5. Complaint Description: [Text entry for complaint description]
  - 1.4.1.6. Complaint Status: [Pending/Resolved/Under Investigation]
  - 1.4.1.7. Assigned Staff: [Name of staff member handling the complaint]
  - 1.4.1.8. Resolution Details: [Description of actions taken to resolve the complaint]
  - 1.4.1.9. Resolution Status: [Resolved/Pending]
  - 1.4.1.10. Follow-up Actions: [Link to follow-up actions or documentation]
- 1.4.2. Complaint Resolution History: P1
  - 1.4.2.1. Resolved Complaints: [List of complaints marked as resolved, with resolution dates]
  - 1.4.2.2. Pending Complaints: [List of unresolved complaints with expected resolution time]

- 1.4.2.3. Escalated Complaints: [List of complaints that were escalated to higher management or other departments]

## **1.5. Complaint Follow-up**

- 1.5.1. Complaint Follow-Up Process: P1
  - 1.5.1.1. Follow-Up Frequency: [Daily/Weekly/Monthly follow-ups for unresolved complaints]
  - 1.5.1.2. Assigned Follow-Up Staff: [List of personnel responsible for following up on complaints]
  - 1.5.1.3. Follow-Up Documentation: [Link to complaint follow-up records]
- 1.5.2. Feedback from Patients on Resolution:
  - 1.5.2.1. Survey Sent to Patient: [Yes/No]
  - 1.5.2.2. Patient Satisfaction: [Link to patient feedback form regarding complaint resolution]
  - 1.5.2.3. Feedback Analysis: [Summary of feedback received regarding complaint resolution]

## **1.6. Dashboard**

- 1.6.1. Automated Notifications: P1
  - 1.6.1.1. Automated alerts for:
    - 1.6.1.1.1. New complaints submitted by patients or visitors.
    - 1.6.1.1.2. Staff when complaints are assigned or escalated.
    - 1.6.1.1.3. Patients when complaint statuses are updated or resolved.
- 1.6.2. Real-Time Data: P1
  - 1.6.2.1. Access for authorized personnel to:
    - 1.6.2.1.1. All complaint records and their respective resolution statuses.
    - 1.6.2.1.2. Live updates on complaint status:
      - 1.6.2.1.2.1. Pending
      - 1.6.2.1.2.2. Resolved
      - 1.6.2.1.2.3. Under Investigation
- 1.6.3. Mobile Access: P1
  - 1.6.3.1. Capabilities for ED staff to:
    - 1.6.3.1.1. Register new complaints.
    - 1.6.3.1.2. Review existing complaints.
    - 1.6.3.1.3. Follow up on complaint resolutions via mobile devices.
  - 1.6.3.2. Capabilities for patients/visitors to:
    - 1.6.3.2.1. Submit complaints using the mobile app.
    - 1.6.3.2.2. Track complaint progress in real time.
- 1.6.4. Compliance Tracking: P1
  - 1.6.4.1. Ensure:
    - 1.6.4.1.1. Every complaint is logged, acknowledged, and resolved per hospital protocols.
    - 1.6.4.1.2. Timeliness of response and resolution for audit readiness.
- 1.6.5. Complaint Statistics: P1
  - 1.6.5.1. Total Complaints:
    - 1.6.5.1.1. Number of complaints lodged within a selected timeframe.
  - 1.6.5.2. Complaints by Type: P1
    - 1.6.5.2.1. Medical
    - 1.6.5.2.2. Billing
    - 1.6.5.2.3. Staff Behavior
    - 1.6.5.2.4. Facility/Environment

- 1.6.5.2.5. Other
  - 1.6.5.3. Complaints by Status: P1
    - 1.6.5.3.1. Pie chart showing distribution of:
      - 1.6.5.3.1.1. Pending
      - 1.6.5.3.1.2. Resolved
      - 1.6.5.3.1.3. Escalated
    - 1.6.5.4. Response Time: P1
      - 1.6.5.4.1. Average time taken to acknowledge and resolve complaints.
    - 1.6.5.5. Follow-Up Actions: P1
      - 1.6.5.5.1. Status and outcomes of follow-up actions taken for each complaint.
- 1.6.6. Complaint Resolution Dashboard: P1
  - 1.6.6.1. Resolved Complaints:
    - 1.6.6.1.1. Number of complaints closed within a specific period.
  - 1.6.6.2. Unresolved Complaints:
    - 1.6.6.2.1. Count of ongoing or open complaints.
  - 1.6.6.3. Escalation Rate:
    - 1.6.6.3.1. Percentage of complaints that required escalation for resolution.

## Continuous Quality Improvement (CQI) Module

![Illustration of a virtual meeting with four participants around a table, one on a screen, with speech bubbles indicating discussion.](be71a1754b1b2d51c246228dedc026c3_img.jpg)

An illustration depicting a virtual meeting or conference. Four people are seated around a light blue circular table. On the left, a woman in a yellow top and blue skirt sits with her back to the viewer, looking at a man in a black suit who is gesturing with his hands. On the right, a woman in a yellow top and a man in a blue suit are seated, both looking towards the center. A large screen at the top center shows a man in a blue suit on a video call. Speech bubbles with question marks, exclamation marks, and ellipses are positioned around the participants, indicating active discussion and interaction.

Illustration of a virtual meeting with four participants around a table, one on a screen, with speech bubbles indicating discussion.

## **CONTINUOUS QUALITY IMPROVEMENT (CQI) MODULE**

**NOTE: This module will be implemented in all clinical and non-clinical areas of the hospital**

### **1. Continuous Quality Improvement: P1**

#### **1.1.CQI Focal Person:**

##### **1.1.1.CQI Focal Person Nomination:**

- 1.1.1.1. Focal Person Name: [Enter name of the nominated CQI focal person]
- 1.1.1.2. Position/Title: [Enter the position of the CQI focal person]
- 1.1.1.3. Contact Information: [Phone/Email]
- 1.1.1.4. Nomination Date: [MM/DD/YYYY]
- 1.1.1.5. Responsibilities: [Link to detailed responsibilities of the CQI focal person]
- 1.1.1.6. CQI Meetings: [Link to meeting notes or calendar of CQI committee meetings]

##### **1.1.2.CQI Committee Participation:**

- 1.1.2.1. Committee Members: [List of all CQI committee members]
- 1.1.2.2. Last Committee Meeting: [Date of last CQI meeting]
- 1.1.2.3. Upcoming Meeting: [Scheduled date of next meeting]
- 1.1.2.4. Meeting Agenda: [Link to agenda]
- 1.1.2.5. Departmental KPIs with Benchmarks

#### **1.2.Departmental KPIs Defined: P1&P2**

##### **1.2.1.List of Key Performance Indicators (KPIs):**

- 1.2.1.1. [e.g., Patient Wait Time, Door-to-Doctor Time, Patient Satisfaction, Patient Discharge Time, etc.]
- 1.2.1.2. Target Benchmarks: [Target values for each KPI, e.g., Door-to-Doctor time should be under 15 minutes]

##### **1.2.2.KPI Categories:**

- 1.2.2.1. Clinical KPIs: [e.g., Time to First Resuscitation, Number of Critical Cases Managed]
- 1.2.2.2. Operational KPIs: [e.g., Bed Utilization, Equipment Functionality, Patient Throughput]
- 1.2.2.3. Patient Satisfaction KPIs: [e.g., Patient Complaints, Patient Feedback Scores]
- 1.2.2.4. Compliance KPIs: [e.g., Adherence to Clinical Guidelines, Medication Errors]

##### **1.2.3.KPI Performance Tracking:**

- 1.2.3.1. Current Status: [Graphical display of current performance against targets]
- 1.2.3.2. Historical Trends: [Trends over the past months/quarters]
- 1.2.3.3. Deviation from Benchmark: [Real-time alerts when KPIs fall below target benchmarks]
- 1.2.3.4. Real-Time Data: [Live updates for real-time KPI data collection]

### **1.3.Data Collection for Relevant KPIs-P1 & P2**

#### **1.3.1.KPI Data Collection Mechanism:**

##### **1.3.1.1. Data Sources:**

- 1.3.1.1.1. [Patient management systems, Electronic Health Records (EHR), ED logs]
- 1.3.1.2. Frequency of Data Collection: [e.g., Daily/Weekly/Monthly updates]
- 1.3.1.3. Responsible Staff for Data Entry: [List of staff responsible for KPI data collection]

- 1.3.1.4. Automated Data Collection: [Whether data is automatically captured via system or manually entered]
- 1.3.2. Data Entry & Monitoring:
  - 1.3.2.1. Live Data Feeds: [Real-time data tracking for each KPI]
  - 1.3.2.2. Reports & Dashboards: [Link to KPI dashboards or reports]
  - 1.3.2.3. Data Accuracy Checks: [Link to procedure for ensuring data accuracy]
- 1.3.3. Data Collection Status:
  - 1.3.3.1. Completed Entries: [Number of KPI data entries completed]
  - 1.3.3.2. Missing Data: [Number of missing data entries for a specific KPI]
  - 1.3.3.3. Automated Alerts: [Alerts triggered if data collection is incomplete or inconsistent]

## **1.4. Corrective Actions by CQI Committee-P1**

- 1.4.1. Corrective Actions for KPIs:
  - 1.4.1.1. KPI Performance Review: [Date of last review of KPI performance]
  - 1.4.1.2. Actions Taken:
    - 1.4.1.2.1. [e.g., Adjusted staffing levels, Improved patient flow protocols, Enhanced patient education materials]
  - 1.4.1.3. Responsible Personnel: [Name of the responsible department or individual]
  - 1.4.1.4. Action Status: [Implemented/Pending/Under Review]
- 1.4.2. Root Cause Analysis (RCA):
  - 1.4.2.1. Issue Identification: [Link to root cause analysis reports for performance deviations]
  - 1.4.2.2. Corrective Action Plan: [Link to detailed corrective action plan]
  - 1.4.2.3. Impact of Action: [Measure of the corrective action's impact on the KPIs]
- 1.4.3. CQI Action Monitoring:
  - 1.4.3.1. Action Completion Date: [Date when corrective actions were implemented]
  - 1.4.3.2. Follow-up Action Plan: [Link to follow-up actions or reviews for effectiveness]
  - 1.4.3.3. Review of Effectiveness: [Link to report evaluating the success of corrective actions]

## Fire & Non-Fire Emergency Module

![A photograph of a hospital hallway with a fire extinguisher in the foreground and medical staff in the background.](082d30318d74ea489bc115970c3e43da_img.jpg)

A photograph of a hospital hallway. In the foreground, a large red fire extinguisher stands on the polished floor. To its left is a blue hospital bed on wheels. In the background, three medical professionals wearing green scrubs and face masks are standing near a doorway. On the right wall, a large red circular object, possibly a fire hose reel, is visible. The hallway is brightly lit with recessed ceiling lights.

A photograph of a hospital hallway with a fire extinguisher in the foreground and medical staff in the background.

## [FIRE AND NON-FIRE EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT]

### 1. Fire and Non-Fire Emergency Management

#### 1.1. Fire & Non-Fire Emergency Plans: P1

- 1.1.1. Fire Emergency Plan Available:
  - 1.1.1.1. Status: [Available/Not Available]
  - 1.1.1.2. Last Updated: [Date of last update]
  - 1.1.1.3. Plan Document: [Link to detailed fire emergency plan]
- 1.1.2.9.1.2. Non-Fire Emergency Plan Available:
  - 1.1.2.1. Status: [Available/Not Available]
  - 1.1.2.2. Last Updated: [Date of last update]
  - 1.1.2.3. Plan Document: [Link to detailed non-fire emergency plan]

#### 1.2. Evacuation Plan: P1

- 1.2.1. Safe Evacuation Plan Displayed:
  - 1.2.1.1. Status: [Displayed/Not Displayed]
  - 1.2.1.2. Location: [Location where evacuation plan is displayed in ED]
  - 1.2.1.3. Last Inspection: [Date of last inspection]
  - 1.2.1.4. Next Inspection: [Scheduled date for next inspection]

#### 1.3. Mock Drills: P1

- 1.3.1. Evidence of Staff Attending Mock Drill:
  - 1.3.1.1. Date of Last Mock Drill: [MM/DD/YYYY]
  - 1.3.1.2. Participants: [List of staff who attended]
  - 1.3.1.3. Drill Type: [Fire/Non-Fire]
  - 1.3.1.4. Feedback: [Link to feedback or evaluation of the drill]
  - 1.3.1.5. Next Mock Drill Scheduled: [Date of next scheduled drill]
- 1.3.2. Attendance Records: P1
  - 1.3.2.1. Mock Drill Attendance: [Link to digital attendance sheet for all drills]
  - 1.3.2.2. Training Completion: [Completion status for all ED staff]

#### 1.4. Staff Training on Fire & Non-Fire Emergency Plans: P1

- 1.4.1. Fire Safety Training for Staff:
  - 1.4.1.1. Training Status: [Completed/Incomplete]
  - 1.4.1.2. Last Training Date: [MM/DD/YYYY]
  - 1.4.1.3. Next Training Scheduled: [MM/DD/YYYY]
  - 1.4.1.4. Trained Staff: [List of staff trained]
  - 1.4.1.5. Training Module: [Link to fire safety training materials]
- 1.4.2. Non-Fire Emergency Training for Staff:
  - 1.4.2.1. Training Status: [Completed/Incomplete]
  - 1.4.2.2. Last Training Date: [MM/DD/YYYY]
  - 1.4.2.3. Next Training Scheduled: [MM/DD/YYYY]
  - 1.4.2.4. Trained Staff: [List of staff trained]
  - 1.4.2.5. Training Module: [Link to non-fire emergency training materials]

#### 1.5. Firefighting Equipment Availability: P1

- 1.5.1. Sufficient Firefighting Equipment Available:

- 1.5.1.1. Status: [Sufficient/Insufficient]
- 1.5.1.2. Inspection Status: [Functional/Under Maintenance]
- 1.5.1.3. Last Inspection Date: [MM/DD/YYYY]
- 1.5.1.4. Next Inspection Date: [MM/DD/YYYY]
- 1.5.2. Number of Fire Extinguishers:
  - 1.5.2.1. Total Number of Fire Extinguishers: [Enter number of fire extinguishers available in ED]
  - 1.5.2.2. Fire Extinguishers in Service: [Number of fire extinguishers currently operational]
  - 1.5.2.3. Fire Extinguishers Requiring Maintenance: [Number of fire extinguishers under maintenance]

## 1.6. Equipment Maintenance

- 1.6.1. Fire Safety Equipment Maintenance: P1
  - 1.6.1.1. Fire Extinguisher Maintenance: [Scheduled/Completed]
  - 1.6.1.2. Fire Alarm Testing: [Scheduled/Completed]
  - 1.6.1.3. Sprinkler System Inspection: [Scheduled/Completed]
  - 1.6.1.4. Emergency Exit Signage Inspection: [Scheduled/Completed]
  - 1.6.1.5. Fire Extinguishers expiry alert: [MM/DD/YYYY]

## 1.7. Fire & Emergency Safety Reports: P1

- 1.7.1. Fire Safety Inspection Report:
  - 1.7.1.1. Last Report Date: [MM/DD/YYYY]
  - 1.7.1.2. Inspection Findings: [Link to digital fire safety inspection report]
  - 1.7.1.3. Next Scheduled Report: [MM/DD/YYYY]
- 1.7.2. Emergency Equipment Status Report:
  - 1.7.2.1. Last Report Date: [MM/DD/YYYY]
  - 1.7.2.2. Functional Equipment: [Number of functional fire safety equipment]
  - 1.7.2.3. Non-Functional Equipment: [Number of non-functional fire safety equipment]
  - 1.7.2.4. Maintenance Status: [List of equipment requiring maintenance]

## 1.8. Fire and Non-Fire Emergency Management

- 1.8.1. Real-Time Notifications: P1
  - 1.8.1.1. Alerts for:
    - 1.8.1.1.1. Upcoming mock drills.
    - 1.8.1.1.2. Scheduled fire safety training sessions.
    - 1.8.1.1.3. Fire equipment maintenance and expiry dates.
  - 1.8.1.2. Notifications triggered when:
    - 1.8.1.2.1. Fire equipment needs recharging.
    - 1.8.1.2.2. Fire extinguishers or alarms require servicing.
- 1.8.2. Automated Scheduling: P1
  - 1.8.2.1. System-generated schedules for:
    - 1.8.2.1.1. Mock drills.
    - 1.8.2.1.2. Fire safety training programs.
    - 1.8.2.1.3. Routine fire equipment inspections.
  - 1.8.2.2. Alerts for:
    - 1.8.2.2.1. Missed or overdue drills and training sessions.
