## **HMIS DASHBOARD MODULE**

### **1 Administrator Dashboard**

#### **1.1 Real-Time Operational Overview**

- 1.1.1 Hospital Bed Occupancy: - P1
  - 1.1.1.1 Total bed capacity, occupied vs. vacant beds.
  - 1.1.1.2 Department/ward-wise bed occupancy.
  - 1.1.1.3 ICU, emergency, and isolation bed availability.
- 1.1.2 Patient Data: - P1
  - 1.1.2.1 Current number of inpatients, admissions, and discharges.
  - 1.1.2.2 Average Length of Stay (ALOS) for various departments/wards.
  - 1.1.2.3 Emergency room patient wait times and volume. – P2

#### **1.2 Patient Flow Management**

- 1.2.1 Admission and Discharge Summary: - P1
  - 1.2.1.1 Total admissions, discharges, and transfers (including reasons).
  - 1.2.1.2 Patient discharge status (pending, in progress).
- 1.2.2 Referral Data: - P2
  - 1.2.2.1 Internal vs. external patient referrals.
  - 1.2.2.2 Referral patterns and reasons for referrals.

#### **1.3 Departmental Performance**

- 1.3.1 Clinical Department Metrics: - P1
  - 1.3.1.1 Admissions and discharge data by department (e.g., Cardiology, Oncology, Orthopedics).
  - 1.3.1.2 Department-specific KPIs (e.g., mortality rates, readmission rates).
  - 1.3.1.3 Consultant-wise patient load and performance data. – P2
- 1.3.2 Operational Efficiency: - P2
  - 1.3.2.1 Performance of high-demand departments like the Emergency Department (ED), Surgical Services, Laboratory, Imaging, and Pharmacy.

#### **1.4 Resource Utilization**

- 1.4.1 Staffing Levels and Productivity: - P1
  - 1.4.1.1 Staffing metrics by department: nurse-to-patient ratio, physician availability, technician load.
  - 1.4.1.2 Productivity tracking (surgeries performed, tests completed, etc.).
- 1.4.2 Medical Equipment Usage: - P2
  - 1.4.2.1 Utilization rates for high-cost equipment (e.g., MRI, CT scanner, ventilators).
  - 1.4.2.2 Equipment downtime, maintenance status, and scheduled maintenance.
- 1.4.3 Consumable and Supply Management: - P1
  - 1.4.3.1 Tracking usage of consumables (e.g., syringes, medications, surgical supplies).
  - 1.4.3.2 Stock levels for critical medical supplies.

#### **1.5 Financial Management**

- 1.5.1 Revenue and Billing Overview: - P2

- 1.5.1.1 Daily/weekly/monthly revenue from inpatient, outpatient, and surgical services.
- 1.5.1.2 Billing status: outstanding bills, pending insurance claims, and payment collection.
- 1.5.1.3 Cost per patient per department (e.g., surgery, ICU, lab tests).
- 1.5.1.4 Hospital operational costs vs. revenue generated.
- 1.5.2 Insurance Data: - P2
  - 1.5.2.1 Breakdown of insurance claims processed, approved, or pending.
  - 1.5.2.2 Insurance reimbursement rates and collection performance.

## **1.6 Clinical Outcomes and Quality Indicators**

- 1.6.1 Patient Safety Metrics: - P2
  - 1.6.1.1 Adverse event tracking (e.g., medication errors, falls, infections).
  - 1.6.1.2 Mortality and morbidity rates by department or condition.
- 1.6.2 Clinical Quality Indicators: - P2
  - 1.6.2.1 Surgical complication rates, readmission rates.
  - 1.6.2.2 Compliance with clinical protocols (e.g., surgical checklists, sepsis protocols).
  - 1.6.2.3 Infection rates (e.g., hospital-acquired infections).
- 1.6.3 Patient Satisfaction Scores: - P3
  - 1.6.3.1 Real-time patient feedback on care quality (patient surveys, complaints).
  - 1.6.3.2 Wait times for consultations, tests, and procedures.

## **1.7 Compliance and Accreditation Monitoring**

- 1.7.1 Regulatory Compliance Reports: - P1
  - 1.7.1.1 Compliance with PHC standards and regulations
  - 1.7.1.2 Documentation of staff certifications, training, and continuing education.
  - 1.7.1.3 Audit status for departments (e.g., Infection Control, Laboratory, Radiology).

## **1.8 Operational and Administrative Alerts**

- 1.8.1 Critical Alerting System: - P3
  - 1.8.1.1 Alerts for pending discharges or delayed surgeries.
  - 1.8.1.2 Bed status alerts (e.g., if a high-priority patient needs a specific bed).
  - 1.8.1.3 Equipment failure or maintenance alerts.
  - 1.8.1.4 Staff shortages or absenteeism.
- 1.8.2 Delays and Workflow Bottlenecks: - P3
  - 1.8.2.1 Real-time alerts for delays in patient care, diagnostics, or treatment procedures.
  - 1.8.2.2 Monitoring of patient transfers and waiting times.

## **1.9 Emergency Preparedness & Disaster Management**

- 1.9.1 Emergency Room Status: - P1
  - 1.9.1.1 Patient triage and waiting times.
  - 1.9.1.2 Emergency procedure and patient flow management.
  - 1.9.1.3 Availability of critical resources (e.g., ventilators, ICU beds).
- 1.9.2 Disaster Response Readiness: - P3
  - 1.9.2.1 Tracking disaster preparedness protocols.

- 1.9.2.2 Availability of emergency medical kits, blood supply, and other critical resources.

## **1.10 Data Visualization**

- 1.10.1 Advanced Analytics & Reporting: - P3
  - 1.10.1.1 Trend analysis (e.g., readmission rates, seasonal disease outbreaks).
  - 1.10.1.2 Predictive analytics (e.g., predicting patient volumes, demand for beds).
  - 1.10.1.3 Real-time data visualization (charts, graphs) for monitoring key metrics (e.g., hospital occupancy, wait times, patient satisfaction).

## **1.11 Reporting and Decision Support**

- 1.11.1 Executive Summary Reports: - P1
  - 1.11.1.1 Summary of hospital performance metrics, financials, patient care outcomes, and key operational indicators.
  - 1.11.1.2 Periodic reports (daily/weekly/monthly/quarterly) with insights into critical areas for administration decision-making.
- 1.11.2 Strategic Planning Support: - P3
  - 1.11.2.1 Dashboard-driven insights for long-term hospital planning (e.g., capacity planning, budget forecasting, staff recruitment).

## **1.12 Mobile and Remote Access**

- 1.12.1 Mobile-Friendly Dashboards: - P3
  - 1.12.1.1 Accessible on mobile devices for MS and senior hospital staff for real-time updates.
  - 1.12.1.2 Alerts and notifications that can be pushed to mobile devices for immediate attention.

## **2 OPD Dashboard**

- 2.1.1 Daily patients' summary: - P1
  - 2.1.1.1 Number of patients registered, visited, canceled, and no-shows.
- 2.1.2 Department-wise OPD load: - P1
  - 2.1.2.1 Cardiology, Orthopedics, etc.
- 2.1.3 Doctor utilization: - P2
  - 2.1.3.1 Consultations/hour, idle time.
- 2.1.4 Revenue summary: - P2
  - 2.1.4.1 Consultation fees collected, department-wise earnings.
- 2.1.5 Waiting time analysis: - P3
  - 2.1.5.1 Average & peak hours.
- 2.1.6 Appointment trends: - P2
  - 2.1.6.1 Hourly/daily/weekly.
- 2.1.7 Personal schedule: - P1
  - 2.1.7.1 List of appointments with time slots and patient details.
- 2.1.8 Queue management: - P3
  - 2.1.8.1 Real-time updates on waiting patients.
- 2.1.9 Consultation timer: - P3

2.1.9.1 For time tracking and analytics.

## **2.2 Functional Components of the OPD Dashboard**

### **2.2.1 Real-Time Metrics – P2**

2.2.1.1 Number of patients currently waiting.

2.2.1.2 Average consultation time per doctor.

2.2.1.3 Current waiting time.

2.2.1.4 Peak hour indicator.

2.2.1.5 Delayed appointments or skipped ones.

### **2.2.2 Appointments Management – P2**

2.2.2.1 Slots view: Daily/weekly/monthly doctor schedule.

2.2.2.2 Filter: Department, doctor, patient name, time slot.

2.2.2.3 Appointment type: Walk-in, scheduled, telemedicine.

2.2.2.4 Status: Booked, checked-in, seen, canceled, no-show.

### **2.2.3 Patient Flow Tracking – P3**

2.2.3.1 **Patient journey mapping:** Registered → Waiting → Consultation → Billing → Pharmacy → Exit.

2.2.3.2 Bottleneck analysis.

2.2.3.3 Time spent at each stage.

### **2.2.4 Doctor Performance KPIs – P3**

2.2.4.1 No. of consultations.

2.2.4.2 Patient satisfaction score (if feedback is collected).

2.2.4.3 Time per consultation.

2.2.4.4 Prescription turnaround.

### **2.2.5 Operational & Support Features – P2**

2.2.6 **Queue display integration:** Screens in the OPD waiting area showing real-time queue status.

2.2.7 **SMS/email alerts:** For appointment reminders, delays, or follow-ups.

2.2.8 **Token system integration:** Auto-token generation and calling.

2.2.9 **Language/localization support:** Multilingual UI (especially for patient-facing panels).

## **3 Emergency Department Dashboard:**

### **3.1 Patient Flow & Triage Management**

#### **3.1.1 Real-Time Patient Status: - P1**

3.1.1.1 Overview of all patients currently in the ED:

3.1.1.1.1 Patient ID, name, age, arrival time, and triage category (e.g., critical, urgent, non-urgent).

3.1.1.1.2 Current status (e.g., awaiting doctor assessment, undergoing treatment, waiting for tests, discharged).

3.1.1.1.3 Diagnosis and attending physician.

#### **3.1.2 Triage & Severity Levels: - P1**

- 3.1.2.1 Display the number of patients in each triage category (e.g., Red, Yellow, Green) to monitor the overall severity and prioritize critical cases.
- 3.1.2.2 Visual indicators of patient severity to ensure timely treatment and monitoring of critical patients.
- 3.1.2.3 Track waiting times by triage category, allowing the MS to identify potential bottlenecks in the ED workflow.
- 3.1.3 Patient Wait Times & Turnaround Time: - P3
  - 3.1.3.1 Real-time tracking of patient waiting times in the ED, from arrival to being seen by a doctor, from consultation to diagnostics, and from diagnostics to treatment.
  - 3.1.3.2 Average turnaround time per patient and by department (e.g., assessment, imaging, lab tests).
  - 3.1.3.3 Alerts for patients who have been waiting longer than expected (e.g., exceeding set thresholds for each triage category).

## **3.2 Bed & Resource Availability**

- 3.2.1 ED Bed Occupancy & Flow: - P1
  - 3.2.1.1 Real-time overview of ED bed availability, including the number of occupied and vacant beds.
  - 3.2.1.2 Tracking of patients being transferred to or from the ED (e.g., to/from the ICU, admission to the hospital ward).
  - 3.2.1.3 Ability to track bed turnover and ensure that critical patients are moved to appropriate care areas without delay.
- 3.2.2 Equipment Availability & Usage: - P3
  - 3.2.2.1 Monitor the availability of critical ED equipment such as ventilators, ECG machines, defibrillators, and infusion pumps.
  - 3.2.2.2 Real-time alerts for equipment in use, under maintenance, or unavailable.
  - 3.2.2.3 Track consumables like oxygen supply, medications, and IV fluids to avoid shortages.
- 3.2.3 Imaging & Lab Resources: - P1
  - 3.2.3.1 Real-time tracking of diagnostic imaging and lab test orders for ED patients (e.g., CT scans, X-rays, blood tests).
  - 3.2.3.2 Monitor turnaround times for diagnostics and lab results to avoid delays in patient care.

## **3.3 Staffing Management & Operational Efficiency**

- 3.3.1 Staffing Overview: - P1
  - 3.3.1.1 Real-time view of staffing in the ED, including the number of doctors, nurses, and support staff on duty.
  - 3.3.1.2 Track shift coverage for physicians, nurses, technicians, and other support personnel to ensure adequate staffing during peak hours.
  - 3.3.1.3 Display the number of available staff against the patient load to monitor staffing adequacy.
  - 3.3.1.4 Workload Management: - P2
  - 3.3.1.5 Monitor the workload distribution among staff (e.g., number of patients assigned to each doctor or nurse).

- 3.3.1.6 Alerts for staffing shortages or workload imbalances, particularly during high-volume times or critical cases.
- 3.3.1.7 Track average response time of staff to patient requests or emergencies (e.g., Code Blue alerts).
- 3.3.1.8 Staff Performance: - P3
- 3.3.1.9 Track key performance indicators (KPIs) for ED staff such as average patient assessment time, time to administer treatment, and patient satisfaction ratings.
- 3.3.1.10 Monitoring compliance with clinical protocols and adherence to treatment guidelines.
- 3.3.1.11 Track any incidents or safety events involving ED staff (e.g., medication errors, patient falls).

### **3.4 Patient Care & Treatment Monitoring**

- 3.4.1 Clinical Care Status: - P3
  - 3.4.1.1 Overview of the status of each patient in the ED (e.g., undergoing examination, awaiting lab results, in treatment, ready for discharge).
- 3.4.2 Treatment Protocols & Order Tracking: - P3
  - 3.4.2.1 Track patient treatment plans, including medications, procedures, and interventions.
  - 3.4.2.2 Ensure compliance with evidence-based clinical guidelines and hospital protocols.
  - 3.4.2.3 Real-time tracking of diagnostic test results, imaging reports, and treatment progress.
- 3.4.3 Patient Disposition:- P1
  - 3.4.3.1 Track patient disposition decisions: discharge, admission to the hospital, or transfer to another facility (e.g., ICU).
  - 3.4.3.2 Monitor discharge times and delays to optimize patient turnover and reduce ED congestion.

### **3.5 Emergency Response & Code Management**

- 3.5.1 Code Alerts & Response Times: - P3
  - 3.5.1.1 Track emergency codes (e.g., Code Blue, Code Red) and ensure prompt staff response.
  - 3.5.1.2 Real-time display of response times for codes, with metrics on the time taken for staff to reach the patient, initiate intervention, and stabilize the patient.
  - 3.5.1.3 Ability to trigger automatic alerts to the required team members when a code is activated.
- 3.5.2 Emergency Protocol Compliance: - P2
  - 3.5.2.1 Monitor adherence to emergency care protocols (e.g., trauma care, cardiac arrest protocols).
  - 3.5.2.2 Track time-sensitive procedures such as thrombolysis in stroke patients or trauma resuscitation.

### **3.6 Patient Outcomes & Satisfaction**

- 3.6.1 Patient Recovery & Outcome Tracking: - P2

6.1.1 **Daily/Weekly/Monthly Test Volume** (number of lab tests and imaging procedures)

6.1.2 **Procedure Type Breakdown:**

6.1.2.1 Lab: Blood tests, urine tests, microbiology, biochemistry, etc.

6.1.2.2 Imaging: X-rays, MRIs, CT scans, ultrasounds, etc.

6.1.3 **Turnaround Time** for each type of test/procedure (time from sample collection to results delivery)

6.1.4 **Utilization Rates** (for each diagnostic modality, e.g., imaging machines, lab instruments)

6.1.5 **Peak Hours of Usage** (most frequent times for tests/procedures, to balance workloads)

**6.2 Test/Procedure Turnaround and Pending Results – P1**

6.2.1 **Average Turnaround Time (TAT)** for different tests/procedures

6.2.2 **Pending Test/Procedure Results** (number of tests/procedures yet to be processed)

6.2.3 **Overdue Tests** (tests that are delayed beyond the expected timeframe)

6.2.4 **Urgent/STAT Test Alerts** (highlight priority cases that need immediate processing)

**6.3 Resource Management – P2**

6.3.1 **Equipment Availability** (status of imaging equipment like MRIs, CT scanners, ultrasound machines, etc.)

6.3.2 **Equipment Maintenance Schedules** (alerts for routine maintenance or breakdowns)

6.3.3 **Staffing Levels** (lab technicians, radiologists, pathologists, imaging technologists)

6.3.4 **Staff Efficiency Metrics** (e.g., number of tests handled per technician)

6.3.5 **Consumable Inventory** (tracking supplies like reagents, imaging contrast agents, etc.)

6.3.6 **Equipment Utilization Rates** (how frequently specific equipment is in use)

**6.4 Diagnostic Accuracy & Quality Control – P3**

6.4.1 **Error Rate Tracking** (incidence of sample collection, processing, or interpretation errors)

6.4.2 **Quality Control Reports** (for both lab tests and imaging)

6.4.3 **Test Result Review Status** (whether results are reviewed and verified by pathologists or radiologists)

6.4.4 **Proficiency Testing Compliance** (for labs, ensuring compliance with proficiency standards)

**6.5 Financial Overview – P3**

6.5.1 **Revenue Generated from Diagnostics** (by department, test type, imaging modality)

6.5.2 **Cost of Tests/Procedures** (per test/procedure or per department)

6.5.3 **Insurance Claims** (status of insurance billing, approved claims, and outstanding payments)

6.5.4 **Cost per Test/Procedure** (to monitor operational efficiency and potential cost-cutting measures)

**6.6 Test Distribution – P3**

6.6.1 **Department/Unit-Wise Test Breakdown** (how tests are distributed across different departments like OPD, IPD, emergency, etc.) – P1

6.6.2 **Insurance vs. Self-Pay Breakdown** (payment type for lab and imaging services)

6.6.3 **Referral Patterns** (whether tests are referred from other hospitals or clinics)

**6.7 Lab & Imaging Performance Indicators – P3**

6.7.1 **Test Completion Rates** (successful tests completed vs. canceled or failed)

6.7.2 **Referral Case Volume** (how many tests come from referrals or outside hospitals)

- 6.7.3 **Patient Feedback** (for both lab and imaging services—wait times, communication, service quality)
- 6.7.4 **Average Cost per Test/Procedure** (by department, modality, or equipment)
- 6.7.5 **Patient Wait Time** for both lab results and imaging procedures
- 6.8 **Equipment and Staff Alerts** – P3
  - 6.8.1 **Equipment Downtime Alerts** (for imaging equipment breakdowns or maintenance)
  - 6.8.2 **Staff Absences** (for key staff in the lab or imaging department)
  - 6.8.3 **Urgent Equipment Requests** (alerts when specific machines or instruments are urgently needed)
- 6.8.4 **Test Result Approval Delays** (alerts for when results are not signed off by responsible clinicians or radiologists)
- 6.9 **Report Generation and Data Visualization** – P2
  - 6.9.1 **Customizable Reports** (for daily, weekly, monthly performance)
  - 6.9.2 **Data Visualizations** (charts and graphs for test volume, turnaround time, and performance)
  - 6.9.3 **Benchmarking** (comparison of department performance against best practices or national standards)
  - 6.9.4 **Trends** (test volume growth, equipment usage trends, demand spikes)

## 7 Pharmacy Dashboard:

### 7.1 Pharmacy Inventory Management

- 7.1.1 **Current Inventory Status:** - P1
  - 7.1.1.1 Real-time view of stock levels for all drugs and consumables (e.g., antibiotics, analgesics, surgical supplies).
  - 7.1.1.2 Alerts for low stock, out-of-stock, or overstocked medications.
  - 7.1.1.3 Stock expiration dates and expiry management (e.g., alerting for medications nearing expiry).
  - 7.1.1.4 Breakdown of inventory by location (e.g., inpatient pharmacy, outpatient pharmacy, ICU, surgical wards).
- 7.1.2 **Drug Procurement and Supply Chain:** - P3
  - 7.1.2.1 Overview of recent and upcoming drug procurement.
  - 7.1.2.2 Tracking of supplier performance, order fulfillment, and delivery times.
  - 7.1.2.3 Purchase orders, backorders, and delayed shipments.
  - 7.1.2.4 Integration with external suppliers and wholesalers for streamlined procurement.
- 7.1.3 **Drug Usage Trends:** - P3
  - 7.1.3.1 Data on high-demand medications or seasonal fluctuations (e.g., flu season medication requirements).
  - 7.1.3.2 Medication consumption rates over time to forecast future demand and reordering needs.

### 7.2 Medication Dispensing & Tracking

- 7.2.1 **Medication Orders Overview:** - P3

- 7.2.1.1 Real-time tracking of medication orders by clinicians (e.g., prescriptions, IVs, chemotherapy orders).
    - 7.2.1.2 Number of orders processed vs. pending orders.
    - 7.2.1.3 Timeliness of medication dispensing (e.g., average dispensing time).
  - 7.2.2 Medication Dispensing Efficiency: - P2
    - 7.2.2.1 Dispensing errors, missed doses, or drug mis-dispensing incidents.
    - 7.2.2.2 Refill requests and tracking.
  - 7.2.3 Drug Administration Compliance: - P3
    - 7.2.3.1 Monitor and ensure that medications are being administered according to prescribed dosages, schedules, and routes.
    - 7.2.3.2 Alerts for missed doses or wrong medications.
- ### **7.3 Drug Safety & Adverse Event Monitoring**
- 7.3.1 Adverse Drug Reactions (ADR) Monitoring: - P1
    - 7.3.1.1 Track and manage reported adverse drug reactions (ADRs) by drug, patient, and severity.
    - 7.3.1.2 Alerts for significant ADRs or new reactions.
    - 7.3.1.3 Maintain and generate ADR reports for regulatory compliance.
  - 7.3.2 Drug Interactions: - P2
    - 7.3.2.1 Real-time alerts on potential drug-drug interactions based on patient medication orders.
    - 7.3.2.2 Warnings for contraindicated drug combinations.
  - 7.3.3 Controlled Substances Management: - P3
    - 7.3.3.1 Real-time monitoring of controlled substances, including usage, stock levels, and wastage.
    - 7.3.3.2 Ensure compliance with narcotic control regulations (e.g., tracking prescriptions, and controlled drug logs).
- ### **7.4 Medication Cost & Financial Overview**
- 7.4.1 Pharmacy Expenses and Revenue: - P3
    - 7.4.1.1 Overview of the total cost of medications dispensed to inpatients and outpatients.
    - 7.4.1.2 Monitoring of revenue generated from pharmacy sales (medications, over-the-counter items).
    - 7.4.1.3 Tracking of insurance claims for medication costs.
  - 7.4.2 Cost per Medication: - P3
    - 7.4.2.1 Average cost per medication or therapy.
    - 7.4.2.2 Cost comparisons across generic vs. brand-name drugs.
    - 7.4.2.3 Medication cost vs. reimbursement trends.
  - 7.4.3 Inventory Turnover: - P2
    - 7.4.3.1 Metrics for the efficiency of inventory usage, helping to control wastage and optimize stock.
  - 7.4.4 Drug Cost Optimization: - P3

- 7.4.4.1 Identify opportunities for cost savings (e.g., switching to generics, bulk procurement, or reducing wastage).

## **7.5 Pharmacy Workforce Management**

- 7.5.1 Pharmacy Staff Efficiency: - P1
  - 7.5.1.1 Monitoring of workload distribution across pharmacy staff
  - 7.5.1.2 Tracking staff working hours, absences, and overtime.

## **7.6 Pharmacy Workflow Optimization**

- 7.6.1 Order Processing Time: - P3
  - 7.6.1.1 Average time to process medication orders and prescription fulfillment.
  - 7.6.1.2 Bottlenecks in the pharmacy workflow, such as delays in medication preparation or dispensing.
- 7.6.2 Medication Reconciliation: - P3
  - 7.6.2.1 Ensure accurate medication reconciliation between hospital admission, transfer, and discharge.

## **7.7 Data Analytics & Reporting**

- 7.7.1 Customized Reports: - P2
  - 7.7.1.1 Real-time reporting on key metrics such as drug utilization, cost analysis, and medication errors.
  - 7.7.1.2 Periodic and customizable reports (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly, or quarterly) for inventory management, clinical outcomes, and financial performance.
- 7.7.2 Trend Analysis & Predictive Analytics: - P3
  - 7.7.2.1 Drug utilization trends (e.g., seasonal demand, high-demand drugs).
  - 7.7.2.2 Predictive analytics for forecasting medication demand, inventory needs, and staffing levels.
- 7.7.3 KPIs for Pharmacy Operations: - P3
  - 7.7.3.1 Track key performance indicators such as order fulfillment time, medication errors, inventory turnover, and cost savings from formulary management.

# **8 ICU Dashboard:**

## **8.1 Real-Time ICU Beds Tracking**

- 8.1.1 ICU Bed Occupancy & Availability: - P1
  - 8.1.1.1 Real-time view of ICU bed occupancy, showing which beds are occupied and which are available.
  - 8.1.1.2 A visual indicator of patient admission, discharge, and transfer status.
  - 8.1.1.3 Monitoring of bed turnover and waiting times for admission in critical cases.

## **8.2 Resource Utilization & Availability**

- 8.2.1 Equipment & Resource Status: - P2
  - 8.2.1.1 Real-time tracking of ICU-specific medical equipment availability (e.g., ventilators, ECG monitors, infusion pumps, dialysis machines).
  - 8.2.1.2 Alerts for equipment that is in use, being repaired, or requires maintenance.
  - 8.2.1.3 Stock levels of consumables, such as oxygen, medical supplies, medications, and personal protective equipment (PPE).

## **8.3 Staff Management**

- 8.3.1 ICU Staff Overview: - P1
  - 8.3.1.1 Real-time tracking of ICU staffing, showing shifts for doctors, nurses, and support staff (e.g., respiratory therapists, physiotherapists).
  - 8.3.1.2 Staffing levels against ICU patient load (e.g., nurse-to-patient ratio).
  - 8.3.1.3 Alerts for staff shortages, particularly during peak times or emergencies.
- 8.3.2 Staff Workload Management: - P2
  - 8.3.2.1 Monitor work hours, shift changes, and staff performance.
  - 8.3.2.2 Ensure that staff are not overburdened and have adequate rest periods.
- 8.3.3 Staff Performance Metrics: - P3
  - 8.3.3.1 Key performance indicators (KPIs) such as response times to patient needs, patient recovery rates, and adherence to ICU protocols.
  - 8.3.3.2 Analytics for staff performance and operational efficiency (e.g., average response time to a code blue or patient needs).

## **8.4 Bed Management & Admission/Discharge Flow**

- 8.4.1 Admission & Discharge Monitoring: - P1
  - 8.4.1.1 Real-time view of patient admission and discharge statuses within the ICU.
  - 8.4.1.2 Integration with hospital bed management system to monitor available ICU beds and waiting times for critical admissions.
  - 8.4.1.3 Ability to track bed turnover rate and manage emergency admissions efficiently.
- 8.4.2 ICU Transfer & Discharge Alerts: - P2
  - 8.4.2.1 Alerts when a patient is eligible for transfer out of the ICU (e.g., stable enough for general ward).
  - 8.4.2.2 Real-time tracking of patient transfers to or from the ICU, ensuring continuity of care.

## **8.5 Clinical Workflow & Protocol Adherence**

- 8.5.1 Clinical Protocol Compliance: - P3
  - 8.5.1.1 Track adherence to ICU protocols (e.g., infection control protocols, ventilator weaning protocols).
  - 8.5.1.2 Monitor for deviations from recommended clinical practices (e.g., frequency of repositioning patients on ventilators, use of antiseptics).
- 8.5.2 Diagnostic & Imaging Requests: - P2
  - 8.5.2.1 Monitor diagnostic requests (e.g., lab tests, radiology images) and their timely completion.

## **8.6 Data Analytics & Reporting**

- 8.6.1 ICU Performance Metrics: - P1
  - 8.6.1.1 Key performance indicators (KPIs) for ICU management, such as:
    - 8.6.1.1.1 Patient admission rates, average length of stay (ALOS), ICU occupancy rate.
    - 8.6.1.1.2 Mortality and morbidity rates.
    - 8.6.1.1.3 Rate of hospital-acquired infections (HAIs) in the ICU.
- 8.6.2 Resource Utilization & Efficiency: - P2
  - 8.6.2.1 Track the utilization rate of ICU resources (e.g., ventilators, infusion pumps, dialysis machines).

- 8.6.2.2 Monitor the cost of ICU care, including staffing, medication, equipment, and consumables.
- 8.6.3 Patient Outcome Analytics: - P2
  - 8.6.3.1 Analyze patient outcomes such as recovery rates, time to discharge, mortality rates, and complications.
  - 8.6.3.2 Report on ICU-related complications like infections, pressure ulcers, and ventilator-associated pneumonia.
- 8.6.4 Customizable Reports: - P2
  - 8.6.4.1 Generate daily, weekly, or monthly reports on patient status, resource utilization, staff performance, and outcomes.
  - 8.6.4.2 Reports on ICU efficiency and operational bottlenecks, identifying areas for improvement.

## **8.7 Infection Control & Safety Monitoring**

- 8.7.1 Infection Control Tracking: - P3
  - 8.7.1.1 Monitor infection rates within the ICU, such as ventilator-associated pneumonia (VAP) and central line-associated bloodstream infections (CLABSI).
  - 8.7.1.2 Track infection control measures being taken, including hand hygiene compliance, use of personal protective equipment (PPE), and disinfection practices.
- 8.7.2 Safety Incident Reporting: - P3
  - 8.7.2.1 Real-time tracking of safety incidents (e.g., falls, medication errors, equipment malfunction).
  - 8.7.2.2 Alerts for potential safety concerns, ensuring timely response and resolution.

## **9 Infection Prevention and Healthcare Waste Management (HWM) Dashboard:**

### **9.1 Infection Prevention Monitoring**

- 9.1.1 Infection Surveillance Data
  - 9.1.1.1 Real-Time Infection Data: - P2
    - 9.1.1.1.1 Display real-time data on hospital-acquired infections (HAIs) such as urinary tract infections (UTIs), ventilator-associated pneumonia (VAP), surgical site infections (SSIs), bloodstream infections (BSI), and Clostridium difficile infections (CDI).
    - 9.1.1.1.2 Track the number of infections per department (e.g., ICU, surgery, general wards) and the total infection burden in the hospital.
  - 9.1.1.2 Infection Rates and Trends: - P3
    - 9.1.1.2.1 Display the monthly/quarterly/yearly infection rates and trends, enabling comparisons with historical data to monitor effectiveness of infection prevention measures.
    - 9.1.1.2.2 Provide visual tools like line charts, heat maps, and bar graphs to display infection rates by departments, units, or infection types.
  - 9.1.1.3 Pathogen Data: - P3

9.1.1.3.1 Track and display the specific pathogens responsible for hospital-acquired infections, including bacterial, viral, and fungal organisms.

9.1.1.3.2 Correlate the data with antimicrobial resistance (AMR) data to identify trends in resistant infections.

## 9.1.2 Infection Prevention Protocols Compliance

### 9.1.2.1 Hand Hygiene Compliance: - P3

9.1.2.1.1 Monitor hand hygiene compliance rates among healthcare workers across departments and units.

9.1.2.1.2 Provide real-time feedback on hand hygiene practices.

9.1.2.1.3 Display compliance data by department and individual staff members, with visual representations of performance (e.g., bar graphs, color-coded indicators).

### 9.1.2.2 Isolation Precautions & PPE Usage: - P3

9.1.2.2.1 Track the use of personal protective equipment (PPE) (e.g., masks, gloves, gowns) across various departments, particularly in high-risk areas like ICU, emergency, and surgery.

9.1.2.2.2 Monitor isolation precaution adherence (e.g., contact, droplet, airborne precautions) for patients with infectious diseases (e.g., COVID-19, tuberculosis).

### 9.1.2.3 Sterilization and Disinfection Compliance: - P2

9.1.2.3.1 Track compliance with sterilization procedures for surgical instruments and hospital environments (e.g., disinfecting high-touch surfaces, patient room disinfection).

9.1.2.3.2 Provide real-time data on the sterilization and disinfection schedules, with alerts for missed or delayed procedures. – P1

### 9.1.2.4 Antibiotic Stewardship: - P3

9.1.2.4.1 Track the implementation of the hospital's **antibiotic stewardship program**, including the usage of broad-spectrum antibiotics.

9.1.2.4.2 Monitor compliance with protocols for **appropriate prescribing and antibiotic resistance prevention**, using data from the pharmacy system.

9.1.2.4.3 Provide metrics on the percentage of patients on broad-spectrum antibiotics or those showing signs of antimicrobial resistance (AMR).

## 9.1.3 Infection Control Audits

### 9.1.3.1 Surveillance & Inspection Data: - P3

9.1.3.1.1 Provide real-time data from infection control audits, including environmental surveillance, hand hygiene audits, and staff compliance checks.

9.1.3.1.2 Track completion and outcomes of surveillance, with actionable insights for quality improvement.

9.1.3.1.3 Monitor surveillance results by department and clinician, allowing for targeted interventions.

### 9.1.3.2 Outbreak Detection & Management: - P3

9.1.3.2.1 Monitor and alert staff to potential outbreaks based on infection trends (e.g., sudden increase in the number of infections in a specific area or with a specific pathogen).

- 9.1.3.2.2 Provide data to help isolate outbreaks and implement control measures in a timely manner, such as quarantines, staff alerts, and patient transfers.

## **9.2 Healthcare Waste Management (HWM) Monitoring**

### **9.2.1 Waste Generation & Segregation**

- 9.2.1.1 Waste Segregation Data: - P1
  - 9.2.1.1.1 Track data on the proper segregation of healthcare waste into categories (e.g., infectious, non-infectious, sharps, general waste).
  - 9.2.1.1.2 Monitor segregation compliance in different departments and clinical areas using visual indicators (e.g., color-coded waste bins for different categories).
  - 9.2.1.1.3 Provide real-time reports on the quantity of waste generated and segregated correctly versus improperly segregated waste.
- 9.2.1.2 Waste Collection & Transportation: - P1
  - 9.2.1.2.1 Track the collection and transportation of healthcare waste from various departments to the waste storage and disposal area.
  - 9.2.1.2.2 Monitor the frequency and timing of waste collection to ensure timely disposal, preventing overflows and potential contamination risks.

### **9.2.2 Waste Disposal Compliance**

- 9.2.2.1 Disposal Methods Monitoring: - P1
  - 9.2.2.1.1 Track the methods used for the disposal of different types of healthcare waste (e.g., incineration, autoclaving, landfill, recycling).
- 9.2.2.2 Waste Management Equipment Tracking: - P3
  - 9.2.2.2.1 Monitor the operational status and compliance of waste disposal equipment (e.g., autoclaves, incinerators).
  - 9.2.2.2.2 Track the regular maintenance, calibration, and performance of equipment used for waste treatment and disposal.

### **9.2.3 Training & Awareness on HWM**

- 9.2.3.1 Staff Training Compliance: - P1
  - 9.2.3.1.1 Track the training status of staff members regarding proper healthcare waste management practices, including segregation, handling, and disposal.
  - 9.2.3.1.2 Monitor mandatory training sessions and provide reports on compliance rates across various departments.
  - 9.2.3.1.3 Ensure staff are trained in the handling of specific waste types, such as sharps and pharmaceutical waste.
- 9.2.3.2 Waste Management Awareness Campaigns: - P3
  - 9.2.3.2.1 Track the implementation of waste management awareness campaigns, such as posters, workshops, and seminars on proper waste segregation and disposal.
  - 9.2.3.2.2 Provide data on staff participation in these campaigns and evaluate their effectiveness in improving waste management practices.

## **9.3 Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) for Infection Prevention & HWM**

### **9.3.1 Infection Control KPIs**

- 9.3.1.1 Hand Hygiene Compliance Rate: - P2
  - 9.3.1.1.1 Target hand hygiene compliance rate (e.g., >95%) and real-time tracking by department, unit, and individual staff member.

- 9.3.1.2 Infection Rate: - P2
  - 9.3.1.2.1 Rate of hospital-acquired infections (HAIs) by type (e.g., SSI) compared to hospital benchmarks or national guidelines.
  - 9.3.1.2.2 The **infection incidence rate** per 1,000 patient days.
- 9.3.1.3 Antibiotic Usage Compliance: - P3
  - 9.3.1.3.1 Percentage of patients prescribed antibiotics according to the hospital's **antibiotic stewardship guidelines**.
  - 9.3.1.3.2 Track the reduction in **antibiotic-resistant infections** over time.
- 9.3.2 HWM KPIs
  - 9.3.2.1 Waste Segregation Compliance Rate: - P3
    - 9.3.2.1.1 Percentage of correctly segregated waste, with targets for each type of waste (e.g., sharps, infectious waste).
  - 9.3.2.2 Disposal Method Compliance Rate: - P3
    - 9.3.2.2.1 Percentage of waste disposed of using the **appropriate and safe methods** (e.g., autoclaving, incineration).
  - 9.3.2.3 Waste Generation per Bed/Per Patient: - P3
    - 9.3.2.3.1 Track the volume of healthcare waste generated per hospital bed or per patient admitted, which can help assess resource consumption and waste reduction efforts.
  - 9.3.2.4 Training Compliance Rate: - P3
    - 9.3.2.4.1 Percentage of clinical and non-clinical staff trained in proper healthcare waste management protocols.
    - 9.3.2.4.2 Track the **frequency of training** and staff participation in related workshops.
- 9.3.3 Alerts & Notifications
  - 9.3.3.1 Real-Time Alerts for Infection Risks: - P3
    - 9.3.3.1.1 Immediate alerts when infection rates exceed predefined thresholds (e.g., surge in surgical site infections or VAP in ICU).
    - 9.3.3.1.2 Notifications for non-compliance with infection prevention protocols (e.g., missed hand hygiene moments or improper isolation procedures).
  - 9.3.3.2 Healthcare Waste Management Alerts: - P3
    - 9.3.3.2.1 Alerts for waste segregation failures (e.g., improper disposal of sharps or hazardous materials).
    - 9.3.3.2.2 Notifications for delayed waste collection or issues with disposal equipment (e.g., autoclave or incinerator malfunction).
- 9.3.4 Data Analytics & Reporting
  - 9.3.4.1 Customizable Reports: - P3
    - 9.3.4.1.1 Generate real-time and historical reports on infection control practices and healthcare waste management compliance.
    - 9.3.4.1.2 Ability to generate detailed reports by department, infection type, waste category, and staff compliance to analyze trends and identify problem areas.
  - 9.3.4.2 Trend Analysis & Predictive Analytics: - P3
    - 9.3.4.2.1 Use predictive analytics to forecast potential outbreaks, assess future infection risks, and identify areas where infection prevention or waste management practices need improvement.

- 9.3.4.2.2 Analyze long-term trends in infection rates, waste generation, and disposal practices to guide policy and operational changes.

## **10 HRM Dashboard:**

### **10.1 Staffing Overview & Workforce Management**

#### **10.1.1 Real-Time Staffing Overview: - P1**

- 10.1.1.1 Display a real-time count of total hospital staff by category (e.g., doctors, nurses, administrative, technical, support staff).
- 10.1.1.2 Breakdown of staff numbers by department (e.g., ICU, Emergency, Radiology, Surgery).
- 10.1.1.3 Staff availability status, including those on leave, off duty, or on-call.

#### **10.1.2 Staff Allocation & Workload: - P1**

- 10.1.2.1 Overview of staff allocation across various departments to ensure that the hospital is adequately staffed.
- 10.1.2.2 Real-time data on shifts (morning, evening, night) and availability to avoid overburdening any department or staff member.
- 10.1.2.3 Nurse-to-patient ratio and staffing adequacy for critical departments like ICU and Emergency.

#### **10.1.3 Vacancy & Recruitment Tracking: - P2**

- 10.1.3.1 Real-time visibility of open positions within the hospital and the number of vacancies by department.
- 10.1.3.2 Tracking of recruitment processes, including interview schedules, shortlisted candidates, and recruitment status.

### **10.2 Staff Performance & Evaluation**

#### **10.2.1 Performance Metrics: - P3**

- 10.2.1.1 Overview of staff performance indicators, including key performance indicators (KPIs) like patient satisfaction, adherence to protocols, response times, and quality of care.
- 10.2.1.2 Evaluation of clinical staff based on specific criteria such as patient recovery rates, treatment adherence, or surgical outcomes.
- 10.2.1.3 Display of performance evaluations and feedback for each staff member based on their department and role.

#### **10.2.2 Staff Turnover & Retention:**

- 10.2.2.1 Monitor turnover rates across different departments and roles.
- 10.2.2.2 Identify departments with high turnover and recommend targeted interventions (e.g., staff engagement, incentives).

### **10.3 Attendance, Timekeeping, & Scheduling**

#### **10.3.1 Attendance Tracking: - P1**

- 10.3.1.1 Real-time tracking of employee attendance, including clock-in/clock-out times, absences etc.
- 10.3.1.2 Integration with the hospital's **Time and Attendance Management System** to track hours worked, overtime, and shift compliance.

#### **10.3.2 Shift & Schedule Management: - P2**

10.3.2.1 Visual representation of staffing schedules, ensuring adequate coverage for each department, especially during peak times.

10.3.2.2 Allow managers to quickly adjust shifts and reschedule staff based on emergency needs, patient demand, or unavailability.

**10.3.3 Leave Management: - P1**

10.3.3.1 Overview of employee leave status (sick leave, vacation, personal leave, etc.) with a breakdown by department and staff type.

10.3.3.2 Integration with HR systems for automated leave requests, approval workflows, and tracking of accrued leave days. – P2

10.3.3.3 Alerts for upcoming or overlapping leave periods, especially for critical staff in high-demand departments. – P3

## **10.4 Payroll & Compensation Management**

**10.4.1 Salary & Payroll Overview: - P3**

10.4.1.1 Overview of monthly/bi-weekly payroll data for all staff, including salary breakdowns (base pay, allowances, bonuses, overtime, deductions).

10.4.1.2 Track compensation against budgetary constraints and staffing efficiency.

**10.4.2 Overtime & Shift Premiums: - P2**

10.4.2.1 Monitor overtime hours worked, ensuring compliance with the hospital's overtime policies.

10.4.2.2 Track shift premiums (e.g., night shift, weekend shift) and ensure appropriate compensation.

**10.4.3 Incentives & Bonuses: - P2**

10.4.3.1 Monitor and track incentive programs, bonus eligibility, and performance-based rewards.

10.4.3.2 Overview of hospital-wide incentive programs (e.g., performance bonuses for staff meeting or exceeding KPIs) and their impact on staff morale.

## **10.5 Compliance & Certification Tracking**

**10.5.1 Licensing & Certification Management: - P3**

10.5.1.1 Track the expiration of professional licenses and certifications for all clinical staff.

10.5.1.2 Alerts for upcoming renewals and automated reminders for staff to submit required documentation for compliance.

**10.5.2 Staff Health & Safety: - P3**

10.5.2.1 Track employee health and safety certifications (e.g., immunization records, PPE training).

10.5.2.2 Monitor the hospital's compliance with health and safety protocols to ensure that staff are protected from hospital-acquired infections (HAIs) and other workplace hazards.

## **10.6 Recruitment & Talent Acquisition**

**10.6.1 Applicant Tracking: - P3**

10.6.1.1 Track recruitment efforts, including the status of job openings, applications, interviews, and candidate selection.

10.6.1.2 Display data on candidate sources, recruitment costs, and time-to-hire for various positions.

#### 10.6.2 Recruitment Pipeline: - P3

10.6.2.1 Provide an overview of the recruitment pipeline, showing stages for each candidate (e.g., application received, interview scheduled, offer extended, etc.).

10.6.2.2 Track key recruitment metrics, such as cost per hire, time-to-hire, and source of hire.

#### 10.6.3 Diversity & Inclusion Metrics: - P3

10.6.3.1 Monitor the diversity of the hospital workforce in terms of gender, ethnicity, and other key diversity factors.

10.6.3.2 Track and report on the success of diversity and inclusion initiatives.

### **10.7 Reporting & Analytics**

#### 10.7.1 Customizable Reporting: - P3

10.7.1.1 Generate detailed and customizable reports on staff demographics, turnover rates, recruitment activities, salary expenditures, and performance data.

10.7.1.2 Create ad-hoc reports for specific departments, job categories, or performance periods.

#### 10.7.2 Workforce Analytics:

- Provide data-driven insights into workforce trends, such as turnover rates by department, and cost-per-staff metrics.

10.7.2.1 Use predictive analytics to anticipate staffing shortages and plan for future hiring needs based on trends and projections.

#### 10.7.3 Staff Retention & Development Analytics:

10.7.3.1 Track employee retention metrics and identify trends or patterns that indicate potential issues (e.g., high turnover in specific departments).

10.7.3.2 Measure the effectiveness of training and development programs in improving staff skills and reducing turnover.

## **11 Equipment Management Dashboard:**

### **11.1 Equipment Inventory Management**

#### 11.1.1 Real-Time Equipment Status: - P1

11.1.1.1 Overview of all medical equipment, categorized by type (e.g., diagnostic, therapeutic, life support).

11.1.1.2 Equipment ID, description, location (e.g., wards, ICUs, operating rooms), and ownership status (hospital-owned, leased, etc.).

11.1.1.3 Real-time status (e.g., in use, available, under maintenance, out of service).

#### 11.1.2 Inventory Levels: - P1

11.1.2.1 Visibility into inventory for consumables related to equipment (e.g., medical supplies, accessories).

11.1.2.2 Alerts for low stock levels of equipment-related consumables (e.g., spare parts, replacement accessories).

#### 11.1.3 Equipment Usage & Utilization: - P3

- 11.1.3.1 Monitoring of equipment usage (e.g., total hours in use, cycles completed, number of patients treated).
- 11.1.3.2 Equipment utilization rates by department (e.g., ICU, radiology, surgery).
- 11.1.3.3 Identify underused or overused equipment.

## **11.2 Maintenance and Calibration**

- 11.2.1 Scheduled Maintenance & Calibration: - P2
  - 11.2.1.1 Automated scheduling for preventive maintenance and calibration of critical medical equipment (e.g., ventilators, MRI machines, defibrillators).
  - 11.2.1.2 Notifications for upcoming or overdue maintenance tasks.
- 11.2.2 Equipment Downtime Tracking: - P3
  - 11.2.2.1 Real-time tracking of downtime incidents, including reasons (e.g., routine maintenance, breakdowns, part failures).
  - 11.2.2.2 Average downtime duration and resolution time.
  - 11.2.2.3 Historical data for equipment performance and maintenance trends.
- 11.2.3 Repair and Service Requests: - P3
  - 11.2.3.1 Ability to track and manage repair/service requests for malfunctioning equipment.
  - 11.2.3.2 Logging of issues reported, assigned technicians, repair status, and completion times.
- 11.2.4 Service Provider & Warranty Tracking: - P3
  - 11.2.4.1 Maintain details of external service providers or vendors (e.g., contracts, service agreements, warranties).
  - 11.2.4.2 Alerts for warranty expiry or service contract renewals.

## **11.3 Equipment Procurement and Lifecycle Management**

- 11.3.1 Procurement and Purchase Orders: - P3
  - 11.3.1.1 Overview of ongoing and upcoming equipment procurement processes, including vendor selection and purchase orders.
  - 11.3.1.2 Tracking of equipment orders, expected delivery dates, and delivery status.
- Equipment Lifecycle Management: - P3
  - 11.3.1.3 Monitoring the age and condition of equipment to predict end-of-life and replacement needs.
  - 11.3.1.4 Tracking equipment depreciation, warranties, and lifespan.
  - 11.3.1.5 Equipment replacement and disposal management (e.g., responsible disposal of outdated or non-functional equipment).

## **11.4 Financial Management & Cost Tracking**

- 11.4.1 Cost Analysis: - P3
  - 11.4.1.1 Tracking the total cost of equipment acquisition, maintenance, and repairs.
  - 11.4.1.2 Cost per unit of equipment usage (e.g., cost per scan for MRI or CT).
  - 11.4.1.3 Comparing capital and operational expenses for equipment.
- 11.4.2 Budgeting & Financial Forecasting: - P3

11.4.2.1 Overview of budget allocation for equipment procurement, maintenance, and repair.

11.4.2.2 Forecasting future equipment needs based on usage trends and department requirements.

11.4.2.3 Real-time tracking of expenses vs. allocated budget for equipment-related costs.

## **11.5 Compliance & Regulatory Monitoring**

11.5.1 Regulatory Compliance: - P2

11.5.1.1 Monitoring adherence to regulatory standards for medical equipment.

11.5.1.2 Alerts for equipment requiring re-certification, inspection, or compliance check-ups.

11.5.2 Monitoring Trails: - P2

11.5.2.1 Maintain an audit trail for all equipment-related activities (e.g., repairs, maintenance, procurement).

11.5.2.2 Tracking access to sensitive equipment and usage logs to ensure accountability.

11.5.3 Safety Compliance: - P3

11.5.3.1 Ensure that medical equipment meets hospital safety protocols (e.g., infection control standards, electrical safety).

11.5.3.2 Tracking of safety testing for equipment (e.g., electrical safety tests, sterilization procedures for surgical instruments).

## **11.6 Equipment Availability & Resource Optimization**

11.6.1 Equipment Availability Monitoring: - P1

11.6.1.1 Real-time tracking of availability and location of equipment across the hospital (e.g., ICUs, emergency department, surgery rooms).

11.6.1.2 Alerts for equipment nearing checkout time or required for another urgent case.

11.6.2 Room and Equipment Scheduling: - P3

11.6.2.1 Integration with the hospital's scheduling system to manage shared use of high-demand equipment (e.g., MRI, CT, ultrasound).

11.6.2.2 Conflict resolution for overlapping scheduled usage times.

11.6.2.3 Tracking of equipment time slots and usage patterns by department.

11.6.3 Equipment Allocation & Optimization: - P3

11.6.3.1 Equipment allocation based on priority, patient needs, and department requirements.

11.6.3.2 Optimization of high-cost equipment usage (e.g., balancing the use of expensive imaging machines or operating rooms).

## **11.7 Equipment Performance & Operational Efficiency**

11.7.1 Equipment Performance Metrics: - P3

11.7.1.1 Real-time performance tracking of critical equipment (e.g., uptime, service calls, performance degradation).

11.7.1.2 Analyzing trends in performance to identify equipment that may require attention or replacement.

11.7.2 Key Performance Indicators (KPIs): - P3

- 11.7.2.1 Operational KPIs, such as the average response time for repairs, downtime duration, cost per procedure using equipment, and equipment utilization rates.
- 11.7.2.2 Identifying trends in equipment failure or the need for replacement.
- 11.7.3 Productivity Metrics: - P3
  - 11.7.3.1 Productivity tracking based on equipment usage (e.g., number of scans performed, surgeries completed with specific equipment).
  - 11.7.3.2 Departmental productivity based on equipment access (e.g., delays in procedures due to equipment unavailability).

## **11.8 Reporting and Analytics**

- 11.8.1 Customizable Reports: - P3
  - 11.8.1.1 Generate real-time and periodic reports (daily, weekly, monthly) on equipment performance, maintenance costs, procurement, and utilization.
  - 11.8.1.2 Reporting on equipment downtime, service requests, and operational performance.
- 11.8.2 Predictive Analytics: - P3
  - 11.8.2.1 Predictive maintenance and replacement insights based on historical data, ensuring timely action before equipment failure.
  - 11.8.2.2 Forecasting equipment needs based on trends, hospital expansion, or seasonal usage spikes.
- 11.8.3 Trend Analysis: - P3
  - 11.8.3.1 Identify recurring issues with specific equipment or types of equipment.
  - 11.8.3.2 Spot patterns in procurement, repair, and maintenance to optimize future purchases and reduce recurring issues.

## **12 Inventory Management Dashboard:**

### **12.1 Real-Time Inventory Tracking**

- 12.1.1 Current Inventory Status: - P1
  - 12.1.1.1 Real-time view of stock levels for all hospital supplies (medications, consumables, medical equipment, etc.).
  - 12.1.1.2 Overview of inventory by category (e.g., pharmaceuticals, medical consumables, surgical instruments, etc.).
  - 12.1.1.3 Visual indicators for stock levels: color-coded (green, yellow, red) to easily identify low, optimal, or overstocked items.
- 12.1.2 Inventory by Department: - P1
  - 12.1.2.1 Breakdown of inventory distribution across different hospital departments (e.g., ICU, operating rooms, emergency department).
  - 12.1.2.2 Real-time tracking of high-demand items (e.g., oxygen, PPE, medications).
- 12.1.3 Stock Expiry Management: - P1
  - 12.1.3.1 Alerts for items nearing expiry dates, including pharmaceuticals, consumables, and other perishable goods.
  - 12.1.3.2 Visibility of expired items that need to be removed or returned.
- 12.1.4 Product Availability & Location: - P2

- 12.1.4.1 Real-time tracking of the availability of critical items across various hospital locations (e.g., wards, operating theatres, pharmacy).
- 12.1.4.2 Tracking of items that are being used or are temporarily unavailable.

## **12.2 Procurement & Supply Chain Management**

- 12.2.1 Procurement Tracking: - P3
  - 12.2.1.1 Overview of pending and ongoing procurement orders for inventory replenishment.
  - 12.2.1.2 Track purchase orders (POs), delivery status, and estimated arrival times for incoming stock.
  - 12.2.1.3 Integration with supplier databases to manage vendor performance, delivery times, and order accuracy.
- 12.2.2 Requisition Management: - P3
  - 12.2.2.1 Management of department-level requisitions for medical supplies and pharmaceuticals.
  - 12.2.2.2 Approval workflows for internal requisitions from hospital departments.
  - 12.2.2.3 Historical view of requests, approvals, and order fulfillment rates.
- 12.2.3 Supplier & Vendor Management:
  - 12.2.3.1 Monitor and evaluate supplier performance based on delivery times, order fulfillment, and quality.
  - 12.2.3.2 Alerts for any delayed deliveries or pending orders that are critical to hospital operations.
  - 12.2.3.3 Ability to track warranty status for procured equipment or high-cost items.

## **12.3 Stock Movement & Consumption Tracking**

- 12.3.1 Stock In/Out Monitoring: - P2
  - 12.3.1.1 Real-time tracking of stock inflow (new deliveries) and outflow (dispensed items).
  - 12.3.1.2 Tracking of usage by department and patient care units (e.g., surgeries, ICUs, emergency).
  - 12.3.1.3 Tracking of product transfers between departments, including stock movement between wards and storage areas.
- 12.3.2 Consumption Patterns: - P3
  - 12.3.2.1 Monitoring of consumables and medications used per procedure, patient, or department.
  - 12.3.2.2 Identifying trends and patterns in usage, such as seasonal fluctuations (e.g., flu season or surge in patient admissions).
  - 12.3.2.3 Tracking of high-usage items and optimizing ordering quantities based on historical data.
- 12.3.3 Inventory Turnover Rate: - P3
  - 12.3.3.1 Calculation and monitoring of inventory turnover, helping to identify slow-moving vs. fast-moving items.
  - 12.3.3.2 Ensures that items are used efficiently, reducing waste and ensuring timely stock rotation.

## **12.4 Automated Alerts & Notifications**

#### 12.4.1 Low Stock Alerts: - P1

12.4.1.1 Automated alerts when stock levels fall below pre-set minimum thresholds for critical supplies (e.g., medications, PPE, sterile items).

12.4.1.2 Alerts for stock-outs of high-demand items or critical equipment.

#### 12.4.2 Expiry Date Notifications: - P1

12.4.2.1 Alerts for items nearing expiration, allowing for stock clearance or returns to suppliers.

12.4.2.2 Notifications for items that should be used first (first-expiry, first-out – FEFO system).

#### 12.4.3 Overstock Alerts: - P3

12.4.3.1 Alerts when stock levels exceed maximum capacity or if overstocked items are approaching storage limits.

12.4.3.2 Identifying slow-moving items to reduce over-purchasing and avoid waste.

### **12.5 Inventory Cost Management & Financial Reporting**

12.5.1.1 Inventory Cost Tracking: - P3

12.5.1.2 Real-time tracking of the cost of inventory, including pharmaceuticals, medical supplies, and equipment.

12.5.1.3 Calculation of the total cost of consumed goods in real time, comparing actual usage against budgeted or forecasted amounts.

12.5.1.4 Inventory Value: - P3

12.5.1.5 Monitoring the financial value of stock on hand, including current value and depreciation for high-cost equipment.

12.5.1.6 Track the total value of pharmaceuticals and supplies used across departments.

12.5.1.7 Budgeting & Financial Forecasting: - P3

12.5.1.8 Track departmental inventory spending to ensure it stays within budget.

12.5.1.9 Financial forecasting for upcoming procurements based on historical usage data and hospital needs.

### **12.6 Reporting & Analytics**

#### 12.6.1 Inventory Reporting: - P3

12.6.1.1 Detailed and customizable reports on stock levels, inventory turnover, procurement status, usage trends, and cost analysis.

12.6.1.2 Reports on fast-moving and slow-moving items, and stock-outs, helping optimize inventory management.

12.6.1.3 Regular and ad-hoc reports (daily, weekly, monthly) for different departments and hospital leadership.

#### 12.6.2 Trend Analysis & Predictive Analytics: - P3

12.6.2.1 Identify trends in inventory usage to optimize procurement cycles, reduce stockouts, and prevent overstocking.

12.6.2.2 Predictive analytics to forecast future inventory needs based on patient volume, historical trends, and seasonal demand.

### **12.7 Waste & Expiry Management**

#### 12.7.1 Waste Reduction: - P3

- 12.7.1.1 Monitor and report on inventory waste, including expired items or items that are discarded due to damage or spoilage.
- 12.7.1.2 Track reasons for waste to help in inventory planning and improve procurement decisions.
- 12.7.2 First Expiry, First Out (FEFO) System: - P2
  - 12.7.2.1 Ensure that items with the closest expiry date are used first, and generate alerts to prevent wastage.
  - 12.7.2.2 Track and report on compliance with FEFO procedures, particularly for critical medications and supplies.

## **12.8 Supplier & Vendor Performance Management**

- 12.8.1 Supplier Performance Metrics: - P3
  - 12.8.1.1 Track and evaluate the performance of suppliers based on delivery times, quality of products, and pricing.
  - 12.8.1.2 Generate reports for vendor comparison to assist with procurement decisions.
- 12.8.2 Contract Management: - P3
  - 12.8.2.1 Overview of active contracts with suppliers, including terms, renewal dates, and performance clauses.

## **13 CSSD Dashboard:**

### **13.1 Sterilization Process & Cycle Management**

- 13.1.1 Sterilization Cycle Overview: - P3
  - 13.1.1.1 Real-time tracking of sterilization cycles for all instruments, including data on the type of sterilization process used.
- 13.1.2 Sterilization Turnaround Time: - P3
  - 13.1.2.1 Monitor the time taken from instrument collection to sterilization completion, and track turnaround time for high-demand items (e.g., surgical instruments, trays for specific procedures).
  - 13.1.2.2 Alerts when turnaround times exceed predefined thresholds, indicating potential delays in the workflow.
- 13.1.3 Load & Tray Management: - P3
  - 13.1.3.1 Tracking of the number and type of trays or loads sterilized, including their destination (e.g., Operating Room (OR), ICU, outpatient department).
  - 13.1.3.2 Ability to link sterilized items to specific departments or surgeries to ensure the availability of required instruments at the right time.

### **13.2 Equipment & Instrument Tracking**

- 13.2.1 Instrument Lifecycle Management: - P3
  - 13.2.1.1 Real-time tracking of individual instruments from collection, through sterilization, usage, and subsequent cleaning and re-sterilization cycles.
  - 13.2.1.2 Record of each instrument's sterilization history, including cycles, maintenance checks, and repairs.
  - 13.2.1.3 Integration with **Inventory Management Systems** to track instrument usage rates, maintenance schedules, and replacements.
- 13.2.2 Equipment Performance & Maintenance: - P3

- 13.2.2.1 Monitoring the performance of sterilization equipment, including machines, autoclaves, washers, and dryers.
- 13.2.2.2 Alerts for routine maintenance schedules, breakdowns, and malfunctions of sterilization machines, ensuring equipment downtime is minimized.
- 13.2.2.3 Tracking maintenance and servicing history for all sterilization equipment.
- 13.2.3 Tracking of Sterilized Items: - P1
  - 13.2.3.1 Maintain a real-time inventory of sterilized items, categorized by type, expiration date, and batch.
  - 13.2.3.2 Alerts for the expiration of sterilized items to prevent the use of expired instruments.

### **13.3 Staff Management & Operational Efficiency**

- 13.3.1 Staffing Overview: - P1
  - 13.3.1.1 Real-time tracking of staffing levels in the CSSD, including number of staff members on each shift (e.g., sterilization technicians, support staff).

### **13.4 Compliance & Quality Control**

- 13.4.1 Sterilization Validation: - P2
  - 13.4.1.1 Track and report on sterilization validation measures.
  - 13.4.1.2 Record outcomes of sterilization validation tests, including any failures or irregularities, and document corrective actions taken.
- 13.4.2 Sterilization Records: - P3
  - 13.4.2.1 Maintain a digital audit trail for every sterilization cycle, including timestamps, personnel involved, sterilization parameters, and instrument batch numbers.
  - 13.4.2.2 Ability to generate reports on sterilization activities, audits, and inspections for compliance reviews.

### **13.5 Inventory Management & Resource Optimization**

- 13.5.1 Sterilized Inventory Tracking: - P2
  - 13.5.1.1 Real-time inventory of sterilized items, including surgical instruments, surgical trays, IV sets, and other medical equipment.
  - 13.5.1.2 Track which items are in circulation (e.g., used in procedures) and which are available for sterilization or in stock.
- 13.5.2 Consumables Management: - P2
  - 13.5.2.1 Track usage and stock levels of consumables used in the sterilization process (e.g., sterilization wraps, chemical indicators, detergents).
  - 13.5.2.2 Alerts for low stock or upcoming expiration dates on consumables to avoid delays in sterilization operations.
  - 13.5.2.3 Ensure optimized inventory levels of sterilized goods for use in various hospital departments, minimizing waste or shortages.

### **13.6 Performance Analytics & Reporting**

- 13.6.1 Key Performance Indicators (KPIs): - P3
  - 13.6.1.1 Track KPIs for CSSD operations, such as:
    - 13.6.1.1.1 Average sterilization cycle time.
    - 13.6.1.1.2 Instrument turnaround time.

- 13.6.1.1.3 Sterilization success rates (pass/fail rates of sterilization cycles).
  - 13.6.1.1.4 Equipment downtime and repair frequencies.
  - 13.6.1.1.5 Staff productivity and adherence to standard operating procedures (SOPs).
- 13.6.2 Sterilization and Equipment Efficiency: - P3
  - 13.6.2.1 Analyze the performance of sterilization equipment over time, including failure rates, maintenance costs, and machine utilization.
  - 13.6.2.2 Data-driven insights on equipment aging and the need for upgrades or replacements based on usage statistics.
- 13.6.3 Custom Reports: - P3
  - 13.6.3.1 Generate detailed, customizable reports on sterilization activities, inventory levels, compliance status, and staffing efficiency.
  - 13.6.3.2 Ability to track and report on critical incidents, such as equipment malfunctions, sterilization failures, or deviations from SOPs.
- 13.6.4 Trend Analysis: - P3
  - 13.6.4.1 Monitor trends in CSSD workload, patient procedures requiring sterilized instruments, and demand for sterilized items across various hospital departments.
  - 13.6.4.2 Predict future sterilization needs based on historical data (e.g., surgeries scheduled, patient admissions, seasonal fluctuations).

## 14 KPIs Dashboard:

### 14.1 Clinical KPIs

#### 14.1.1 Patient Care Quality Indicators

- 14.1.1.1 Patient Satisfaction Scores: - P3
  - 14.1.1.1.1 Display real-time data on patient satisfaction levels using surveys across different departments.
  - 14.1.1.1.2 Provide a breakdown of satisfaction metrics such as communication with doctors/nurses, cleanliness, pain management, and discharge instructions.
  - 14.1.1.1.3 Show trends and actionable insights on areas needing improvement (e.g., low satisfaction scores in the emergency department or ICU).
- 14.1.1.2 Clinical Outcomes: - P2
  - 14.1.1.2.1 Track mortality rates, readmission rates, and complications (e.g., surgical site infections, sepsis).
  - 14.1.1.2.2 Monitor outcomes for specific procedures (e.g., cardiac surgeries, orthopedic surgeries) to assess the effectiveness of treatment protocols.
  - 14.1.1.2.3 Show comparative analysis with national benchmarks to ensure performance is within acceptable ranges.
- 14.1.1.3 Mortality Indicators: - P2
  - 14.1.1.3.1 **Overall Mortality Rate** (in-hospital deaths per 1,000 admissions)
  - 14.1.1.3.2 **Case-specific Mortality Rates** (e.g., stroke, MI, sepsis, trauma)
  - 14.1.1.3.3 **Surgical Mortality Rate** (within 30 days of surgery)
  - 14.1.1.3.4 **ICU Mortality Rate**

- 14.1.1.3.5 **Early Post-discharge Mortality** (within 7 or 30 days)
- 14.1.1.4 Hospital-Acquired Infections (HAIs): - P2
  - 14.1.1.4.1 Track infection rates such as ventilator-associated pneumonia (VAP), central-line-associated bloodstream infections (CLABSI), catheter-associated urinary tract infections (CAUTI), and surgical site infections (SSI).
  - 14.1.1.4.2 Display trends of HAIs over time and compare them with infection control goals and best practices.
- 14.1.1.5 Adverse Events & Complications: - P3
  - 14.1.1.5.1 Monitor adverse events such as medication errors, falls, pressure ulcers, surgical complications, and adverse drug reactions.
  - 14.1.1.5.2 Real-time tracking of near-misses for prompt corrective action.

## 14.2 Surgical KPIs

- 14.2.1 Surgical Site Infection (SSI) Rates: - P2
  - 14.2.1.1 Monitor **SSI rates** per procedure and track trends over time to ensure compliance with infection prevention protocols.
  - 14.2.1.2 Compare the hospital's SSI rates against national or international benchmarks to evaluate performance.
- 14.2.2 Surgical Complications: - P2
  - 14.2.2.1 Track complications related to surgery, including bleeding, anastomotic leaks, organ perforations, and anesthesia-related issues.
  - 14.2.2.2 Monitor outcomes for high-risk surgeries and departments (e.g., cardiothoracic surgery, orthopedic surgeries).

## 14.3 Operational KPIs

- 14.3.1 Patient Flow & Capacity Management
  - 14.3.1.1 Emergency Department (ED) Wait Times: - P3
    - 14.3.1.1.1 Monitor patient treatment times for patients entering the emergency department.
    - 14.3.1.1.2 Display real-time wait times to help manage ED capacity and improve response times.
  - 14.3.1.2 Length of Stay (LOS): - P2
    - 14.3.1.2.1 Track average **length of stay (LOS)** in various hospital departments (e.g., ICU, general wards, surgical recovery) and compare it with hospital benchmarks.
    - 14.3.1.2.2 Identify departments with extended LOS to investigate possible inefficiencies in care or discharge planning.
  - 14.3.1.3 Bed Occupancy Rate: - P1
    - 14.3.1.3.1 Monitor the **bed occupancy rate** across the hospital, including ICU and general wards, and identify potential bottlenecks or underutilization.
    - 14.3.1.3.2 Ensure that the hospital maintains optimal bed capacity without overburdening certain departments.
  - 14.3.1.4 Readmission Rates: - P1
    - 14.3.1.4.1 30-day Readmission Rate for key conditions (e.g., CHF, COPD, diabetes)
    - 14.3.1.4.2 Department-wise Readmission Analysis
    - 14.3.1.4.3 Preventable Readmission Rate

#### 14.3.1.5 Discharge Delays: - P2

14.3.1.5.1 Track the number of **discharge delays** due to administrative, medical, or patient-related reasons.

14.3.1.5.2 Identify and monitor the causes of discharge delays, such as waiting for test results, medical clearance, or insurance approval.

### 14.4 Staff Performance & Utilization

#### 14.4.1 Staff to Patient Ratios: - P2

14.4.1.1 Monitor **staff-to-patient ratios** across departments to ensure adequate staffing levels.

14.4.1.2 Track the ratios for key clinical staff, including nurses, doctors, and support staff (e.g., for high-acuity areas such as ICU or surgery).

#### 14.4.2 Employee Turnover & Retention: - P3

14.4.2.1 Track **employee turnover rates and staff retention** across departments to assess workforce stability.

14.4.2.2 Display data on staffing needs and the effectiveness of retention programs.

### 14.5 Financial KPIs

#### 14.5.1 Hospital Revenue: - P3

14.5.1.1 Track overall hospital **revenue and revenue by department** (e.g., emergency, surgery, outpatient) on a daily, weekly, or monthly basis.

14.5.1.2 Monitor **billing compliance** to ensure that all patient services are accurately billed.

#### 14.5.2 Cost per Patient: - P3

14.5.2.1 Track **cost per patient** by department or treatment type to assess the financial efficiency of patient care.

14.5.2.2 Monitor key cost drivers (e.g., high-cost medications, procedures, and consumables) and identify areas for cost reduction without compromising care quality.

#### 14.5.3 Operational Costs: - P3

14.5.3.1 Track hospital **operational costs**, including salaries, utilities, medical supplies, and equipment maintenance.

14.5.3.2 Identify areas where cost-saving measures can be implemented (e.g., reducing waste, improving supply chain management).

### 14.6 Patient Experience KPIs

#### 14.6.1 Patient Satisfaction and Feedback

##### 14.6.1.1 Patient Complaints & Grievances: - P3

14.6.1.1.1 Monitor **patient complaints and grievances**, including categorizing issues (e.g., billing, communication, clinical care).

14.6.1.1.2 Track **response time and resolution time** for complaints to ensure that issues are addressed promptly.

##### 14.6.1.2 Post-Discharge Follow-up: - P3

14.6.1.2.1 Track the rate of **post-discharge follow-ups** with patients, including calls for medication adherence, recovery, and satisfaction.

14.6.1.2.2 Monitor the effectiveness of follow-up care in reducing readmission rates.

### 14.7 Real-Time Monitoring & Alerts

#### 14.7.1 Alerts for KPI Deviations

##### 14.7.1.1 Real-Time Alerts: - P3

14.7.1.1.1 Set thresholds for critical KPIs (e.g., infection rates, patient wait times) and receive **real-time alerts** when performance deviates from expected levels.

14.7.1.1.2 For example, an alert when infection rates rise above a threshold or when bed occupancy exceeds capacity limits.

##### 14.7.1.2 Operational Alerts: - P3

14.7.1.2.1 Alerts for operational issues, such as **long wait times** in the ED or **staff shortages** in critical areas like ICU or emergency services.

14.7.1.2.2 Alerts for non-compliance with important hospital protocols or regulations (e.g., missed medication administration, incomplete documentation).

### 14.8 Customized Reports

#### 14.8.1 Customizable Reports: - P3

14.8.1.1 Generate **customized reports** for specific KPIs, which can be tailored for different hospital departments (e.g., clinical, financial, operational).

14.8.1.2 Schedule automated generation of **weekly/monthly reports** to share with stakeholders (e.g., hospital leadership, regulatory bodies).

#### 14.8.2 Benchmarking Reports: - P3

14.8.2.1 Enable **benchmarking** with national or international standards and peer hospitals to compare hospital performance across KPIs.

14.8.2.2 Generate reports comparing **departmental performance**, allowing hospital leadership to identify trends and implement corrective actions.
